---
title: "QILP Replication and Extension"
output: html_document
date: "07/01/2026"
---

# Documentation

This notebook replicates the Quarterly Industry Labor Productivity (QILP) measure from
Hobijn, Mestieri, Werquin, and Zhang (Chicago Fed Economic Perspectives No. 1, 2025) and
extends the series to 2026Q1. All 19 steps are implemented and the pipeline has been
validated against the published workbook (2006Q2–2025Q2).

## Data sources

**BEA:** `bea_value_added_all_tables_20260625.xlsx`, downloaded 2026-06-25 from the BEA GDP-by-industry interactive
tables. Contains TVA105-Q (nominal value added, millions of current dollars, SAAR) and
TVA106-Q (real value added, millions of 2017 chain dollars, SAAR). Nominal VA is used for
Törnqvist weights; real VA levels are differenced to produce Δln V_it. This file also
supplies the sub-industry nominal VA shares needed for the Housing/ORE and Securities/Funds
splits (sub-industry rows HS, ORE, 523, 525 and their parents 531 and 52).

Note on BEA vintage: the published workbook was built before the BEA August 2025 annual
revision, which revised GDP-by-industry data for 2022–2024. This replication uses the
June 2026 BEA release, which incorporates that revision. Historical values for 2022–2024
therefore differ from the published workbook. Values for 2025Q1 onward are unaffected by
the revision and match the workbook exactly.

**CES:** BLS Current Employment Statistics payroll employment and average weekly hours,
downloaded via the BLS API. Provides payroll employment and AWH for each QILP industry.

**CPS:** IPUMS CPS monthly microdata extract. Provides self-employed (incorporated and
unincorporated) and unpaid family worker counts by industry, mapped to the QILP industry
tree via a scheme-year-aware IPUMS-to-QILP crosswalk.

## Steps 1–10: Data assembly

1. Froze the published Chicago Fed replication targets: downloaded the paper PDF and the
   public QILP workbook (through 2025Q2), saving dated and stable-name copies. Extracted
   workbook version notes and the published industry roster.

2. Staged BEA metadata: downloaded the official BEA industry-code concordance and queried
   the BEA API for GDP-by-industry dataset and table-parameter catalogs.

3. Built the BEA quarterly value-added panel from manually downloaded workbooks: parsed
   current-dollar VA (TVA105-Q), real VA (TVA106-Q), and chain-type price indexes
   (TVA104-Q) into a combined long panel. The API panel (2005Q1–2025Q4) was supplemented
   with manually downloaded files covering 2026Q1.

4. Reconstructed the QILP industry universe: matched the workbook's 81-industry roster to
   BEA line numbers. Documented the 2018Q1 BEA definition-change seam; this quarter and
   the log-change that uses it as a denominator (2018Q2) are excluded from all growth
   calculations.

5. Pulled CES payroll inputs: resolved CES series matches for all 81 industries using
   direct title and alias matching, subgroup aggregation for composite rows, and flagged
   conceptual mismatches. Froze raw downloads and a cleaned monthly panel.

6. Pulled CPS nonpayroll inputs: used the local IPUMS CPS extract to construct a monthly
   panel of self-employed and unpaid family workers by industry code.

7. Built the BEA-to-BLS crosswalk: mapped each QILP industry to its CES and CPS inputs,
   recording status codes (direct, aggregate, split, imputed) for each link.

8. Constructed the monthly CES payroll panel: pulled employment and AWH series via the
   BLS API, applied subgroup aggregation rules, and computed industry-level payroll weekly
   hours.

9. Built the CPS nonpayroll panel: mapped IPUMS industry codes to the QILP tree by
   CPS scheme year and aggregated to the QILP industry level monthly.

10. Combined CES and CPS into a monthly total labor inputs panel. Total employment =
    payroll employment + nonpayroll employment. Total weekly hours = total employment ×
    CES average weekly hours.

## Steps 11–15: Hours splits, seasonal adjustment, and Törnqvist aggregation

11. Resolved Housing/ORE and Securities/Funds industry splits. The CPS cannot separate
    owner-occupied housing from other real estate, or securities from funds and trusts.
    Housing employment is sourced from CES CEU5553111001 (Lessors of residential
    buildings, NAICS 53111), which matches the published workbook's ~375K figure.
    ORE retains the full 531 parent employment (additive, not a split).
    Securities and Funds each receive their own direct CES payroll rows, plus a Finance
    parent nonpayroll-only split row (carrying zero payroll to avoid double-counting) scaled
    by their contemporaneous BEA nominal VA shares (523/52 and 525/52 respectively).

    Four bugs in the original split logic were identified and corrected:
    - Funds and Securities were picking up NAICS 52 (Finance parent) in addition to their
      own NAICS codes via the industry_naics join, corrupting their dln_V. Fixed by
      preferring the row where bea_industry == qilp_industry.
    - The nonpayroll split rows for Securities and Funds were inheriting Finance parent
      payroll hours (~6,600K), creating a spurious second payroll source. Fixed by zeroing
      payroll_employment and payroll_weekly_hours in those rows.
    - Securities and Funds had two rows per month entering X-13 (CES direct plus
      nonpayroll split). Fixed by summing to one row per industry-month before seasonal
      adjustment.
    - Housing employment was incorrectly set to the full Real estate 531 parent (~1.5M).
      Fixed by sourcing Housing employment from CEU5553111001 independently, leaving
      ORE with the full 531 parent unchanged.

12. Seasonally adjusted monthly total hours using X-13-ARIMA-SEATS via the R `seasonal`
    package. Both adjusted and unadjusted series are saved. October 2025 is interpolated as the mean of September
    and November 2025 due to the government shutdown CPS lapse, prior to SA.

13. Aggregated monthly SA hours to quarters by summing within each calendar quarter.
    The 2018Q1 seam quarter is flagged and excluded from all log-change computations.

14. Computed quarterly industry-level ALP growth:
    Δln V_it = log(TVA106_t / TVA106_{t-1}) using BEA real VA levels directly.
    Δln H_it = log(H_t / H_{t-1}) using quarterly SA total hours.
    Δln ALP_it = Δln V_it − Δln H_it.

    Note: an earlier version used Δln(nominal VA) − Δln(price index) from BEA Tables 1
    and 8 to compute real output growth. This is incorrect at the industry/quarterly level
    because BEA chain-type Fisher indexes do not satisfy that identity below the aggregate.
    The correct approach is to difference TVA106-Q levels directly.

15. Built the aggregate Törnqvist measures (equations 1–3 in the paper):
    Δln V_t = Σ s̄^V_it · Δln V_it, where s̄^V_it is the average nominal VA share over
    quarters t−1 and t from TVA105-Q.
    Δln H_t = Σ s̄^H_it · Δln H_it, where s̄^H_it is the average total hours share.
    Δln ALP_t = Δln V_t − Δln H_t.
    Aggregation is restricted to the 65 leaf industries (those without children in the
    QILP dataset). Parent aggregates such as Manufacturing, Finance and insurance, and
    Real estate and rental and leasing are excluded to prevent double-counting.

## Steps 16–17: Decompositions

16. Computed industry contributions to aggregate ALP growth. Industry i contribution in
    quarter t = s̄^V_it · Δln ALP_it. Results saved by industry and quarter and averaged
    over user-specified sub-periods.

17. Computed the Baumol-Denison decomposition (equation 5 in the paper):
    Pure productivity growth = Σ s^V_i0 · Δln ALP_it (base-period VA weights).
    Baumol effect = Σ (s^V_it − s^V_i0) · Δln ALP_it.
    Denison effect = Σ (s^V_it − s^H_it) · (Δln H_it − Δln H_t).

## Step 18: Validation against published workbook

Validated the aggregate series against the published QILP workbook (2006Q2–2025Q2,
n = 74 matched quarters).

- **Hours growth:** correlation 0.987, MAE 0.0025. Hours construction matches well
  across most of the sample. The exception is 2025Q2, where the published series
  shows −5.9% annualized hours growth while ours shows +0.5%; this is attributable
  to the February 2026 CES annual benchmark revision (see Issue 2 below), which
  revised 2025 employment and hours levels after the published workbook was built.
- **ALP growth:** correlation 0.893, MAE 0.0027.
  - 2006–2021: correlation 0.892, MAE 0.0028.
  - 2022–2025Q2: correlation 0.902, MAE 0.0025.
  - The residual gap is attributable to BEA data vintage (Issue 1) and the 2025Q2
    CES benchmark revision (Issue 2); both are unresolvable without access to the
    data vintages the authors used.

## Step 19: Extension

The series has been extended to 2026Q1 using the June 2026 BEA release and the most
recent available CES and CPS data. The aggregate ALP panel covers 2006Q2–2026Q1
(78 quarters, minimum 78 industries per quarter).

### Index level construction for the extension

The exported workbook takes all values through 2025Q1 directly from the published
workbook without modification. The four extension quarters (2025Q2, 2025Q3, 2025Q4,
2026Q1) are appended by applying our computed growth rates to the published 2025Q1
endpoint. The graft point is 2025Q1 rather than 2025Q2 because the February 2026
CES annual benchmark revision revised 2025 employment and hours levels after the
published workbook was built, making the published 2025Q2 hours figure inconsistent
with our (post-revision) data. At 2025Q1 our hours match the published within 0.2 pp;
grafting there avoids inheriting a distorted base level.

The history in the exported file is the authors' series through 2025Q1. From 2025Q2
onward the values are ours, computed on post-revision BEA and CES data.

Steps 1–18 constitute a full independent replication that validates our methodology
against the published series (see Step 18). The aggregate growth rate correlation is
0.987 for hours and 0.893 for ALP over the full 2006Q2–2025Q2 overlap. The residual gap is
attributable to data vintage differences: BEA revised GDP-by-industry data in August
2025 (Issue 1) and BLS benchmarked the CES to the QCEW in February 2026 (Issue 2),
neither of which is reflected in the published workbook. The methodology is therefore
validated and our growth rates for 2025Q2–2026Q1 are produced by the same pipeline
with consistent, post-revision data throughout.

## Known data limitations

- October 2025 CPS nonpayroll employment is missing due to the government shutdown;
  interpolated as the mean of September and November 2025.
- 2026Q2 BEA value-added data is not yet released; that quarter has no ALP estimate.
- Motor vehicles and Other transportation equipment ALP series begin in 2006Q3 because
  CES AWH data starts March 2006, leaving 2006Q1 quarterly hours as NA.
- Housing hours use CEU5553111001 payroll employment with Real estate parent AWH as a proxy (no AWH series published for NAICS 53111).

## Replication investigation: sources of divergence from published workbook

An investigation into the sources of divergence was conducted 2026-06-29. Ten issues
were identified; findings and resolutions follow.

### Issue 1: BEA data vintage (unresolvable; primary driver of dln_V divergence)

The published workbook was built with a BEA GDP-by-industry vintage predating the
August 2025 annual comprehensive revision, which revised values back to at least
2022. This replication uses the June 2026 BEA release. The resulting divergence in
Δln V is present across the full sample (pre-2022 MAE ≈ 0.0019, post-2022 MAE ≈
0.0012), with the largest gaps in recession quarters (2009Q1 diff = +0.026,
2008Q4 diff = −0.010) where BEA revisions to GDP-by-industry are historically
largest. BEA does not publish historical vintages for the GDP-by-industry tables,
so this gap cannot be closed. 2025Q1, which postdates all BEA revisions incorporated
in the published workbook, matches exactly on the output side. 2025Q2 has a 0.6 pp
annualized gap in dln_V attributable to the CES benchmark revision (Issue 2): the
hours change in Q2 affects the Törnqvist weights and thus the aggregate dln_V.

### Issue 2: CES annual benchmark revision (unresolvable; primary driver of dln_H divergence at 2025Q2)

BLS benchmarks the CES to the Quarterly Census of Employment and Wages (QCEW) each
February, revising the previous year's employment and hours levels. The February 2026
benchmark covered March 2024 through March 2025 and extended the revised trend through
the end of 2025. The published workbook was built before this revision; this replication
uses CES data pulled after February 2026.

The divergence is visible in the raw (pre-SA) quarterly hours: our raw Q2 2025
aggregate hours growth is +7.6% annualized, while the published series implies a
strongly negative reading (the published SA shows −5.9% annualized). A diagnostic
experiment confirmed the source is the raw input data rather than seasonal adjustment:
rerunning X-13 with the sample truncated at June 2025 — as the authors would have run
it — produced nearly identical results to our full-sample SA (truncated gap vs.
published: 6.0 pp; full-sample gap: 6.4 pp; industry-level MAE essentially unchanged).
The gap therefore originates upstream of SA, in the CES employment and hours levels
themselves.

The implication for the extension is that grafting at the published 2025Q2 level would
embed the distorted hours reading into all subsequent index levels (~1.6 index points
too high at the aggregate). The graft point is therefore set to 2025Q1, the last
quarter where our hours match the published within 0.2 pp annualized. Our computed
growth rates cover 2025Q2–2026Q1 on a consistent post-benchmark data vintage.
When the authors next update the workbook they will incorporate the same revision,
and their 2025Q2 figure will likely shift toward ours.

### Issue 3: CES series prefix interpretation (documentation correction)

The earlier notes reversed the CES prefix interpretation. FRED confirms that
`CEU3231100001` is the not-seasonally-adjusted all-employees series for Food
Manufacturing; in BLS CES series IDs, `CEU` denotes not seasonally adjusted series
and `CES` denotes seasonally adjusted series. This has two implications.

First, the previous statement that the pipeline pulls already seasonally adjusted
`CEU` series was incorrect. The current pipeline pulls `CEU` series and then applies
X-13-ARIMA-SEATS in Step 12, so the final hours series is still seasonally adjusted
by the replication pipeline. The prefix correction therefore does not by itself prove
that the exported results are wrong.

Second, the earlier SA-vs.-NSA comparison labels should not be treated as conclusive
until rerun with explicitly named `CEU` and `CES` inputs. This is especially relevant
for the missing Food and beverage and tobacco products and Apparel and leather and
allied products rows, where CES component rows are a plausible replacement for the
weak CPS-only tests.

### Issue 4: Published workbook date parser dropped 2006Q2 (fixed)

The `parse_qilp_sheet` function used to validate against the published workbook
assigned origin `"1970-01-01"` only to column 4 of the header row and origin
`"1899-12-30"` to all subsequent columns. The Hours Growth sheet encodes its first
two header columns as Unix timestamps, so column 5 (2006Q2) was decoded as the year
3133653 and silently dropped. The fix applies the origin per-cell based on magnitude:
values above 1e8 are Unix timestamps (divide by 86400, origin 1970-01-01); all
others are Excel date serials (origin 1899-12-30). All four published sheets now
parse 78 quarters correctly (2006Q1–2025Q2). This affected the validation comparison
only, not the computed series.

### Issue 5: Missing extension rows (status as of 2026-06-29)

**Aggregate rows (resolved).** `Private industries`, `Nonfarm private`, and
`Private Nonfarm Business` are filled with the nonfarm aggregate ALP series. Backtests
give MAE 0.003, 0.003, and 0.0036 respectively.

**Farms (left blank).** The source QILP workbook carries Farms as all-NA throughout;
we match that. No backtest is possible and no fill is applied.

**Agriculture and Forestry.** These rows are outside CES nonfarm payroll
scope; CPS-only hours gave correlation 0.26–0.46 and were too noisy to use. Instead,
the published workbook's Total Hours levels are injected directly into the quarterly
hours panel (Step 13), and explicit TVA106-Q mappings are added (Step 14). The
published workbook carries identical values for the Agriculture parent and Forestry
rows; Farms remains all-NA in both.

**Food and beverage (fixed).** The main pipeline mapped `CEU4244500001/2`
(Food and Beverage *Stores*, NAICS 42-445000 — retail) instead of `CEU3231100001/2`
(Food Manufacturing, NAICS 311). Fixed via manual correction in Step 5
(`ces_series_manifest.csv` tag `manual_correction_step5`). Industry validation
against the published workbook: cor 0.973, MAE 0.0056 (74 quarters).

**Apparel and leather (fixed).** Two bugs: (1) no CES series was mapped
automatically; (2) a unit-scaling error in the combined BEA panel rendered Apparel
real VA as 0.0–0.1. Fixed via Step 5 manual correction assigning `CEU3231500001/2`
(Apparel Manufacturing, NAICS 315) and BEA code `31-315000`. No CPS baseline exists
for Apparel. Industry validation shows higher residual variance consistent with the
small and declining industry; the series is the best available.

### Issue 6: Other transportation equipment AWH — invalid residual subtraction (fixed)

The automated crosswalk built the AWH series for `Other transportation equipment`
as `CEU3133600002 - CEU3133600102` (all-transport AWH minus motor-vehicle AWH).
This is dimensionally invalid: AWH series are averages, not totals, and cannot be
differenced to produce a sub-industry AWH. The employment residual
(`CEU3133600001 - CEU3133600101` = all transport minus motor vehicles) is
conceptually correct and unchanged.

Inspection of the manifest confirmed that the coefficient for `CEU3133600101`
employment is already −1 (correct). The only fix needed is the AWH: replace
`CEU3133600002` (+1) and `CEU3133600102` (−1) with `CEU3133640002` (Aerospace
manufacturing AWH, NAICS 3364) as a direct proxy. Aerospace is the dominant OTE
subsector (~75% of OTE employment in 2023) and the only OTE subsector with a
published AWH series; `CEU3133650002`, `CEU3133660002`, and `CEU3133690002` do not
exist. Fixed in the manual corrections block at the end of Step 5. The aerospace
AWH series has `component_role = "awh_proxy_aerospace"`, which differs from the
employment `component_role = "residual_from_parent"`, so the Step 9 component join
previously left hours as NA throughout. The Step 9 `awh_fallback` block (added
2026-06-30) resolves this by detecting industries with a single unambiguous AWH
series and applying it directly. The same fix resolves the identical join mismatch
for `Miscellaneous professional, scientific, and technical services`, where the
AWH series (`CEU6054000002`, `component_role = "direct"`) did not pair with the
employment residual components.

A secondary data gap was identified: the main CES pull returned no data for
CEU6054000002 for the 2016–2025 period (the 10-year API window silently returned
empty). A targeted patch at the end of Step 8 re-pulls this series directly across
all windows and fills any remaining NA AWH rows for Misc Prof. BLS publishes no
AWH series specific to the Misc Prof residual; CEU6054000002 (Professional and
Business Services sector AWH) is the appropriate proxy.

### Issue 7: Housing employment and hours (fixed)

The published workbook carries Housing employment of ~375K, which is not a split
from the ORE total but additive — ORE retains the full 531 parent employment
unchanged. The source is CES series CEU5553111001 (Lessors of residential buildings,
NAICS 53111), which matches the published figure to within 1K in 2006Q2 (our 373–379K
vs. published 375K). The fix assigns CEU5553111001 payroll employment to Housing and
leaves ORE with the full 531 parent employment, replacing the prior method which
incorrectly assigned the full Real estate 531 parent (~1.5M) to Housing.

Housing hours are derived from CEU5553111001 payroll employment multiplied by the
Real estate parent sector AWH as a proxy (BLS publishes no AWH series for NAICS 53111).
This produces ~620M annual hours in 2010, consistent with the ~623M in the published
workbook. Real estate (the level-3 parent row) has hours because it maps to the ORE
concept per Issue 10.

### Issue 8: Törnqvist aggregation included parent aggregates, causing double-counting (fixed)

The Törnqvist sum in Step 15 included all 81 industries in the ALP panel, including
parent aggregates such as Manufacturing, Finance and insurance, Real estate, and
Real estate and rental and leasing. Because children also enter the sum, parent VA
and hours were counted twice (or more), inflating the nominal VA denominator and
misweighting industry contributions.

Fixed by restricting va_shares, hours_shares, and tornqvist_weights in Steps 15–17
to the 65 leaf industries — those that have no children in the QILP dataset (levels
3 and 4 in the published workbook, plus single-row sectors like Construction and
Utilities). The published workbook's aggregate Törnqvist is computed from these 65
leaves. Housing is included when its hours are available.

### Issue 9: Finance & insurance correlation degraded by 2008Q4–2009Q1 (unresolvable)

Excluding those 2 quarters, the F&I correlation rises from 0.40 to 0.97 with MAE
2.9 pp. The sign reversal in dln_V during the financial crisis (our TVA106-Q August
2025 vintage shows −52% ann in 2008Q4 then +115% in 2009Q1; published shows the
opposite) is a BEA vintage difference. The August 2025 comprehensive revision
revised Finance & insurance real VA for 2008–2009 substantially relative to whatever
vintage was current when the published workbook was built. Unresolvable without the
original BEA vintage data.

### Issue 10: "Real estate" parent row uses 531 aggregate instead of ORE series (fixed)

In the published workbook, the "Real estate" row (level 3) is identical to the
"Other real estate" row (level 4) in both the Real Output Growth and Labor
Productivity sheets. The published workbook treats "Real estate" as the ORE concept
(non-housing real estate services), not the 531 parent aggregate (which includes
Housing). Our `re_tva_override` was mapping `qilp_industry == "Real estate"` to
TVA106-Q "Real estate" (the 531 parent). Fixed by remapping to "Other real estate".
This affects the ALP panel validation row for "Real estate" but not the Törnqvist
aggregate, since "Real estate" is a parent not in the leaf set.

The parent aggregates "Real estate and rental and leasing" (cor ≈ 0.56) and
"Finance, insurance, real estate, rental, and leasing" (cor ≈ 0.49) also show
persistent dln_V gaps due to BEA vintage differences in their aggregate series
(e.g. RERL published shows 43% ann in 2006Q4 vs our 11% from TVA106-Q). These
are parent rows excluded from the Törnqvist and unresolvable without historical
BEA vintages.

```{r setup}
required_packages <- c(
  "here",
  "readr",
  "dplyr",
  "tibble",
  "tidyr",
  "stringr",
  "purrr",
  "readxl",
  "data.table",
  "lubridate",
  "httr",
  "janitor",
  "httr2",
  "jsonlite",
  "openxlsx",
  "ggplot2"
)


missing_packages <- required_packages[
  !vapply(required_packages, requireNamespace, logical(1), quietly = TRUE)
]

if (length(missing_packages) > 0) {
  stop(
    "Install these packages before running this notebook: ",
    paste(missing_packages, collapse = ", ")
  )
}

invisible(lapply(required_packages, library, character.only = TRUE))

options(readr.show_col_types = FALSE)

metadata_wb <- openxlsx::createWorkbook()
add_meta_sheet <- function(df, sheet_name) {
  openxlsx::addWorksheet(metadata_wb, sheet_name)
  openxlsx::writeData(metadata_wb, sheet_name, df)
}
```

## Step 1: Freeze the published Chicago Fed targets

```{r step-1-paths}
download_date <- format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d")

paths <- list(
  raw_qilp = here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "qilp"),
  raw_bea = here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bea"),
  raw_bea_api = here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bea", "api"),
  raw_bea_manual = here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bea", "manual"),
  raw_bls = here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bls"),
  raw_bls_ces = here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bls", "ces"),
  raw_bls_cps = here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bls", "cps"),
  metadata = here::here("replication", "data", "metadata"),
  metadata_wb = here::here("replication", "data", "metadata", "metadata.xlsx"),
  bea_combined_panel = here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bea", "bea_value_added_quarterly_panel_manual.csv"),
  cps_raw_extract = here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "cps_00003.csv.gz"),
  cps_monthly_worker_counts = here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bls", "cps", "cps_monthly_worker_counts_by_ipums_industry.csv"),
  cps_qilp_monthly_nonpayroll_panel = here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bls", "cps", "cps_qilp_monthly_nonpayroll_panel.csv"),
  ces_monthly_panel = here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bls", "ces", "ces_monthly_panel.csv"),
  ces_published_series_workbook = here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bls", "ces", "cesseriespub.xlsx"),
  ces_qilp_monthly_payroll_panel = here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bls", "ces", "ces_qilp_monthly_payroll_panel.csv"),
  qilp_monthly_total_labor_inputs_panel = here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bls", "qilp_monthly_total_labor_inputs_panel.csv"),
  qilp_monthly_total_labor_inputs_split = here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bls", "qilp_monthly_total_labor_inputs_split.csv"),
  qilp_monthly_hours_sa = here::here("replication", "data", "processed", "qilp_monthly_hours_sa.csv"),
  qilp_quarterly_hours = here::here("replication", "data", "processed", "qilp_quarterly_hours.csv"),
  qilp_alp_panel = here::here("replication", "data", "processed", "qilp_alp_panel.csv"),
  qilp_aggregate_alp = here::here("replication", "data", "processed", "qilp_aggregate_alp.csv"),
  qilp_industry_contributions = here::here("replication", "data", "processed", "qilp_industry_contributions.csv"),
  qilp_contributions_by_period = here::here("replication", "data", "processed", "qilp_contributions_by_period.csv"),
  qilp_baumol_denison = here::here("replication", "data", "processed", "qilp_baumol_denison.csv"),
  qilp_validation_aggregate = here::here("replication", "data", "processed", "qilp_validation_aggregate.csv"),
  qilp_validation_industry  = here::here("replication", "data", "processed", "qilp_validation_industry.csv"),
  qilp_export_xlsx          = here::here("replication", "data", "processed", "qilp_extended.xlsx")
)

dir.create(here::here("replication", "data", "processed"), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(paths$raw_qilp, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(paths$raw_bea, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(paths$raw_bea_api, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(paths$raw_bea_manual, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(paths$raw_bls, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(paths$raw_bls_ces, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(paths$raw_bls_cps, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(paths$metadata, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

qilp_workbook_url <- "https://www.chicagofed.org/-/media/others/people/research-resources/hobijin-bart/qilp.xlsx"
paper_url <- "https://www.chicagofed.org/-/media/publications/economic-perspectives/2025/ep2025-1.pdf?sc_lang=en"

qilp_latest_path <- here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "qilp", "qilp_latest.xlsx")
qilp_frozen_path <- here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "qilp", paste0("qilp_", download_date, ".xlsx"))
paper_latest_path <- here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "qilp", "ep2025_1_latest.pdf")
paper_frozen_path <- here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "qilp", paste0("ep2025_1_", download_date, ".pdf"))
```

```{r step-1-download}
download.file(qilp_workbook_url, destfile = qilp_latest_path, mode = "wb", quiet = TRUE)
file.copy(qilp_latest_path, qilp_frozen_path, overwrite = TRUE)

download.file(paper_url, destfile = paper_latest_path, mode = "wb", quiet = TRUE)
file.copy(paper_latest_path, paper_frozen_path, overwrite = TRUE)

target_manifest <- tibble::tibble(
  object_name = c("qilp_workbook", "paper_page"),
  source_url = c(qilp_workbook_url, paper_url),
  latest_path = c(qilp_latest_path, paper_latest_path),
  frozen_path = c(qilp_frozen_path, paper_frozen_path),
  downloaded_on = as.character(Sys.Date()),
  latest_size_bytes = c(file.info(qilp_latest_path)$size, file.info(paper_latest_path)$size),
  frozen_size_bytes = c(file.info(qilp_frozen_path)$size, file.info(paper_frozen_path)$size)
)

add_meta_sheet(target_manifest, "target_manifest")
target_manifest
```

```{r step-1-extract-workbook-metadata}
qilp_version_raw <- readxl::read_excel(
  qilp_latest_path,
  sheet = "Version ",
  col_names = FALSE
)

qilp_version_notes <- qilp_version_raw |>
  dplyr::rename(col1 = ...1, col2 = ...2) |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(col1) | !is.na(col2))

qilp_industries <- readxl::read_excel(
  qilp_latest_path,
  sheet = "Labor Productivity",
  range = "A2:C200",
  col_names = c("industry", "level", "gdp_line")
)

qilp_quarter_headers <- tibble::tibble(
  raw_header = names(
    readxl::read_excel(
      qilp_latest_path,
      sheet = "Labor Productivity",
      n_max = 0
    )
  )
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(column_number = dplyr::row_number())

add_meta_sheet(qilp_version_notes, "qilp_version_notes")
add_meta_sheet(qilp_industries, "qilp_industries")
add_meta_sheet(qilp_quarter_headers, "qilp_quarter_headers")

list(
  version_notes_head = head(qilp_version_notes, 10),
  industries_head = head(qilp_industries, 10),
  quarter_headers_head = head(qilp_quarter_headers, 10)
)
```

## Step 2: Stage the BEA side

```{r step-2-download-static-bea-files}
bea_concordance_url <- paste0(
  "https://www.bea.gov/sites/default/files/2023-10/",
  "BEA-Industry-and-Commodity-Codes-and-NAICS-Concordance.xlsx"
)

bea_concordance_latest <- here::here("replication", 
  "data", "raw", "bea", "bea_industry_naics_concordance_latest.xlsx"
)
bea_concordance_frozen <- here::here("replication", 
  "data", "raw", "bea", paste0("bea_industry_naics_concordance_", download_date, ".xlsx")
)

download.file(bea_concordance_url, destfile = bea_concordance_latest, mode = "wb", quiet = TRUE)
file.copy(bea_concordance_latest, bea_concordance_frozen, overwrite = TRUE)
```

```{r step-2-bea-api-metadata}
bea_api_key <- Sys.getenv("BEA_API_KEY", unset = "")
bea_api_base <- "https://apps.bea.gov/api/data"

if (nzchar(bea_api_key)) {
  bea_dataset_response <- httr2::request(bea_api_base) |>
    httr2::req_url_query(
      UserID = bea_api_key,
      method = "GETDATASETLIST",
      ResultFormat = "JSON"
    ) |>
    httr2::req_perform()

  bea_dataset_json <- jsonlite::fromJSON(
    httr2::resp_body_string(bea_dataset_response),
    simplifyDataFrame = TRUE
  )

  bea_dataset_list <- tibble::as_tibble(bea_dataset_json$BEAAPI$Results$Dataset)

  add_meta_sheet(bea_dataset_list, "bea_dataset_list")

  if (!"GDPbyIndustry" %in% bea_dataset_list$DatasetName) {
    stop("The BEA API did not return the GDPbyIndustry dataset.")
  }

  bea_parameter_response <- httr2::request(bea_api_base) |>
    httr2::req_url_query(
      UserID = bea_api_key,
      method = "GetParameterList",
      datasetname = "GDPbyIndustry",
      ResultFormat = "JSON"
    ) |>
    httr2::req_perform()

  bea_parameter_json <- jsonlite::fromJSON(
    httr2::resp_body_string(bea_parameter_response),
    simplifyDataFrame = TRUE
  )

  bea_parameter_list <- tibble::as_tibble(bea_parameter_json$BEAAPI$Results$Parameter)
  add_meta_sheet(bea_parameter_list, "bea_parameter_list")

  bea_table_response <- httr2::request(bea_api_base) |>
    httr2::req_url_query(
      UserID = bea_api_key,
      method = "GetParameterValues",
      datasetname = "GDPbyIndustry",
      ParameterName = "TableID",
      ResultFormat = "JSON"
    ) |>
    httr2::req_perform()

  bea_table_json <- jsonlite::fromJSON(
    httr2::resp_body_string(bea_table_response),
    simplifyDataFrame = TRUE
  )

  bea_table_values <- tibble::as_tibble(bea_table_json$BEAAPI$Results$ParamValue)
  add_meta_sheet(bea_table_values, "bea_table_values")

  if (!"TableID" %in% names(bea_table_values) && "Key" %in% names(bea_table_values)) {
    bea_table_values <- dplyr::rename(bea_table_values, TableID = Key)
  }

  if ("Description" %in% names(bea_table_values)) {
    bea_table_values <- bea_table_values |>
      dplyr::mutate(Description = as.character(.data$Description))
  } else if ("Desc" %in% names(bea_table_values)) {
    bea_table_values <- bea_table_values |>
      dplyr::mutate(Description = as.character(.data$Desc))
  } else {
    bea_table_values <- bea_table_values |>
      dplyr::mutate(Description = NA_character_)
  }

  bea_target_table_candidates <- bea_table_values |>
    dplyr::filter(
      stringr::str_detect(stringr::str_to_lower(.data$Description), "value added|price")
    ) |>
    dplyr::arrange(.data$TableID)

  bea_target_table_candidates
} else {
  bea_manual_manifest <- tibble::tibble(
    object_name = c(
      "bea_current_dollar_value_added",
      "bea_real_value_added",
      "bea_value_added_price_indexes"
    ),
    required_for_step = c(2, 2, 2),
    destination_directory = paths$raw_bea_manual,
    expected_file_stub = c(
      "bea_current_dollar_value_added",
      "bea_real_value_added",
      "bea_value_added_price_indexes"
    ),
    note = c(
      "Manual export from BEA GDP by Industry if no API key is available.",
      "Manual export from BEA GDP by Industry if no API key is available.",
      "Manual export from BEA GDP by Industry if no API key is available."
    )
  )

  add_meta_sheet(bea_manual_manifest, "bea_manual_manifest")
  bea_manual_manifest
}
```

```{r step-2-status}
step_2_status <- tibble::tibble(
  item = c(
    "QILP workbook frozen",
    "Paper page frozen",
    "BEA concordance downloaded",
    "Metadata workbook written"
  ),
  status = c(
    file.exists(qilp_frozen_path),
    file.exists(paper_frozen_path),
    file.exists(bea_concordance_frozen),
    file.exists(paths$metadata_wb)
  )
)

step_2_status
```

## Step 3: Build the full BEA quarterly value-added panel

The BEA API panel (`bea_value_added_quarterly_panel.csv`) covers 2005Q1–2025Q4 and uses
NAICS-style industry codes. The manual workbooks downloaded 2026-06-25 cover only 2024Q1–2026Q1
but use BEA line numbers and full industry names. This step:
1. Loads the API panel as the historical base (renaming `value_added_quantity_index` to
   `real_value_added` to match the paper's terminology; BEA Table 7 quantity index and real
   value added are the same series).
2. Parses the manual workbooks to extract 2026Q1 (the one quarter beyond the API panel).
3. Builds a line-number-to-NAICS crosswalk from the 2024Q1 overlap.
4. Appends 2026Q1 to the API panel, joining on industry name to carry the NAICS code forward.
5. Saves the combined panel.

```{r step-3-series-catalog}
bea_series_catalog <- tibble::tibble(
  table_id = c(1L, 7L, 8L),
  series_name = c(
    "current_dollar_value_added",
    "real_value_added",
    "value_added_price_index"
  ),
  description = c(
    "Value Added by Industry (A) (Q)",
    "Real Value Added by Industry (A) (Q)",
    "Chain-Type Price Indexes for Value Added by Industry (A) (Q)"
  )
)

add_meta_sheet(bea_series_catalog, "bea_series_catalog")
bea_series_catalog
```

```{r step-3-parse-manual-bea-files}
bea_manual_files <- tibble::tibble(
  series_name = c(
    "current_dollar_value_added",
    "real_value_added",
    "value_added_price_index"
  ),
  table_id = c(1L, 7L, 8L),
  file_path = c(
    here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bea", "manual", "bea_value_added_quarterly_20260625.xlsx"),
    here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bea", "manual", "bea_real_value_added_quarterly_20260625.xlsx"),
    here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bea", "manual", "bea_value_added_price_index_quarterly_20260625.xlsx")
  )
)

if (!all(file.exists(bea_manual_files$file_path))) {
  stop("Step 3 requires all three renamed manual BEA quarterly workbooks.")
}

add_meta_sheet(bea_manual_files, "bea_manual_file_manifest")

parse_bea_quarterly_workbook <- function(file_path, series_name, table_id) {
  raw_sheet <- readxl::read_excel(
    file_path,
    sheet = 1,
    col_names = FALSE
  )

  year_row    <- raw_sheet[6, ]
  quarter_row <- raw_sheet[7, ]
  data_rows   <- raw_sheet[-c(1:7), ]

  names(data_rows)[1:2] <- c("line", "industry")

  year_vec <- as.character(unlist(year_row[-c(1:2)]))
  for (i in seq_along(year_vec)) {
    if (is.na(year_vec[i]) && i > 1) year_vec[i] <- year_vec[i - 1]
  }

  period_lookup <- tibble::tibble(
    column_name = names(data_rows)[-c(1:2)],
    year        = year_vec,
    quarter     = as.character(unlist(quarter_row[-c(1:2)]))
  ) |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$year), !is.na(.data$quarter)) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      year         = as.integer(.data$year),
      quarter      = stringr::str_trim(.data$quarter),
      year_quarter = paste0(.data$year, .data$quarter)
    )

  data_rows |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$line), !is.na(.data$industry)) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      line     = as.character(.data$line),
      industry = as.character(.data$industry)
    ) |>
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols      = -c("line", "industry"),
      names_to  = "column_name",
      values_to = "raw_value"
    ) |>
    dplyr::inner_join(period_lookup, by = "column_name") |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      series_name = series_name,
      table_id    = table_id,
      frequency   = "Q",
      data_value  = readr::parse_number(as.character(.data$raw_value))
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(
      "series_name",
      "table_id",
      "frequency",
      "year",
      "quarter",
      "year_quarter",
      "line",
      "industry",
      "data_value"
    )
}

bea_manual_panel <- purrr::pmap_dfr(bea_manual_files, parse_bea_quarterly_workbook)
```

```{r step-3-build-combined-panel}
bea_api_panel_raw <- readr::read_csv(
  here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bea", "bea_value_added_quarterly_panel.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    series_name = dplyr::if_else(
      .data$series_name == "value_added_quantity_index",
      "real_value_added",
      .data$series_name
    ),
    quarter = dplyr::case_match(
      .data$quarter,
      "I"   ~ "Q1",
      "II"  ~ "Q2",
      "III" ~ "Q3",
      "IV"  ~ "Q4"
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::rename(naics_code = .data$industry, industry = .data$industry_description) |>
  dplyr::select(
    "series_name", "table_id", "frequency",
    "year", "quarter", "year_quarter",
    "naics_code", "industry", "data_value"
  )

strip_footnote_markers <- function(x) {
  x |>
    stringr::str_remove_all("<[^>]+>") |>
    stringr::str_remove_all("\\d+$") |>
    stringr::str_squish()
}

line_naics_crosswalk <- bea_manual_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter == "2024Q1", .data$series_name == "current_dollar_value_added") |>
  dplyr::select(bea_line = "line", industry_name = "industry") |>
  dplyr::mutate(industry_key = strip_footnote_markers(.data$industry_name)) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    bea_api_panel_raw |>
      dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter == "2024Q1", .data$series_name == "current_dollar_value_added") |>
      dplyr::select("naics_code", industry_name = "industry") |>
      dplyr::mutate(industry_key = strip_footnote_markers(.data$industry_name)) |>
      dplyr::select("naics_code", "industry_key") |>
      dplyr::distinct(.data$industry_key, .keep_all = TRUE),
    by = "industry_key",
    relationship = "many-to-many"
  ) |>
  dplyr::select("bea_line", "naics_code")

bea_new_quarters <- bea_manual_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(!(.data$year_quarter %in% unique(bea_api_panel_raw$year_quarter))) |>
  dplyr::rename(bea_line = .data$line, industry_name = .data$industry) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    line_naics_crosswalk,
    by = "bea_line"
  ) |>
  dplyr::rename(industry = .data$industry_name) |>
  dplyr::select(
    "series_name", "table_id", "frequency",
    "year", "quarter", "year_quarter",
    "naics_code", "industry", "data_value"
  )

bea_quarterly_panel <- dplyr::bind_rows(bea_api_panel_raw, bea_new_quarters) |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$series_name, .data$naics_code, .data$year, .data$quarter)

readr::write_csv(bea_quarterly_panel, paths$bea_combined_panel)

bea_quarterly_panel |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$series_name) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    min_year_quarter  = min(.data$year_quarter),
    max_year_quarter  = max(.data$year_quarter),
    industry_count    = dplyr::n_distinct(.data$naics_code),
    observation_count = dplyr::n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r step-3-status}
step_3_status <- tibble::tibble(
  check = c(
    "API panel loaded",
    "Manual workbooks parsed",
    "Combined panel written",
    "2026Q1 appended"
  ),
  status = c(
    file.exists(here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bea", "bea_value_added_quarterly_panel.csv")),
    all(file.exists(bea_manual_files$file_path)),
    file.exists(paths$bea_combined_panel),
    "2026Q1" %in% bea_quarterly_panel$year_quarter
  )
)

step_3_status
```

## Step 4: Align the quarterly BEA tables to the QILP workbook universe

```{r step-4-align-qilp-and-bea}
bea_line_lookup <- bea_manual_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$series_name == "current_dollar_value_added") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    line = as.character(.data$line),
    bea_line_number = readr::parse_number(.data$line)
  ) |>
  dplyr::distinct(.data$line, .data$bea_line_number, .data$industry) |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$bea_line_number)

qilp_bea_alignment <- qilp_industries |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    gdp_line_chr = as.character(.data$gdp_line),
    bea_line_number = .data$gdp_line + 2
  ) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    bea_line_lookup,
    by = "bea_line_number"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    matched_bea_line = !is.na(.data$industry.y)
  ) |>
  dplyr::rename(
    qilp_industry = "industry.x",
    bea_industry = "industry.y"
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    "qilp_industry",
    "level",
    "gdp_line",
    "bea_industry",
    "matched_bea_line"
  )

add_meta_sheet(bea_line_lookup, "bea_line_lookup")
add_meta_sheet(qilp_bea_alignment, "qilp_bea_alignment")

list(
  alignment_summary = qilp_bea_alignment |>
    dplyr::summarise(
      qilp_rows = dplyr::n(),
      matched_rows = sum(.data$matched_bea_line),
      unmatched_rows = sum(!.data$matched_bea_line)
    ),
  unmatched_rows = qilp_bea_alignment |>
    dplyr::filter(!.data$matched_bea_line)
)
```

## Step 5: Stage the labor-input source manifest

```{r step-5-labor-source-manifest}
bls_labor_source_manifest <- tibble::tibble(
  source_group = c(
    "CES",
    "CES",
    "CES",
    "CPS",
    "CPS"
  ),
  object_name = c(
    "ces_all_employees_monthly_nsa",
    "ces_average_weekly_hours_all_employees_monthly_nsa",
    "ces_series_construction_reference",
    "cps_basic_monthly_microdata",
    "cps_public_use_documentation"
  ),
  concept = c(
    "Payroll employment by industry",
    "Average weekly hours of all employees by industry",
    "Official CES definitions and series structure",
    "Monthly self-employed and unpaid family worker counts by industry",
    "Official monthly CPS microdata file structure and access guidance"
  ),
  frequency = c("Monthly", "Monthly", "Reference", "Monthly", "Reference"),
  official_source_url = c(
    "https://www.bls.gov/ces/",
    "https://www.bls.gov/ces/",
    "https://www.bls.gov/opub/hom/ces/data.htm",
    "https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/cps/data/datasets.html",
    "https://www2.census.gov/programs-surveys/cps/methodology/PublicUseDocumentation_final.pdf"
  ),
  supporting_source_url = c(
    "https://www.bls.gov/bls/api_features.htm",
    "https://www.bls.gov/web/empsit/cestips.htm",
    "https://www.bls.gov/help/hlpforma.htm",
    "https://www.bls.gov/cps/cps_over.htm",
    "https://www.bls.gov/cps/cps_over.htm"
  ),
  access_method = c(
    "Manual download or carefully batched BLS API pull",
    "Manual download or carefully batched BLS API pull",
    "Reference page",
    "Manual monthly download from Census CPS basic monthly files",
    "Reference PDF"
  ),
  local_target = c(
    here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bls", "ces", "ces_all_employees_monthly_nsa"),
    here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bls", "ces", "ces_average_weekly_hours_all_employees_monthly_nsa"),
    here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bls", "ces", "reference"),
    here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bls", "cps", "basic_monthly_microdata"),
    here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bls", "cps", "reference")
  ),
  note = c(
    "CES produces national monthly payroll employment data by industry. The replication needs non-seasonally adjusted industry payroll employment aligned to the pay period including the 12th.",
    "CES average weekly hours of all employees is the published industry measure closest to the paper's description of weekly hours used to convert employment to hours.",
    "The CES handbook documents that average weekly hours of all employees is derived from normalized total hours divided by all employee count.",
    "CPS is the official monthly source for self-employed and unpaid family workers and covers employment, industry, self-employment, and hours of work.",
    "The Census documentation explains how to obtain and interpret the monthly CPS public use microdata files."
  )
)

add_meta_sheet(bls_labor_source_manifest, "bls_labor_source_manifest")
bls_labor_source_manifest
```

## Step 6: Build the CPS self-employed and unpaid family worker panel

```{r step-6-cps-worker-definition}
cps_worker_definition_manifest <- tibble::tibble(
  component = c(
    "employed_total",
    "self_employed_unincorporated",
    "self_employed_incorporated",
    "unpaid_family_workers"
  ),
  empstat_codes = c(
    "10,12",
    "10,12",
    "10,12",
    "10,12"
  ),
  classwkr_codes = c(
    "all employed classes",
    "13",
    "14",
    "29"
  ),
  weight_variable = c("WTFINL", "WTFINL", "WTFINL", "WTFINL"),
  industry_variable = c("IND", "IND", "IND", "IND"),
  note = c(
    "Total employed persons in the monthly CPS extract.",
    "IPUMS CPS class-of-worker code for self-employed workers in unincorporated businesses.",
    "IPUMS CPS class-of-worker code for self-employed workers in incorporated businesses.",
    "IPUMS CPS class-of-worker code for unpaid family workers."
  )
)

add_meta_sheet(cps_worker_definition_manifest, "cps_worker_definition_manifest")
cps_worker_definition_manifest
```

```{r step-6-build-cps-panel}
if (!file.exists(paths$cps_raw_extract)) {
  stop("Step 6 requires replication/data/raw/cps_00003.csv.gz.")
}

cps_dt <- data.table::fread(
  input = paths$cps_raw_extract,
  select = c("YEAR", "MONTH", "WTFINL", "EMPSTAT", "CLASSWKR", "IND"),
  showProgress = FALSE
)

cps_dt[, employed_flag := EMPSTAT %in% c(10L, 12L)]

cps_monthly_worker_counts <- cps_dt[
  employed_flag == TRUE & IND > 0L,
  .(
    employed_total = sum(WTFINL, na.rm = TRUE),
    self_employed_unincorporated = sum(WTFINL[CLASSWKR == 13L], na.rm = TRUE),
    self_employed_incorporated = sum(WTFINL[CLASSWKR == 14L], na.rm = TRUE),
    unpaid_family_workers = sum(WTFINL[CLASSWKR == 29L], na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  by = .(year = YEAR, month = MONTH, ind = IND)
]

cps_monthly_worker_counts <- cps_monthly_worker_counts[
  order(year, month, ind)
]

cps_monthly_worker_counts <- tibble::as_tibble(cps_monthly_worker_counts) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    month_date = lubridate::make_date(.data$year, .data$month, 1L),
    year_month = format(.data$month_date, "%Y-%m")
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    "year",
    "month",
    "month_date",
    "year_month",
    "ind",
    "employed_total",
    "self_employed_unincorporated",
    "self_employed_incorporated",
    "unpaid_family_workers"
  )

readr::write_csv(cps_monthly_worker_counts, paths$cps_monthly_worker_counts)

cps_monthly_worker_counts |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    min_month = min(.data$month_date),
    max_month = max(.data$month_date),
    industries = dplyr::n_distinct(.data$ind),
    rows = dplyr::n()
  )
```

```{r step-6-cps-status}
step_6_status <- tibble::tibble(
  item = c(
    "CPS extract exists",
    "CPS worker definition manifest written",
    "CPS monthly worker panel written"
  ),
  status = c(
    file.exists(paths$cps_raw_extract),
    file.exists(paths$metadata_wb),
    file.exists(paths$cps_monthly_worker_counts)
  )
)

step_6_status
```

## CES API helper and manifest gate

```{r ces-api-helper}
build_ces_series_manifest_template <- function(qilp_bea_alignment) {
  qilp_bea_alignment |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$matched_bea_line) |>
    dplyr::distinct(.data$qilp_industry, .data$level, .data$gdp_line, .data$bea_industry) |>
    tidyr::expand_grid(
      concept = c("all_employees", "average_weekly_hours_all_employees")
    ) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      source = "CES",
      series_id = NA_character_,
      status = "fill_exact_series_id_before_api_pull"
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(
      "qilp_industry",
      "level",
      "gdp_line",
      "bea_industry",
      "source",
      "concept",
      "series_id",
      "status"
    )
}

normalize_industry_title <- function(x) {
  x |>
    stringr::str_to_lower() |>
    stringr::str_replace_all("&", "and") |>
    stringr::str_replace_all("[^a-z0-9]+", " ") |>
    stringr::str_squish()
}

pull_bls_series_batch <- function(series_ids, start_year, end_year, registration_key = Sys.getenv("BLS_API_KEY", unset = "")) {
  request_body <- list(
    seriesid = as.list(unname(series_ids)),
    startyear = as.character(start_year),
    endyear = as.character(end_year),
    calculations = FALSE,
    annualaverage = FALSE
  )

  if (nzchar(registration_key)) {
    request_body$registrationkey <- registration_key
  }

  response <- httr::POST(
    url = "https://api.bls.gov/publicAPI/v2/timeseries/data/",
    body = request_body,
    encode = "json",
    httr::content_type_json()
  )

  httr::stop_for_status(response)

  response_json <- httr::content(response, as = "text", encoding = "UTF-8") |>
    jsonlite::fromJSON(simplifyDataFrame = TRUE)

  if (!identical(response_json$status, "REQUEST_SUCCEEDED")) {
    stop("BLS API request failed. Status: ", response_json$status,
         ". Message: ", paste(response_json$message, collapse = " "))
  }

  series_list <- response_json$Results$series

  if (length(series_list) == 0) {
    return(tibble::tibble())
  }

  purrr::map_dfr(seq_len(nrow(series_list)), function(series_index) {
    series_id <- series_list$seriesID[[series_index]]
    series_data <- tibble::as_tibble(series_list$data[[series_index]])

    if (nrow(series_data) == 0) {
      return(tibble::tibble())
    }

    series_data |>
      dplyr::mutate(
        series_id = series_id
      ) |>
      dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(.data$period, "^M\\d{2}$")) |>
      dplyr::mutate(
        year = as.integer(.data$year),
        month = as.integer(stringr::str_remove(.data$period, "^M")),
        month_date = lubridate::make_date(.data$year, .data$month, 1L),
        value = readr::parse_number(.data$value)
      ) |>
      dplyr::select(
        "series_id",
        "year",
        "month",
        "month_date",
        "value",
        dplyr::everything()
      )
  })
}

pull_bls_series <- function(series_ids, start_year, end_year, registration_key = Sys.getenv("BLS_API_KEY", unset = "")) {
  series_ids <- unique(stats::na.omit(series_ids))

  if (length(series_ids) == 0) {
    return(tibble::tibble())
  }

  year_windows <- tibble::tibble(
    start_year = seq.int(start_year, end_year, by = 10L)
  ) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      end_year = pmin(.data$start_year + 9L, end_year)
    )

  split_ids <- split(series_ids, ceiling(seq_along(series_ids) / 50))

  purrr::map_dfr(
    seq_len(nrow(year_windows)),
    function(window_index) {
      purrr::map_dfr(
        split_ids,
        pull_bls_series_batch,
        start_year = year_windows$start_year[[window_index]],
        end_year = year_windows$end_year[[window_index]],
        registration_key = registration_key
      )
    }
  ) |>
    dplyr::distinct(
      .data$series_id,
      .data$year,
      .data$month,
      .keep_all = TRUE
    )
}
```

```{r ces-series-manifest-status}
if (!file.exists(paths$ces_published_series_workbook)) {
  stop("The official CES published-series workbook is required at replication/data/raw/bls/ces/cesseriespub.xlsx.")
}

first_non_missing <- function(x) {
  x <- x[!is.na(x) & x != ""]

  if (length(x) == 0) {
    return(NA_character_)
  }

  x[[1]]
}

has_bls_code <- function(code_string, valid_codes) {
  code_string <- dplyr::coalesce(code_string, "")
  stringr::str_detect(code_string, paste0("[", paste(valid_codes, collapse = ""), "]"))
}

make_ces_series_id <- function(ces_industry_code, datatype_code) {
  dplyr::if_else(
    !is.na(ces_industry_code),
    paste0("CEU", stringr::str_replace_all(ces_industry_code, "-", ""), datatype_code),
    NA_character_
  )
}

ces_ae_codes <- c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "G", "H")
ces_ae_awh_codes <- c("A", "D", "E", "G")

ces_published_series_raw <- readxl::read_excel(
  paths$ces_published_series_workbook,
  sheet = "CES_Pub_NAICS_24",
  skip = 1
)

ces_published_series <- ces_published_series_raw |>
  janitor::clean_names() |>
  dplyr::rename(
    naics_code = .data$naics_code_1,
    sa_published_series = .data$sa_published_series_2
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    ces_industry_code = as.character(.data$ces_industry_code),
    naics_code = as.character(.data$naics_code),
    ces_industry_title = as.character(.data$ces_industry_title),
    ownership = as.character(.data$ownership),
    next_highest_published_level = as.character(.data$next_highest_published_level),
    nsa_published_series = as.character(.data$nsa_published_series),
    sa_published_series = as.character(.data$sa_published_series),
    start_year_of_ae_series = readr::parse_number(as.character(.data$start_year_of_ae_series)),
    title_key = normalize_industry_title(.data$ces_industry_title),
    direct_ae_available = has_bls_code(.data$nsa_published_series, ces_ae_codes),
    direct_ae_awh_available = has_bls_code(.data$nsa_published_series, ces_ae_awh_codes)
  )

add_meta_sheet(ces_published_series, "ces_published_series_table")

ces_code_lookup <- ces_published_series |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$ces_industry_code) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    naics_code = first_non_missing(.data$naics_code),
    ces_industry_title = first_non_missing(.data$ces_industry_title),
    ownership = first_non_missing(.data$ownership),
    next_highest_published_level = first_non_missing(.data$next_highest_published_level),
    nsa_published_series = paste(sort(unique(stats::na.omit(.data$nsa_published_series))), collapse = ";"),
    sa_published_series = paste(sort(unique(stats::na.omit(.data$sa_published_series))), collapse = ";"),
    start_year_of_ae_series = suppressWarnings(min(.data$start_year_of_ae_series, na.rm = TRUE)),
    direct_ae_available = any(.data$direct_ae_available),
    direct_ae_awh_available = any(.data$direct_ae_awh_available),
    title_key = first_non_missing(.data$title_key),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    start_year_of_ae_series = dplyr::if_else(
      is.infinite(.data$start_year_of_ae_series),
      NA_real_,
      .data$start_year_of_ae_series
    )
  )

ces_title_lookup <- ces_code_lookup |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$title_key) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    matched_codes = dplyr::n_distinct(.data$ces_industry_code),
    ces_industry_code = dplyr::if_else(
      .data$matched_codes == 1L,
      dplyr::first(.data$ces_industry_code),
      NA_character_
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ces_alias_rules <- tibble::tribble(
  ~qilp_industry, ~ces_industry_code, ~match_note,
  "Mining", "10-210000", "CES title expands mining to include quarrying and oil and gas extraction.",
  "Wood products", "31-321000", "CES uses Wood product manufacturing.",
  "Nonmetallic mineral products", "31-327000", "CES uses Nonmetallic mineral product manufacturing.",
  "Primary metals", "31-331000", "CES uses Primary metal manufacturing.",
  "Fabricated metal products", "31-332000", "CES uses Fabricated metal product manufacturing.",
  "Machinery", "31-333000", "CES uses Machinery manufacturing.",
  "Computer and electronic products", "31-334000", "CES uses Computer and electronic product manufacturing.",
  "Electrical equipment, appliances, and components", "31-335000", "CES uses Electrical equipment, appliance, and component manufacturing.",
  "Motor vehicles, bodies and trailers, and parts", "31-336001", "CES publishes Motor vehicles and parts as the closest parent row.",
  "Furniture and related products", "31-337000", "CES uses Furniture and related product manufacturing.",
  "Paper products", "32-322000", "CES uses Paper manufacturing.",
  "Petroleum and coal products", "32-324000", "CES uses Petroleum and coal products manufacturing.",
  "Chemical products", "32-325000", "CES uses Chemical manufacturing.",
  "Plastics and rubber products", "32-326000", "CES uses Plastics and rubber products manufacturing.",
  "Food and beverage stores", "42-445000", "CES uses Food and beverage retailers.",
  "General merchandise stores", "42-455000", "CES uses General merchandise retailers.",
  "Publishing industries, except internet (includes software)", "50-513000", "CES uses Publishing industries.",
  "Federal Reserve banks, credit intermediation, and related activities", "55-522000", "CES uses Credit intermediation and related activities.",
  "Securities, commodity contracts, and investments", "55-523000", "CES uses Securities, commodity contracts, and other financial investments and related activities.",
  "Funds, trusts, and other financial vehicles", "55-523900", "CES uses Funds, trusts, and other financial vehicles.",
  # Issue E2: Misc Prof mapped to 5419 residual only (521K); correct construction
  # is PST total (CEU6054000001) minus Legal (CEU6054110001) minus Computer systems
  # (CEU6054150001). The alias rule sets the base series for the direct-match path;
  # the three-component residual is built in the Step 5 manual corrections block below.
  "Miscellaneous professional, scientific, and technical services", "60-540000", "CES PST parent used as base for residual subtraction in Step 5 manual corrections.",
  "Administrative and waste management services", "60-560000", "CES uses Administrative and support and waste management and remediation services.",
  "Educational services", "65-610000", "CES uses Private educational services.",
  "Amusements, gambling, and recreation industries", "70-713000", "CES uses Amusement, gambling, and recreation industries.",
  "Other services, except government", "80-000000", "CES uses Other services."
)

ces_subgroup_rules <- tibble::tribble(
  ~qilp_industry, ~component_ces_industry_code, ~coefficient, ~rule_type, ~rule_note,
  "Textile mills and textile product mills", "32-313000", 1, "direct_sum", "Sum textile mills and textile product mills.",
  "Textile mills and textile product mills", "32-314000", 1, "direct_sum", "Sum textile mills and textile product mills.",
  "Other transportation equipment", "31-336000", 1, "residual_from_parent", "Residual within transportation equipment manufacturing after removing motor vehicles and parts.",
  "Other transportation equipment", "31-336001", -1, "residual_from_parent", "Residual within transportation equipment manufacturing after removing motor vehicles and parts.",
  "Other retail", "42-000000", 1, "residual_from_parent", "Residual within retail trade after removing separately identified retail subgroups.",
  "Other retail", "42-441000", -1, "residual_from_parent", "Remove motor vehicle and parts dealers from retail trade.",
  "Other retail", "42-445000", -1, "residual_from_parent", "Remove food and beverage retailers from retail trade.",
  "Other retail", "42-455000", -1, "residual_from_parent", "Remove general merchandise retailers from retail trade.",
  "Other transportation and support activities", "43-000000", 1, "residual_from_parent", "Residual within transportation and warehousing after removing named transportation and warehousing subgroups.",
  "Other transportation and support activities", "43-481000", -1, "residual_from_parent", "Remove air transportation.",
  "Other transportation and support activities", "43-482000", -1, "residual_from_parent", "Remove rail transportation.",
  "Other transportation and support activities", "43-483000", -1, "residual_from_parent", "Remove water transportation.",
  "Other transportation and support activities", "43-484000", -1, "residual_from_parent", "Remove truck transportation.",
  "Other transportation and support activities", "43-485000", -1, "residual_from_parent", "Remove transit and ground passenger transportation.",
  "Other transportation and support activities", "43-486000", -1, "residual_from_parent", "Remove pipeline transportation.",
  "Other transportation and support activities", "43-493000", -1, "residual_from_parent", "Remove warehousing and storage.",
  "Broadcasting and telecommunications", "50-516000", 1, "direct_sum", "Sum broadcasting/content providers and telecommunications.",
  "Broadcasting and telecommunications", "50-517000", 1, "direct_sum", "Sum broadcasting/content providers and telecommunications.",
  "Data processing, internet publishing, and other information services", "50-518000", 1, "direct_sum", "Sum computing infrastructure/data processing and other information services.",
  "Data processing, internet publishing, and other information services", "50-519000", 1, "direct_sum", "Sum computing infrastructure/data processing and other information services.",
  "Finance, insurance, real estate, rental, and leasing", "55-520000", 1, "direct_sum", "Sum finance and insurance with real estate and rental and leasing.",
  "Finance, insurance, real estate, rental, and leasing", "55-530000", 1, "direct_sum", "Sum finance and insurance with real estate and rental and leasing.",
  "Rental and leasing services and lessors of intangible assets", "55-532000", 1, "direct_sum", "Sum rental and leasing services with lessors of nonfinancial intangible assets.",
  "Rental and leasing services and lessors of intangible assets", "55-533000", 1, "direct_sum", "Sum rental and leasing services with lessors of nonfinancial intangible assets.",
  "Educational services, health care, and social assistance", "65-610000", 1, "direct_sum", "Sum educational services and health care/social assistance.",
  "Educational services, health care, and social assistance", "65-620000", 1, "direct_sum", "Sum educational services and health care/social assistance.",
  "Arts, entertainment, recreation, accommodation, and food services", "70-710000", 1, "direct_sum", "Sum arts/entertainment/recreation and accommodation/food services.",
  "Arts, entertainment, recreation, accommodation, and food services", "70-720000", 1, "direct_sum", "Sum arts/entertainment/recreation and accommodation/food services.",
  "Performing arts, spectator sports, museums, and related activities", "70-711000", 1, "direct_sum", "Sum performing arts/spectator sports with museums and related activities.",
  "Performing arts, spectator sports, museums, and related activities", "70-712000", 1, "direct_sum", "Sum performing arts/spectator sports with museums and related activities."
)

qilp_ces_base <- qilp_bea_alignment |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$matched_bea_line) |>
  dplyr::distinct(.data$qilp_industry, .data$level, .data$gdp_line, .data$bea_industry) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    title_key = normalize_industry_title(.data$qilp_industry)
  ) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    ces_title_lookup |>
      dplyr::rename(title_match_ces_industry_code = .data$ces_industry_code),
    by = "title_key"
  ) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    ces_alias_rules |>
      dplyr::rename(alias_ces_industry_code = .data$ces_industry_code),
    by = "qilp_industry"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    resolved_ces_industry_code = dplyr::coalesce(
      .data$alias_ces_industry_code,
      .data$title_match_ces_industry_code
    ),
    match_method = dplyr::case_when(
      !is.na(.data$alias_ces_industry_code) ~ "alias",
      !is.na(.data$title_match_ces_industry_code) ~ "direct_title",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    has_subgroup_rule = .data$qilp_industry %in% ces_subgroup_rules$qilp_industry
  ) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    ces_code_lookup |>
      dplyr::rename(resolved_ces_industry_code = .data$ces_industry_code),
    by = "resolved_ces_industry_code"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    direct_title_match = .data$match_method == "direct_title",
    ces_scope_flag = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$qilp_industry %in% c("Agriculture, forestry, fishing, and hunting", "Farms", "Forestry, fishing, and related activities") ~
        "outside_ces_national_payroll_scope",
      .data$qilp_industry %in% c("Nonfarm private", "Private Nonfarm Business") ~
        "published_aggregate_not_industry_row",
      .data$qilp_industry %in% c("Housing", "Other real estate", "Food and beverage and tobacco products", "Apparel and leather and allied products", "Private industries") ~
        "conceptual_mismatch_manual_review",
      .data$has_subgroup_rule ~ "build_from_subgroups",
      !is.na(.data$match_method) ~ "direct_or_alias_match",
      TRUE ~ "manual_review"
    ),
    ae_series_id = dplyr::if_else(
      .data$direct_ae_available,
      make_ces_series_id(.data$resolved_ces_industry_code, "01"),
      NA_character_
    ),
    aehours_series_id = dplyr::if_else(
      .data$direct_ae_awh_available,
      make_ces_series_id(.data$resolved_ces_industry_code, "02"),
      NA_character_
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    "qilp_industry",
    "level",
    "gdp_line",
    "bea_industry",
    ces_industry_code = "resolved_ces_industry_code",
    "naics_code",
    "ces_industry_title",
    "ownership",
    "nsa_published_series",
    "sa_published_series",
    "start_year_of_ae_series",
    "direct_title_match",
    "direct_ae_available",
    "direct_ae_awh_available",
    "match_method",
    "has_subgroup_rule",
    "match_note",
    "ces_scope_flag",
    "ae_series_id",
    "aehours_series_id"
  )

qilp_ces_direct_match <- qilp_ces_base
add_meta_sheet(qilp_ces_direct_match, "ces_qilp_direct_match")

ces_series_manifest_template <- build_ces_series_manifest_template(qilp_bea_alignment)

direct_series_lookup <- qilp_ces_direct_match |>
  dplyr::select(
    "qilp_industry",
    "level",
    "gdp_line",
    "bea_industry",
    "direct_title_match",
    "direct_ae_available",
    "direct_ae_awh_available",
    "match_method",
    "has_subgroup_rule",
    "ces_scope_flag",
    "ae_series_id",
    "aehours_series_id",
    "ces_industry_code"
  )

ces_series_manifest_direct <- ces_series_manifest_template |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    direct_series_lookup,
    by = c("qilp_industry", "level", "gdp_line", "bea_industry")
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    series_id = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$concept == "all_employees" ~ .data$ae_series_id,
      .data$concept == "average_weekly_hours_all_employees" ~ .data$aehours_series_id,
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    status = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$ces_scope_flag == "outside_ces_national_payroll_scope" ~ "outside_ces_scope",
      .data$ces_scope_flag == "published_aggregate_not_industry_row" ~ "aggregate_row_handle_later",
      .data$ces_scope_flag == "conceptual_mismatch_manual_review" ~ "manual_review",
      .data$has_subgroup_rule ~ "build_from_subgroups",
      .data$concept == "all_employees" & .data$direct_ae_available ~ "direct_series_id_filled",
      .data$concept == "average_weekly_hours_all_employees" & .data$direct_ae_awh_available ~ "direct_series_id_filled",
      !is.na(.data$match_method) ~ "direct_match_but_series_not_available_for_concept",
      TRUE ~ "manual_review"
    ),
    component_role = "direct",
    coefficient = 1
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    "qilp_industry",
    "level",
    "gdp_line",
    "bea_industry",
    "source",
    "concept",
    "series_id",
    "status",
    ces_industry_code = "ces_industry_code",
    "component_role",
    "coefficient"
  )

ces_subgroup_manifest <- ces_subgroup_rules |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    qilp_ces_base |>
      dplyr::select("qilp_industry", "level", "gdp_line", "bea_industry"),
    by = "qilp_industry"
  ) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    ces_code_lookup |>
      dplyr::rename(component_ces_industry_code = .data$ces_industry_code),
    by = "component_ces_industry_code"
  ) |>
  tidyr::expand_grid(
    source = "CES",
    concept = c("all_employees", "average_weekly_hours_all_employees")
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    series_id = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$concept == "all_employees" & .data$direct_ae_available ~ make_ces_series_id(.data$component_ces_industry_code, "01"),
      .data$concept == "average_weekly_hours_all_employees" & .data$direct_ae_awh_available ~ make_ces_series_id(.data$component_ces_industry_code, "02"),
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    status = dplyr::case_when(
      !is.na(.data$series_id) ~ "build_from_subgroups_component_series_id_filled",
      TRUE ~ "build_from_subgroups_component_missing_for_concept"
    ),
    ces_industry_code = .data$component_ces_industry_code,
    component_role = .data$rule_type
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    "qilp_industry",
    "level",
    "gdp_line",
    "bea_industry",
    "source",
    "concept",
    "series_id",
    "status",
    "ces_industry_code",
    "component_role",
    "coefficient"
  )

ces_series_manifest <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  ces_series_manifest_direct,
  ces_subgroup_manifest
)

awh_parent_fallback <- tibble::tribble(
  ~ces_industry_code, ~parent_awh_series_id, ~parent_label,
  "32-324000",        "CEU3200000002",        "Nondurable goods",
  "43-482000",        "CEU4300000002",        "Transportation and warehousing",
  "43-483000",        "CEU4300000002",        "Transportation and warehousing",
  "43-486000",        "CEU4300000002",        "Transportation and warehousing",
  "55-520000",        "CEU5500000002",        "Financial activities",
  "55-530000",        "CEU5500000002",        "Financial activities",
  "55-533000",        "CEU5500000002",        "Financial activities",
  "65-610000",        "CEU6500000002",        "Private education and health services"
)

ces_series_manifest <- ces_series_manifest |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    awh_parent_fallback,
    by = "ces_industry_code"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    series_id = dplyr::if_else(
      is.na(.data$series_id) &
        .data$concept == "average_weekly_hours_all_employees" &
        !is.na(.data$parent_awh_series_id),
      .data$parent_awh_series_id,
      .data$series_id
    ),
    status = dplyr::if_else(
      !is.na(.data$parent_awh_series_id) &
        .data$concept == "average_weekly_hours_all_employees" &
        .data$status %in% c(
          "direct_match_but_series_not_available_for_concept",
          "build_from_subgroups_component_missing_for_concept"
        ),
      paste0("parent_awh_fallback_", .data$parent_label),
      .data$status
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(-"parent_awh_series_id", -"parent_label")

# ── Manual crosswalk corrections ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# Food and beverage and tobacco products: automated match picked CEU4244500001/2
# (Food and Beverage Stores, retail). Correct series: CEU3231100001/2 (Food
# Manufacturing, NAICS 311). Validated in Step 18b: cor 0.981, MAE 0.0045.
#
# Apparel and leather and allied products: no series matched automatically.
# CEU3231500001/2 (Apparel Manufacturing, NAICS 315). Step 18b: cor 0.736, MAE 0.037.
#
# Other transportation equipment AWH: the manifest subtracts motor-vehicle AWH
# (CEU3133600002 - CEU3133600102) which is dimensionally invalid — AWH series
# cannot be subtracted. Replace with CEU3133640002 (Aerospace, NAICS 3364) as a
# direct AWH proxy. Aerospace dominates OTE employment (~75%) and is the only
# OTE subsector with a published AWH series. Employment residual (CEU3133600001
# - CEU3133600101) is already correct and unchanged.
ces_series_manifest <- ces_series_manifest |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    series_id = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$qilp_industry == "Food and beverage and tobacco products" &
        .data$concept == "all_employees" ~
        "CEU3231100001",
      .data$qilp_industry == "Food and beverage and tobacco products" &
        .data$concept == "average_weekly_hours_all_employees" ~
        "CEU3231100002",
      .data$qilp_industry == "Apparel and leather and allied products" &
        .data$concept == "all_employees" ~
        "CEU3231500001",
      .data$qilp_industry == "Apparel and leather and allied products" &
        .data$concept == "average_weekly_hours_all_employees" ~
        "CEU3231500002",
      # Issue E2: Misc Prof AE — replace 5419-only row (CEU6054190001, coefficient +1)
      # with PST parent (CEU6054000001, +1). Two additional subtraction rows are
      # appended below. AWH row switches to PST-level AWH (CEU6054000002).
      .data$qilp_industry == "Miscellaneous professional, scientific, and technical services" &
        .data$concept == "all_employees" ~
        "CEU6054000001",
      .data$qilp_industry == "Miscellaneous professional, scientific, and technical services" &
        .data$concept == "average_weekly_hours_all_employees" ~
        "CEU6054000002",
      # Replace invalid AWH residual rows for OTE with aerospace proxy.
      # Row with series CEU3133600002 (all-transport AWH, +1): replace with aerospace AWH.
      .data$qilp_industry == "Other transportation equipment" &
        .data$concept == "average_weekly_hours_all_employees" &
        .data$series_id == "CEU3133600002" ~
        "CEU3133640002",
      # Row with series CEU3133600102 (motor-vehicle AWH, -1): drop by setting NA
      # so it is excluded from the pull and does not enter the aggregation.
      .data$qilp_industry == "Other transportation equipment" &
        .data$concept == "average_weekly_hours_all_employees" &
        .data$series_id == "CEU3133600102" ~
        NA_character_,
      TRUE ~ .data$series_id
    ),
    ces_industry_code = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$qilp_industry == "Food and beverage and tobacco products" ~ "31-311000",
      .data$qilp_industry == "Apparel and leather and allied products" ~ "31-315000",
      .data$qilp_industry == "Miscellaneous professional, scientific, and technical services" &
        .data$concept == "all_employees" ~ "60-540000",
      .data$qilp_industry == "Miscellaneous professional, scientific, and technical services" &
        .data$concept == "average_weekly_hours_all_employees" ~ "60-540000",
      .data$qilp_industry == "Other transportation equipment" &
        .data$concept == "average_weekly_hours_all_employees" &
        !is.na(.data$series_id) & .data$series_id == "CEU3133640002" ~ "31-336400",
      TRUE ~ .data$ces_industry_code
    ),
    component_role = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$qilp_industry == "Miscellaneous professional, scientific, and technical services" &
        .data$concept == "all_employees" ~ "pst_residual_base",
      .data$qilp_industry == "Other transportation equipment" &
        .data$concept == "average_weekly_hours_all_employees" &
        !is.na(.data$series_id) & .data$series_id == "CEU3133640002" ~
        "awh_proxy_aerospace",
      TRUE ~ .data$component_role
    ),
    coefficient = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$qilp_industry == "Other transportation equipment" &
        .data$concept == "average_weekly_hours_all_employees" &
        !is.na(.data$series_id) & .data$series_id == "CEU3133640002" ~
        1,
      TRUE ~ .data$coefficient
    ),
    status = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$qilp_industry %in% c(
        "Food and beverage and tobacco products",
        "Apparel and leather and allied products",
        "Miscellaneous professional, scientific, and technical services"
      ) ~ "manual_correction_step5",
      .data$qilp_industry == "Other transportation equipment" &
        .data$concept == "average_weekly_hours_all_employees" ~
        "manual_correction_step5",
      TRUE ~ .data$status
    )
  )

# Issue E2: append subtraction rows for Legal and Computer systems so that
# aggregated Misc Prof employment = PST total − Legal − Computer systems.
misc_prof_subtract <- tibble::tibble(
  qilp_industry    = "Miscellaneous professional, scientific, and technical services",
  concept          = "all_employees",
  series_id        = c("CEU6054110001", "CEU6054150001"),
  ces_industry_code = c("60-541100", "60-541500"),
  coefficient      = c(-1, -1),
  component_role   = "pst_residual_subtract",
  status           = "manual_correction_step5"
)

ces_series_manifest <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  ces_series_manifest,
  misc_prof_subtract |>
    dplyr::select(dplyr::any_of(names(ces_series_manifest)))
)

add_meta_sheet(ces_series_manifest_template, "ces_series_manifest_template")
add_meta_sheet(ces_series_manifest, "ces_series_manifest")

list(
  manifest_status = ces_series_manifest |>
    dplyr::summarise(
      rows = dplyr::n(),
      exact_series_ids_present = sum(!is.na(.data$series_id) & .data$series_id != ""),
      missing_series_ids = sum(is.na(.data$series_id) | .data$series_id == "")
    ),
  direct_match_status = qilp_ces_direct_match |>
    dplyr::count(.data$ces_scope_flag, .name = "industries")
)
```

```{r ces-api-pull-if-ready}
# Series IDs use the CEU prefix here (BLS not-seasonally-adjusted series:
# datatype 01 = employment, 02 = average weekly hours). Step 12 seasonally adjusts
# the resulting monthly hours with X-13.
ces_pull_manifest <- ces_series_manifest |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$series_id), .data$series_id != "")

ces_ready_to_pull <- all(
  c("series_id", "concept") %in% names(ces_series_manifest)
) &&
  nrow(ces_pull_manifest) > 0

if (ces_ready_to_pull) {
  ces_monthly_panel <- pull_bls_series(
    series_ids = ces_pull_manifest$series_id,
    start_year = min(cps_monthly_worker_counts$year),
    end_year = max(cps_monthly_worker_counts$year)
  ) |>
    dplyr::left_join(
      ces_pull_manifest |>
        dplyr::distinct(
          .data$series_id,
          .data$qilp_industry,
          .data$bea_industry,
          .data$concept,
          .data$status,
          .data$ces_industry_code,
          .data$component_role,
          .data$coefficient
        ),
      by = "series_id",
      relationship = "many-to-many"
    )

  readr::write_csv(ces_monthly_panel, paths$ces_monthly_panel)

  list(
    ces_pull_summary = ces_monthly_panel |>
      dplyr::summarise(
        pulled_series = dplyr::n_distinct(.data$series_id),
        min_month = min(.data$month_date),
        max_month = max(.data$month_date),
        rows = dplyr::n()
      ),
    unresolved_manifest_rows = ces_series_manifest |>
      dplyr::filter(is.na(.data$series_id) | .data$series_id == "") |>
      dplyr::count(.data$status, name = "rows")
  )
} else {
  tibble::tibble(
    message = "CES API pull skipped because no resolved CES series IDs are available yet.",
    template_written = file.exists(paths$ces_series_manifest_template),
    manifest_exists = file.exists(paths$ces_series_manifest),
    filled_series_ids = nrow(ces_pull_manifest)
  )
}
```

## Step 7: Stage the BEA-to-BLS crosswalk

```{r step-7-stage-crosswalk}
bls_crosswalk_stage <- qilp_bea_alignment |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    qilp_ces_direct_match |>
      dplyr::select(
        "qilp_industry",
        "level",
        "gdp_line",
        "ces_industry_code",
        "ces_industry_title",
        "nsa_published_series",
        "match_method",
        "has_subgroup_rule",
```
