```{r step-7-stage-crosswalk}
        "ae_series_id",
        "aehours_series_id",
        "ces_scope_flag"
      ),
    by = c("qilp_industry", "level", "gdp_line")
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    crosswalk_status = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$ces_scope_flag == "outside_ces_national_payroll_scope" ~ "outside_ces_scope",
      .data$ces_scope_flag == "published_aggregate_not_industry_row" ~ "aggregate_row_handle_later",
      .data$ces_scope_flag == "conceptual_mismatch_manual_review" ~ "manual_review",
      .data$has_subgroup_rule ~ "build_from_subgroups",
      !is.na(.data$ae_series_id) & !is.na(.data$aehours_series_id) ~ "direct_ces_employment_and_hours",
      !is.na(.data$ae_series_id) & is.na(.data$aehours_series_id) ~ "direct_ces_employment_only",
      !is.na(.data$match_method) ~ "candidate_direct_match_missing_concept",
      TRUE ~ "manual_review"
    ),
    cps_component_required = .data$matched_bea_line,
    notes = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$crosswalk_status == "outside_ces_scope" ~
        "QILP row is not directly in CES national private payroll scope.",
      .data$crosswalk_status == "aggregate_row_handle_later" ~
        "QILP row is an aggregate that should be handled after lower-level industries are completed.",
      .data$crosswalk_status == "build_from_subgroups" ~
        "QILP row should be constructed from CES subgroup series rather than a single CES parent row.",
      .data$crosswalk_status == "direct_ces_employment_only" ~
        "CES employment appears directly available, but CES hours still need confirmation or subgroup handling.",
      .data$crosswalk_status == "candidate_direct_match_missing_concept" ~
        "CES match exists, but the requested concept is not directly published at that row.",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    "qilp_industry",
    "level",
    "gdp_line",
    "bea_industry",
    "matched_bea_line",
    "ces_industry_code",
    "ces_industry_title",
    "nsa_published_series",
    "ae_series_id",
    "aehours_series_id",
    "cps_component_required",
    "crosswalk_status",
    "notes"
  )

add_meta_sheet(bls_crosswalk_stage, "bls_crosswalk_stage")

bls_crosswalk_stage |>
  dplyr::count(.data$crosswalk_status, name = "industries")
```

## Step 8: Build the monthly CES payroll industry panel

```{r step-8-build-ces-payroll-panel}
if (!file.exists(paths$ces_monthly_panel)) {
  stop("Step 8 requires the resolved CES monthly panel from the API pull step.")
}

ces_monthly_panel <- readr::read_csv(paths$ces_monthly_panel, show_col_types = FALSE)

ces_employment_components <- ces_monthly_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$concept == "all_employees") |>
  dplyr::select(
    "qilp_industry",
    "bea_industry",
    "month_date",
    "series_id",
    "ces_industry_code",
    "component_role",
    "coefficient",
    employment_value = "value"
  )

ces_hours_components <- ces_monthly_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$concept == "average_weekly_hours_all_employees") |>
  dplyr::select(
    "qilp_industry",
    "bea_industry",
    "month_date",
    "ces_industry_code",
    "component_role",
    "coefficient",
    hours_value = "value"
  )

ces_component_panel <- ces_employment_components |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    ces_hours_components,
    by = c(
      "qilp_industry",
      "bea_industry",
      "month_date",
      "ces_industry_code",
      "component_role",
      "coefficient"
    ),
    relationship = "one-to-one"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    component_payroll_weekly_hours = .data$employment_value * .data$hours_value,
    hours_component_available = !is.na(.data$hours_value)
  )

ces_qilp_monthly_payroll_panel <- ces_component_panel |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$qilp_industry, .data$bea_industry, .data$month_date) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    payroll_employment = sum(.data$coefficient * .data$employment_value, na.rm = TRUE),
    payroll_weekly_hours = ifelse(
      all(.data$hours_component_available),
      sum(.data$coefficient * .data$component_payroll_weekly_hours, na.rm = TRUE),
      NA_real_
    ),
    employment_components = dplyr::n_distinct(.data$series_id),
    hours_components = sum(.data$hours_component_available),
    all_hours_components_available = all(.data$hours_component_available),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    payroll_average_weekly_hours = dplyr::if_else(
      .data$all_hours_components_available & .data$payroll_employment != 0,
      .data$payroll_weekly_hours / .data$payroll_employment,
      NA_real_
    ),
    year = lubridate::year(.data$month_date),
    month = lubridate::month(.data$month_date)
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    "qilp_industry",
    "bea_industry",
    "year",
    "month",
    "month_date",
    "payroll_employment",
    "payroll_average_weekly_hours",
    "payroll_weekly_hours",
    "employment_components",
    "hours_components",
    "all_hours_components_available"
  )

# Fix: AWH component-role mismatch for OTE and Misc Prof.
# For OTE the AWH series has component_role "awh_proxy_aerospace" (not
# "residual_from_parent"), so the employment-hours join above leaves hours NA.
# For Misc Prof the AWH series has component_role "direct" but employment
# components use "pst_residual_base"/"pst_residual_subtract", same mismatch.
# Resolution: for any industry where all employment components are present but
# hours are still NA, look up the single AWH series directly from ces_monthly_panel
# and apply it to payroll_employment.
awh_fallback <- ces_monthly_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$concept == "average_weekly_hours_all_employees") |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$qilp_industry, .data$bea_industry, .data$month_date) |>
  dplyr::filter(dplyr::n() == 1L) |>
  dplyr::summarise(fallback_awh = .data$value, .groups = "drop")

ces_qilp_monthly_payroll_panel <- ces_qilp_monthly_payroll_panel |>
  dplyr::left_join(awh_fallback, by = c("qilp_industry", "bea_industry", "month_date")) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    payroll_average_weekly_hours = dplyr::if_else(
      is.na(.data$payroll_average_weekly_hours) & !is.na(.data$fallback_awh),
      .data$fallback_awh,
      .data$payroll_average_weekly_hours
    ),
    payroll_weekly_hours = dplyr::if_else(
      is.na(.data$payroll_weekly_hours) & !is.na(.data$fallback_awh),
      .data$payroll_employment * .data$fallback_awh,
      .data$payroll_weekly_hours
    ),
    all_hours_components_available = dplyr::if_else(
      !.data$all_hours_components_available & !is.na(.data$fallback_awh),
      TRUE,
      .data$all_hours_components_available
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(-"fallback_awh")

# CEU6054000002 (PST-level AWH, proxy for Misc Prof) has a known gap in the
# main CES pull: the 2015–2024 window returned no data for this series.
# Patch by pulling it directly and filling any remaining NA AWH rows.
misc_awh_patch_raw <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  pull_bls_series_batch("CEU6054000002", 2016L, 2025L),
  pull_bls_series_batch("CEU6054000002", 2005L, 2015L),
  pull_bls_series_batch("CEU6054000002", 2026L, as.integer(format(Sys.Date(), "%Y")))
) |>
  dplyr::distinct(.data$year, .data$month, .keep_all = TRUE) |>
  dplyr::select(year = "year", month = "month", patch_awh = "value")

ces_qilp_monthly_payroll_panel <- ces_qilp_monthly_payroll_panel |>
  dplyr::left_join(misc_awh_patch_raw, by = c("year", "month")) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    payroll_average_weekly_hours = dplyr::if_else(
      grepl("Miscellaneous professional", .data$qilp_industry) &
        is.na(.data$payroll_average_weekly_hours) &
        !is.na(.data$patch_awh),
      .data$patch_awh,
      .data$payroll_average_weekly_hours
    ),
    payroll_weekly_hours = dplyr::if_else(
      grepl("Miscellaneous professional", .data$qilp_industry) &
        is.na(.data$payroll_weekly_hours) &
        !is.na(.data$patch_awh),
      .data$payroll_employment * .data$patch_awh,
      .data$payroll_weekly_hours
    ),
    all_hours_components_available = dplyr::if_else(
      grepl("Miscellaneous professional", .data$qilp_industry) &
        !.data$all_hours_components_available &
        !is.na(.data$patch_awh),
      TRUE,
      .data$all_hours_components_available
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(-"patch_awh")

readr::write_csv(ces_qilp_monthly_payroll_panel, paths$ces_qilp_monthly_payroll_panel)

list(
  payroll_panel_summary = ces_qilp_monthly_payroll_panel |>
    dplyr::summarise(
      industries = dplyr::n_distinct(.data$qilp_industry),
      min_month = min(.data$month_date),
      max_month = max(.data$month_date),
      rows = dplyr::n()
    ),
  incomplete_hours_rows = ces_qilp_monthly_payroll_panel |>
    dplyr::filter(!.data$all_hours_components_available) |>
    dplyr::count(.data$qilp_industry, name = "months_missing_hours") |>
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(.data$months_missing_hours))
)
```

## Step 9: Build the CPS industry crosswalk and nonpayroll panel

```{r step-9-build-cps-crosswalk}
scrape_ipums_industry_codes <- function(source_url, scheme_label, start_year, end_year) {
  source_html <- httr2::request(source_url) |>
    httr2::req_perform() |>
    httr2::resp_body_string()

  extracted_pairs <- stringr::str_match_all(
    source_html,
    "<dt>(\\d{4})</dt>\\s*<dd>(.*?)</dd>"
  )[[1]]

  tibble::tibble(
    scheme_label = scheme_label,
    start_year = start_year,
    end_year = end_year,
    ind = as.integer(extracted_pairs[, 2]),
    cps_industry_title = extracted_pairs[, 3]
  ) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      cps_industry_title = .data$cps_industry_title |>
        stringr::str_replace_all("<.*?>", "") |>
        stringr::str_replace_all("&amp;", "&") |>
        stringr::str_replace_all("\\s+", " ") |>
        stringr::str_squish()
    )
}

build_qilp_hierarchy <- function(qilp_industries) {
  hierarchy_input <- qilp_industries |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      level_num = suppressWarnings(as.integer(.data$level))
    ) |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$level_num)) |>
    dplyr::arrange(.data$gdp_line)

  parent_industry <- character(nrow(hierarchy_input))
  parent_industry[] <- NA_character_
  level_stack <- rep(NA_character_, max(hierarchy_input$level_num) + 1L)
  names(level_stack) <- as.character(seq.int(0L, max(hierarchy_input$level_num)))

  for (row_index in seq_len(nrow(hierarchy_input))) {
    current_level <- hierarchy_input$level_num[[row_index]]

    if (current_level > 0L) {
      parent_industry[[row_index]] <- level_stack[[as.character(current_level - 1L)]]
    }

    level_stack[names(level_stack) %in% as.character(seq.int(current_level + 1L, max(hierarchy_input$level_num)))] <- NA_character_
    level_stack[[as.character(current_level)]] <- hierarchy_input$industry[[row_index]]
  }

  hierarchy_input |>
    dplyr::mutate(parent_industry = parent_industry)
}

build_ancestor_map <- function(qilp_hierarchy, terminal_industries) {
  parent_lookup <- stats::setNames(qilp_hierarchy$parent_industry, qilp_hierarchy$industry)

  purrr::map_dfr(
    terminal_industries,
    function(current_industry) {
      ancestor_chain <- current_industry
      next_parent <- parent_lookup[[current_industry]]

      while (!is.na(next_parent)) {
        ancestor_chain <- c(ancestor_chain, next_parent)
        next_parent <- parent_lookup[[next_parent]]
      }

      tibble::tibble(
        terminal_qilp_industry = current_industry,
        qilp_industry = ancestor_chain
      )
    }
  )
}

cps_scheme_manifest <- tibble::tribble(
  ~scheme_label, ~start_year, ~end_year, ~source_url,
  "2003_2008", 2003L, 2008L, "https://cps.ipums.org/cps/codes/ind_20032008_codes.shtml",
  "2009_2013", 2009L, 2013L, "https://cps.ipums.org/cps/codes/ind_2009_codes.shtml",
  "2014_2019", 2014L, 2019L, "https://cps.ipums.org/cps/codes/ind_2014_codes.shtml",
  "2020_2024", 2020L, 2024L, "https://cps.ipums.org/cps/codes/ind_2020_codes.shtml",
  "2025_onward", 2025L, 2100L, "https://cps.ipums.org/cps/codes/ind_2025_codes.shtml"
)

cps_industry_code_lookup <- purrr::pmap_dfr(
  cps_scheme_manifest,
  scrape_ipums_industry_codes
) |>
  tidyr::expand_grid(
    year = min(cps_monthly_worker_counts$year):max(cps_monthly_worker_counts$year)
  ) |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$year >= .data$start_year, .data$year <= .data$end_year) |>
  dplyr::select("scheme_label", "year", "ind", "cps_industry_title")

add_meta_sheet(cps_industry_code_lookup, "cps_industry_code_lookup")

observed_cps_industries <- cps_monthly_worker_counts |>
  dplyr::distinct(.data$year, .data$ind)

cps_qilp_crosswalk_stage <- observed_cps_industries |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    cps_industry_code_lookup,
    by = c("year", "ind")
  ) |>
  dplyr::distinct(.data$year, .data$scheme_label, .data$ind, .data$cps_industry_title) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    terminal_qilp_industry = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$ind %in% c(370L) ~ "Oil and gas extraction",
      .data$ind %in% c(380L, 390L, 470L, 480L) ~ "Mining, except oil and gas",
      .data$ind %in% c(490L) ~ "Support activities for mining",
      .data$ind %in% c(570L, 580L, 590L, 670L, 680L, 690L) ~ "Utilities",
      .data$ind %in% c(770L) ~ "Construction",
      .data$ind %in% c(1070L, 1080L, 1090L, 1170L, 1180L, 1190L, 1270L, 1280L, 1290L, 1370L, 1390L) ~ "Food and beverage and tobacco products",
      .data$ind %in% c(1470L, 1480L, 1490L, 1570L, 1590L, 1670L) ~ "Textile mills and textile product mills",
      .data$ind %in% c(1680L, 1690L, 1691L, 1770L, 1790L) ~ "Apparel and leather and allied products",
      .data$ind %in% c(1870L, 1880L, 1890L) ~ "Paper products",
      .data$ind %in% c(1990L) ~ "Printing and related support activities",
      .data$ind %in% c(2070L, 2090L) ~ "Petroleum and coal products",
      .data$ind %in% c(2170L, 2180L, 2190L, 2270L, 2280L, 2290L) ~ "Chemical products",
      .data$ind %in% c(2370L, 2380L, 2390L) ~ "Plastics and rubber products",
      .data$ind %in% c(2470L, 2480L, 2490L, 2570L, 2590L) ~ "Nonmetallic mineral products",
      .data$ind %in% c(2670L, 2680L, 2690L, 2770L) ~ "Primary metals",
      .data$ind %in% c(2780L, 2790L, 2870L, 2880L, 2890L, 2970L, 2980L, 2990L) ~ "Fabricated metal products",
      .data$ind %in% c(3070L, 3080L, 3090L, 3095L, 3170L, 3180L, 3190L, 3290L, 3291L) ~ "Machinery",
      .data$ind %in% c(3360L, 3365L, 3370L, 3380L, 3390L) ~ "Computer and electronic products",
      .data$ind %in% c(3470L, 3490L) ~ "Electrical equipment, appliances, and components",
      .data$ind %in% c(3570L) ~ "Motor vehicles, bodies and trailers, and parts",
      .data$ind %in% c(3580L, 3590L, 3670L, 3680L, 3690L) ~ "Other transportation equipment",
      .data$ind %in% c(3770L, 3780L, 3790L, 3870L, 3875L) ~ "Wood products",
      .data$ind %in% c(3890L, 3895L) ~ "Furniture and related products",
      .data$ind %in% c(3960L, 3970L, 3980L, 3990L) ~ "Miscellaneous manufacturing",
      dplyr::between(.data$ind, 4070L, 4590L) ~ "Wholesale trade",
      .data$ind %in% c(4670L, 4680L, 4681L, 4690L, 4691L) ~ "Motor vehicle and parts dealers",
      .data$ind %in% c(4970L, 4971L, 4972L, 4973L, 4980L, 4981L, 4990L, 4991L) ~ "Food and beverage stores",
      .data$ind %in% c(5380L, 5381L, 5382L, 5390L, 5391L, 5392L) ~ "General merchandise stores",
      .data$ind %in% c(4770L, 4771L, 4780L, 4790L, 4795L, 4796L, 4870L, 4871L, 4880L, 4881L, 4890L, 4891L, 5070L, 5071L, 5080L, 5081L, 5090L, 5170L, 5171L, 5180L, 5181L, 5190L, 5191L, 5270L, 5275L, 5276L, 5280L, 5281L, 5290L, 5295L, 5296L, 5370L, 5371L, 5470L, 5471L, 5480L, 5481L, 5490L, 5491L, 5570L, 5571L, 5580L, 5581L, 5590L, 5591L, 5592L, 5593L, 5670L, 5680L, 5690L, 5790L, 5791L) ~ "Other retail",
      .data$ind %in% c(6070L) ~ "Air transportation",
      .data$ind %in% c(6080L) ~ "Rail transportation",
      .data$ind %in% c(6090L) ~ "Water transportation",
      .data$ind %in% c(6170L) ~ "Truck transportation",
      .data$ind %in% c(6180L, 6190L) ~ "Transit and ground passenger transportation",
      .data$ind %in% c(6270L) ~ "Pipeline transportation",
      .data$ind %in% c(6280L, 6290L, 6370L, 6380L) ~ "Other transportation and support activities",
      .data$ind %in% c(6390L) ~ "Warehousing and storage",
      .data$ind %in% c(6470L, 6471L, 6480L, 6481L, 6490L) ~ "Publishing industries, except internet (includes software)",
      .data$ind %in% c(6570L, 6590L) ~ "Motion picture and sound recording industries",
      .data$ind %in% c(6670L, 6671L, 6680L, 6690L, 6692L) ~ "Broadcasting and telecommunications",
      .data$ind %in% c(6672L, 6675L, 6695L, 6770L, 6780L, 6781L, 6790L) ~ "Data processing, internet publishing, and other information services",
      .data$ind %in% c(6870L, 6871L, 6880L, 6881L, 6890L) ~ "Federal Reserve banks, credit intermediation, and related activities",
      .data$ind %in% c(6970L) ~ "Finance and insurance",
      .data$ind %in% c(6990L, 6991L, 6992L) ~ "Insurance carriers and related activities",
      .data$ind %in% c(7070L, 7071L, 7072L) ~ "Real estate",
      .data$ind %in% c(7080L, 7170L, 7180L, 7181L, 7190L) ~ "Rental and leasing services and lessors of intangible assets",
      .data$ind %in% c(7270L) ~ "Legal services",
      .data$ind %in% c(7380L) ~ "Computer systems design and related services",
      .data$ind %in% c(7280L, 7290L, 7370L, 7390L, 7460L, 7470L, 7480L, 7490L) ~ "Miscellaneous professional, scientific, and technical services",
      .data$ind %in% c(7570L) ~ "Management of companies and enterprises",
      .data$ind %in% c(7580L, 7590L, 7670L, 7680L, 7690L, 7770L, 7780L) ~ "Administrative and support services",
      .data$ind %in% c(7790L) ~ "Waste management and remediation services",
      .data$ind %in% c(7860L, 7870L, 7880L, 7890L) ~ "Educational services",
      .data$ind %in% c(7970L, 7980L, 7990L, 8070L, 8080L, 8090L, 8170L, 8180L) ~ "Ambulatory health care services",
      .data$ind %in% c(8190L, 8191L, 8192L) ~ "Hospitals",
      .data$ind %in% c(8270L, 8290L) ~ "Nursing and residential care facilities",
      .data$ind %in% c(8370L, 8380L, 8390L, 8470L) ~ "Social assistance",
      .data$ind %in% c(8560L, 8561L, 8562L, 8563L, 8564L, 8570L) ~ "Performing arts, spectator sports, museums, and related activities",
      .data$ind %in% c(8580L, 8590L) ~ "Amusements, gambling, and recreation industries",
      .data$ind %in% c(8660L, 8670L) ~ "Accommodation",
      .data$ind %in% c(8680L, 8690L) ~ "Food services and drinking places",
      .data$ind %in% c(8770L, 8780L, 8790L, 8870L, 8880L, 8890L, 8891L, 8970L, 8980L, 8990L, 9070L, 9080L, 9090L, 9160L, 9170L, 9180L, 9190L, 9290L) ~ "Other services, except government",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    mapping_status = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$ind %in% c(170L, 180L, 190L, 270L, 280L, 290L) ~ "outside_scope_agriculture",
      .data$ind %in% c(9370L, 9380L, 9390L, 9470L, 9480L, 9490L, 9570L, 9590L) ~ "outside_scope_government",
      .data$ind %in% c(6970L) ~ "mapped_to_finance_and_insurance_parent_funds_split_unavailable",
      .data$ind %in% c(7070L, 7071L, 7072L) ~ "mapped_to_real_estate_parent_housing_split_unavailable",
      !is.na(.data$terminal_qilp_industry) ~ "mapped",
      TRUE ~ "manual_review"
    ),
    mapping_note = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$mapping_status == "outside_scope_agriculture" ~
        "Agriculture is outside the nonfarm payroll scope used for the CES side of the replication.",
      .data$mapping_status == "outside_scope_government" ~
        "Government is excluded from the paper's private-sector productivity construction.",
      .data$mapping_status == "mapped_to_finance_and_insurance_parent_funds_split_unavailable" ~
        "IPUMS code 6970 combines securities activities with funds and trusts, so the CPS detail cannot isolate the QILP funds row.",
      .data$mapping_status == "mapped_to_real_estate_parent_housing_split_unavailable" ~
        "CPS real-estate detail does not isolate owner-occupied housing from other real-estate activity.",
      .data$mapping_status == "mapped" ~
        "Detailed CPS code mapped directly to a QILP industry bucket.",
      TRUE ~ "Code not yet assigned to a QILP industry."
    )
  )

add_meta_sheet(cps_qilp_crosswalk_stage, "cps_qilp_crosswalk_stage")
openxlsx::saveWorkbook(metadata_wb, paths$metadata_wb, overwrite = TRUE)

qilp_hierarchy <- build_qilp_hierarchy(qilp_industries)

terminal_qilp_industries <- cps_qilp_crosswalk_stage |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$mapping_status %in% c("mapped", "mapped_to_finance_and_insurance_parent_funds_split_unavailable", "mapped_to_real_estate_parent_housing_split_unavailable")) |>
  dplyr::distinct(.data$terminal_qilp_industry) |>
  dplyr::pull("terminal_qilp_industry")

qilp_ancestor_map <- build_ancestor_map(qilp_hierarchy, terminal_qilp_industries)

cps_qilp_monthly_nonpayroll_panel <- cps_monthly_worker_counts |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    cps_qilp_crosswalk_stage |>
      dplyr::select("year", "ind", "cps_industry_title", "terminal_qilp_industry", "mapping_status"),
    by = c("year", "ind")
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    # Issue E1: use unincorporated (CLASSWKR=13) + unpaid family (CLASSWKR=29) only.
    # Including incorporated self-employed (CLASSWKR=14) overstates nonpayroll by
    # ~5.5M nationally and drives a 7-13% employment overcount in high-self-employment
    # industries. The published workbook's implied nonpayroll rates match 13+29 only.
    nonpayroll_employment = .data$self_employed_unincorporated + .data$unpaid_family_workers
  ) |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$mapping_status %in% c("mapped", "mapped_to_finance_and_insurance_parent_funds_split_unavailable", "mapped_to_real_estate_parent_housing_split_unavailable")) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$year, .data$month, .data$month_date, .data$year_month, .data$terminal_qilp_industry) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    self_employed_unincorporated = sum(.data$self_employed_unincorporated, na.rm = TRUE),
    self_employed_incorporated = sum(.data$self_employed_incorporated, na.rm = TRUE),
    unpaid_family_workers = sum(.data$unpaid_family_workers, na.rm = TRUE),
    nonpayroll_employment = sum(.data$nonpayroll_employment, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    qilp_ancestor_map,
    by = "terminal_qilp_industry",
    relationship = "many-to-many"
  ) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$qilp_industry, .data$year, .data$month, .data$month_date, .data$year_month) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    nonpayroll_employment = sum(.data$nonpayroll_employment, na.rm = TRUE),
    self_employed_unincorporated = sum(.data$self_employed_unincorporated, na.rm = TRUE),
    self_employed_incorporated = sum(.data$self_employed_incorporated, na.rm = TRUE),
    unpaid_family_workers = sum(.data$unpaid_family_workers, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    qilp_hierarchy |>
      dplyr::select("industry", "level", "gdp_line", "parent_industry"),
    by = c("qilp_industry" = "industry")
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    nonpayroll_status = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$qilp_industry == "Real estate" ~ "includes_housing_and_other_real_estate_combined",
      .data$qilp_industry == "Finance and insurance" ~ "includes_combined_securities_and_funds_component",
      TRUE ~ "constructed_from_mapped_cps_detail"
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    "qilp_industry",
    "level",
    "gdp_line",
    "parent_industry",
    "year",
    "month",
    "month_date",
    "year_month",
    "nonpayroll_employment",
    "self_employed_unincorporated",
    "self_employed_incorporated",
    "unpaid_family_workers",
    "nonpayroll_status"
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$qilp_industry, .data$month_date)

readr::write_csv(cps_qilp_monthly_nonpayroll_panel, paths$cps_qilp_monthly_nonpayroll_panel)

list(
  cps_lookup_summary = cps_industry_code_lookup |>
    dplyr::summarise(
      years = dplyr::n_distinct(.data$year),
      unique_codes = dplyr::n_distinct(.data$ind),
      rows = dplyr::n()
    ),
  cps_crosswalk_status = cps_qilp_crosswalk_stage |>
    dplyr::count(.data$mapping_status, name = "rows"),
  cps_nonpayroll_panel_summary = cps_qilp_monthly_nonpayroll_panel |>
    dplyr::summarise(
      industries = dplyr::n_distinct(.data$qilp_industry),
      min_month = min(.data$month_date),
      max_month = max(.data$month_date),
      rows = dplyr::n()
    )
)
```

## Step 10: Combine CES payroll inputs and CPS nonpayroll inputs

```{r step-10-build-total-labor-inputs}
if (!file.exists(paths$cps_qilp_monthly_nonpayroll_panel)) {
  stop("Step 10 requires the CPS nonpayroll panel from Step 9.")
}

cps_qilp_monthly_nonpayroll_panel <- readr::read_csv(
  paths$cps_qilp_monthly_nonpayroll_panel,
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

# October 2025 CPS data is missing due to the government shutdown (BLS did not
# conduct the survey). Interpolate as the average of September and November 2025.
cps_oct2025_interp <- cps_qilp_monthly_nonpayroll_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$year == 2025, .data$month %in% c(9L, 11L)) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$qilp_industry, .data$level, .data$gdp_line, .data$parent_industry) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    year                      = 2025L,
    month                     = 10L,
    month_date                = as.Date("2025-10-01"),
    year_month                = "2025-10",
    nonpayroll_employment     = mean(.data$nonpayroll_employment,     na.rm = TRUE),
    self_employed_unincorporated = mean(.data$self_employed_unincorporated, na.rm = TRUE),
    self_employed_incorporated   = mean(.data$self_employed_incorporated,   na.rm = TRUE),
    unpaid_family_workers        = mean(.data$unpaid_family_workers,        na.rm = TRUE),
    nonpayroll_status            = dplyr::first(.data$nonpayroll_status),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cps_qilp_monthly_nonpayroll_panel <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  cps_qilp_monthly_nonpayroll_panel,
  cps_oct2025_interp
) |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$qilp_industry, .data$year, .data$month)

qilp_monthly_total_labor_inputs_panel <- ces_qilp_monthly_payroll_panel |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    cps_qilp_monthly_nonpayroll_panel |>
      dplyr::select(
        "qilp_industry",
        "year",
        "month",
        "month_date",
        "nonpayroll_employment",
        "self_employed_unincorporated",
        "self_employed_incorporated",
        "unpaid_family_workers",
        "nonpayroll_status"
      ),
    by = c("qilp_industry", "year", "month", "month_date")
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    # CPS nonpayroll is in persons; CES payroll is in thousands of workers.
    # Divide nonpayroll by 1000 to put everything in thousands before combining.
    nonpayroll_employment = dplyr::coalesce(.data$nonpayroll_employment, 0) / 1000,
    self_employed_unincorporated = dplyr::coalesce(.data$self_employed_unincorporated, 0) / 1000,
    self_employed_incorporated = dplyr::coalesce(.data$self_employed_incorporated, 0) / 1000,
    unpaid_family_workers = dplyr::coalesce(.data$unpaid_family_workers, 0) / 1000,
    total_employment = .data$payroll_employment + .data$nonpayroll_employment,
    # payroll_weekly_hours is already in thousands×hours/week; nonpayroll uses same AWH.
    total_weekly_hours = dplyr::if_else(
      !is.na(.data$payroll_weekly_hours) & !is.na(.data$payroll_average_weekly_hours),
      .data$payroll_weekly_hours + .data$nonpayroll_employment * .data$payroll_average_weekly_hours,
      NA_real_
    ),
    total_inputs_status = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$qilp_industry == "Private Nonfarm Business" ~ "manual_review_private_nonfarm_business_not_built",
      .data$qilp_industry %in% c("Housing", "Other real estate") ~ "manual_review_housing_split_unavailable",
      .data$qilp_industry %in% c("Securities, commodity contracts, and investments", "Funds, trusts, and other financial vehicles") ~ "manual_review_funds_split_unavailable",
      .data$all_hours_components_available & is.na(.data$nonpayroll_status) ~ "constructed",
      .data$all_hours_components_available & !is.na(.data$nonpayroll_status) ~ .data$nonpayroll_status,
      !.data$all_hours_components_available ~ "ces_hours_incomplete",
      TRUE ~ "constructed"
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    "qilp_industry",
    "bea_industry",
    "year",
    "month",
    "month_date",
    "payroll_employment",
    "nonpayroll_employment",
    "total_employment",
    "payroll_average_weekly_hours",
    "total_weekly_hours",
    "self_employed_unincorporated",
    "self_employed_incorporated",
    "unpaid_family_workers",
    "payroll_weekly_hours",
    "employment_components",
    "hours_components",
    "all_hours_components_available",
    "nonpayroll_status",
    "total_inputs_status"
  )

readr::write_csv(qilp_monthly_total_labor_inputs_panel, paths$qilp_monthly_total_labor_inputs_panel)

list(
  total_inputs_summary = qilp_monthly_total_labor_inputs_panel |>
    dplyr::summarise(
      industries = dplyr::n_distinct(.data$qilp_industry),
      min_month = min(.data$month_date),
      max_month = max(.data$month_date),
      rows = dplyr::n()
    ),
  total_inputs_status = qilp_monthly_total_labor_inputs_panel |>
    dplyr::count(.data$total_inputs_status, name = "rows") |>
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(.data$rows))
)
```

## Step 11: Resolve Housing and Funds/Trusts industry splits

```{r step-11-split-method}
# Toggle here: "A" = zero hours for Housing, VA-share split for Securities/Funds
#              "B" = VA-share split for both pairs
HOURS_SPLIT_METHOD <- "A"
```

```{r step-11-build-quarterly-va-shares}
bea_quarterly_panel <- readr::read_csv(paths$bea_combined_panel, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Quarterly nominal VA for the six sub-industries needed for splitting
va_split_series <- bea_quarterly_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(
    .data$series_name == "current_dollar_value_added",
    .data$naics_code %in% c("531", "HS", "ORE", "52", "523", "525")
  ) |>
  dplyr::select("naics_code", "year", "quarter", "data_value") |>
  dplyr::mutate(quarter_num = as.integer(stringr::str_remove(.data$quarter, "Q")))

# Compute within-parent VA shares each quarter
re_shares <- va_split_series |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$naics_code %in% c("531", "HS", "ORE")) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = "naics_code", values_from = "data_value") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    share_hs  = .data$HS  / .data$`531`,
    share_ore = .data$ORE / .data$`531`
  ) |>
  dplyr::select("year", "quarter", "quarter_num", "share_hs", "share_ore")

fi_shares <- va_split_series |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$naics_code %in% c("52", "523", "525")) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = "naics_code", values_from = "data_value") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    share_523 = .data$`523` / .data$`52`,
    share_525 = .data$`525` / .data$`52`
  ) |>
  dplyr::select("year", "quarter", "quarter_num", "share_523", "share_525")
```

```{r step-11-apply-split}
# Assign each month to its quarter for joining
total_panel <- qilp_monthly_total_labor_inputs_panel |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    quarter_num = ceiling(.data$month / 3L),
    quarter     = paste0("Q", .data$quarter_num)
  )

# ── Real estate split ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
re_combined <- total_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$qilp_industry == "Real estate") |>
  dplyr::left_join(re_shares, by = c("year", "quarter", "quarter_num"))

if (HOURS_SPLIT_METHOD == "A") {
  # Housing employment: CES series CEU5553111001 (Lessors of residential buildings,
  # NAICS 53111). This matches the published workbook's ~375K Housing employment
  # exactly (2006Q2: our 376K vs published 375K). Housing employment is additive —
  # ORE retains the full 531 parent employment unchanged, not a split.
  # Hours remain NA (Method A: owner-occupied imputed rent involves no actual labor).
  # Nonpayroll is set to 0: CPS cannot separate Housing from ORE and the published
  # workbook Housing employment is fully explained by CES payroll alone.
  ces_lessors_res <- dplyr::bind_rows(
    pull_bls_series_batch("CEU5553111001", 2005L, 2015L),
    pull_bls_series_batch("CEU5553111001", 2016L, as.integer(format(Sys.Date(), "%Y")))
  ) |>
    dplyr::select(year = "year", month = "month", housing_payroll_emp = "value")

  re_housing <- re_combined |>
    dplyr::left_join(ces_lessors_res, by = c("year", "month")) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      qilp_industry         = "Housing",
      payroll_employment    = dplyr::coalesce(.data$housing_payroll_emp, 0),
      nonpayroll_employment = 0,
      total_employment      = .data$payroll_employment,
      # No AWH published for NAICS 53111; use Real estate parent AWH as proxy.
      payroll_weekly_hours  = .data$payroll_employment * .data$payroll_average_weekly_hours,
      total_weekly_hours    = .data$payroll_employment * .data$payroll_average_weekly_hours,
      split_method          = "ces53111_housing_parent_awh"
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(-"housing_payroll_emp")

  re_other <- re_combined |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      qilp_industry = "Other real estate",
      split_method  = "method_A_full_labor_to_ore"
    )
} else {
  # Method B: distribute hours by quarterly BEA nominal VA shares
  re_housing <- re_combined |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      qilp_industry         = "Housing",
      nonpayroll_employment = .data$nonpayroll_employment * .data$share_hs,
      total_employment      = .data$total_employment     * .data$share_hs,
      total_weekly_hours    = dplyr::if_else(
        !is.na(.data$total_weekly_hours), .data$total_weekly_hours * .data$share_hs, NA_real_
      ),
      split_method          = "method_B_va_share"
    )
  re_other <- re_combined |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      qilp_industry         = "Other real estate",
      nonpayroll_employment = .data$nonpayroll_employment * .data$share_ore,
      total_employment      = .data$total_employment     * .data$share_ore,
      total_weekly_hours    = dplyr::if_else(
        !is.na(.data$total_weekly_hours), .data$total_weekly_hours * .data$share_ore, NA_real_
      ),
      split_method          = "method_B_va_share"
    )
}

# ── Securities / Funds split (VA-share for both methods) ──────────────────────
# The combined hours are currently on Finance and insurance (nonpayroll) and on
# the individual Securities/Funds CES rows (payroll). Nonpayroll must be split;
# payroll is already separate.
fi_combined_nonpayroll <- total_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$qilp_industry == "Finance and insurance") |>
  dplyr::left_join(fi_shares, by = c("year", "quarter", "quarter_num"))

fi_securities_nonpayroll <- fi_combined_nonpayroll |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    qilp_industry         = "Securities, commodity contracts, and investments",
    nonpayroll_employment = .data$nonpayroll_employment * .data$share_523,
    # Payroll for Securities is already in its own CES row; zero it out here so
    # the two rows can be summed in quarterly aggregation without double-counting.
    payroll_employment    = 0,
    payroll_weekly_hours  = 0,
    total_employment      = .data$nonpayroll_employment * .data$share_523,
    total_weekly_hours    = dplyr::if_else(
      !is.na(.data$payroll_average_weekly_hours),
      .data$nonpayroll_employment * .data$share_523 * .data$payroll_average_weekly_hours,
      NA_real_
    ),
    split_method          = "va_share_523"
  )

fi_funds_nonpayroll <- fi_combined_nonpayroll |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    qilp_industry         = "Funds, trusts, and other financial vehicles",
    nonpayroll_employment = .data$nonpayroll_employment * .data$share_525,
    payroll_employment    = 0,
    payroll_weekly_hours  = 0,
    total_employment      = .data$nonpayroll_employment * .data$share_525,
    total_weekly_hours    = dplyr::if_else(
      !is.na(.data$payroll_average_weekly_hours),
      .data$nonpayroll_employment * .data$share_525 * .data$payroll_average_weekly_hours,
      NA_real_
    ),
    split_method          = "va_share_525"
  )

# ── Assemble the resolved split rows ─────────────────────────────────────────
split_cols <- c(
  "qilp_industry", "bea_industry", "year", "month", "month_date",
  "payroll_employment", "nonpayroll_employment", "total_employment",
  "payroll_average_weekly_hours", "total_weekly_hours",
  "self_employed_unincorporated", "self_employed_incorporated", "unpaid_family_workers",
  "payroll_weekly_hours", "employment_components", "hours_components",
  "all_hours_components_available", "nonpayroll_status", "total_inputs_status",
  "split_method"
)

make_split_row <- function(df, status_label) {
  if (!"split_method" %in% names(df)) df <- dplyr::mutate(df, split_method = status_label)
  df |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      nonpayroll_status   = status_label,
      total_inputs_status = dplyr::if_else(
        .data$all_hours_components_available, status_label, "ces_hours_incomplete"
      )
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(dplyr::any_of(split_cols))
}

qilp_monthly_total_labor_inputs_split <- total_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(!(.data$qilp_industry %in% c(
    "Housing", "Other real estate",
    "Finance and insurance", "Real estate"
  ))) |>
  dplyr::mutate(split_method = NA_character_) |>
  dplyr::select(dplyr::any_of(split_cols)) |>
  dplyr::bind_rows(
    make_split_row(re_housing,             paste0("housing_split_", HOURS_SPLIT_METHOD)),
    make_split_row(re_other,               paste0("ore_split_",     HOURS_SPLIT_METHOD)),
    # Nonpayroll portions only — payroll already in the Securities/Funds CES rows above
    make_split_row(fi_securities_nonpayroll, "securities_split_va_share"),
    make_split_row(fi_funds_nonpayroll,      "funds_split_va_share"),
    # Retain parent rows for aggregation convenience — hours unchanged
    make_split_row(
      total_panel |> dplyr::filter(.data$qilp_industry == "Real estate") |>
        dplyr::mutate(split_method = "parent_retained"),
      "real_estate_parent_retained"
    ),
    make_split_row(
      total_panel |> dplyr::filter(.data$qilp_industry == "Finance and insurance") |>
        dplyr::mutate(split_method = "parent_retained"),
      "finance_insurance_parent_retained"
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$qilp_industry, .data$month_date)

readr::write_csv(
  qilp_monthly_total_labor_inputs_split,
  here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bls", "qilp_monthly_total_labor_inputs_split.csv")
)

list(
  split_method_used = HOURS_SPLIT_METHOD,
  status_breakdown  = qilp_monthly_total_labor_inputs_split |>
    dplyr::filter(
      .data$qilp_industry %in% c(
        "Housing", "Other real estate",
        "Securities, commodity contracts, and investments",
        "Funds, trusts, and other financial vehicles"
      )
    ) |>
    dplyr::count(.data$qilp_industry, .data$split_method, name = "months") |>
    dplyr::arrange(.data$qilp_industry),
  sample_housing_2023 = qilp_monthly_total_labor_inputs_split |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$qilp_industry %in% c("Housing", "Other real estate"),
                  .data$year == 2023, .data$month == 6) |>
    dplyr::select("qilp_industry", "total_employment",
                  "total_weekly_hours", "split_method")
)
```

## Step 12: Seasonal adjustment of monthly total hours

```{r step-12-sa-setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
if (!requireNamespace("seasonal",   quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("seasonal")
if (!requireNamespace("x13binary",  quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("x13binary")
library(seasonal)
library(x13binary)
Sys.setenv(X13_PATH = x13binary::x13path())
```

```{r step-12-seasonal-adjustment}
# Apply X-13ARIMA-SEATS to monthly total_weekly_hours for each industry.
# Industries with all-NA hours are passed through as-is.

monthly_split <- readr::read_csv(paths$qilp_monthly_total_labor_inputs_split,
                                 show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$qilp_industry, .data$year, .data$month)

safe_seas <- function(x, start_year, start_month) {
  n <- length(x)
  if (all(is.na(x))) return(rep(NA_real_, n))
  last_valid <- max(which(!is.na(x)))
  x_trim <- x[seq_len(last_valid)]
  ts_trim <- ts(x_trim, start = c(start_year, start_month), frequency = 12)
  fit <- tryCatch(seasonal::seas(ts_trim, x11 = ""), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(fit)) return(x)
  sa_trim <- as.numeric(seasonal::final(fit))
  if (length(sa_trim) == 0L) return(x)  # X-13 decomposition collapsed (e.g. extreme outliers)
  if (length(sa_trim) < last_valid) {
    sa_trim <- c(rep(NA_real_, last_valid - length(sa_trim)), sa_trim)
  }
  c(sa_trim, rep(NA_real_, n - last_valid))
}

hours_sa <- monthly_split |>
  # Some industries (Securities, Funds) have two rows per month: CES direct plus
  # Finance-parent nonpayroll split. Sum to one row per industry-month before SA.
  dplyr::group_by(.data$qilp_industry, .data$year, .data$month) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    total_weekly_hours = sum(.data$total_weekly_hours, na.rm = FALSE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$qilp_industry, .data$year, .data$month) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$qilp_industry) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    total_weekly_hours_sa = safe_seas(
      .data$total_weekly_hours,
      start_year  = min(.data$year),
      start_month = .data$month[which.min(as.Date(paste0(.data$year, "-",
                                                          sprintf("%02d", .data$month), "-01")))]
    ),
    # Plausibility check: if any month's SA/raw ratio falls outside [0.1, 10],
    # the X-13 decomposition is unreliable for this series (extreme outliers or
    # end-of-sample instability). Fall back to unadjusted for the whole series.
    sa_implausible = any(
      !is.na(.data$total_weekly_hours_sa) &
      !is.na(.data$total_weekly_hours) &
      .data$total_weekly_hours > 0 &
      (.data$total_weekly_hours_sa / .data$total_weekly_hours < 0.1 |
       .data$total_weekly_hours_sa / .data$total_weekly_hours > 10),
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    total_weekly_hours_sa = dplyr::if_else(
      .data$sa_implausible,
      .data$total_weekly_hours,
      .data$total_weekly_hours_sa
    ),
    sa_method = dplyr::case_when(
      all(is.na(.data$total_weekly_hours)) ~ "all_na_passthrough",
      .data$sa_implausible                 ~ "x13_fallback_implausible",
      all(.data$total_weekly_hours_sa == .data$total_weekly_hours, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "x13_fallback_unadjusted",
      TRUE                                 ~ "x13_adjusted"
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(-sa_implausible) |>
  dplyr::ungroup()

readr::write_csv(hours_sa, paths$qilp_monthly_hours_sa)

list(
  n_industries  = dplyr::n_distinct(hours_sa$qilp_industry),
  sa_method_counts = dplyr::count(hours_sa, .data$sa_method, name = "rows"),
  sample_construction = hours_sa |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$qilp_industry == "Construction",
                  .data$year == 2023, .data$month %in% c(1L, 4L, 7L, 10L)) |>
    dplyr::select("year", "month", "total_weekly_hours",
                  "total_weekly_hours_sa")
)
```

## Step 13: Aggregate to quarters; enforce 2018Q1 exclusion

```{r step-13-quarterly-aggregation}
# Sum SA monthly hours within each quarter; then drop 2018Q1 before any
# log-change computations (BEA definition change seam).

quarterly_hours <- readr::read_csv(paths$qilp_monthly_hours_sa,
                                   show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    quarter_num = ceiling(.data$month / 3L),
    quarter     = paste0("Q", .data$quarter_num),
    year_quarter = paste0(.data$year, .data$quarter)
  ) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$qilp_industry, .data$year, .data$quarter_num,
                  .data$quarter, .data$year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    total_weekly_hours_sa_q    = sum(.data$total_weekly_hours_sa,    na.rm = FALSE),
    total_weekly_hours_raw_q   = sum(.data$total_weekly_hours,       na.rm = FALSE),
    months_available           = dplyr::n(),
    months_with_sa             = sum(!is.na(.data$total_weekly_hours_sa)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  # Treat quarter as NA if any constituent month is missing SA hours
  dplyr::mutate(
    total_weekly_hours_sa_q  = dplyr::if_else(
      .data$months_with_sa == .data$months_available,
      .data$total_weekly_hours_sa_q,
      NA_real_
    )
  ) |>
  # Enforce 2018Q1 seam exclusion: retain the row but flag it so downstream
  # log-change code can skip across this seam.
  dplyr::mutate(
    exclude_from_log_change = (.data$year_quarter == "2018Q1")
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$qilp_industry, .data$year, .data$quarter_num)

# Inject Agriculture and Forestry quarterly hours from the published QILP workbook.
# These industries are outside CES nonfarm payroll scope so CES provides no hours.
# The published workbook's Total Hours sheet carries identical values for
# "Agriculture, forestry, fishing, and hunting" (level 1) and
# "Forestry, fishing, and related activities" (level 2); Farms is all-NA.
# We read the published levels directly and compute dln_H from them in Step 14.
# The Excel header row encodes quarter end-dates as Excel date serials; values
# above 1e8 are Unix timestamps (origin 1970-01-01), others are Excel serials
# (origin 1899-12-30), consistent with the Step 18 workbook parser.
pub_wb_path <- here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "qilp",
                          "qilp_20260630.xlsx")
pub_total_hrs <- readxl::read_excel(pub_wb_path, sheet = "Total Hours",
                                    col_names = FALSE, .name_repair = "minimal")
pub_header_raw <- suppressWarnings(
  as.numeric(unlist(pub_total_hrs[1L, 4:ncol(pub_total_hrs)]))
)
pub_quarters <- sapply(pub_header_raw, function(x) {
  if (is.na(x)) return(NA_character_)
  d <- if (x > 1e8) as.Date(x / 86400, origin = "1970-01-01") else
                    as.Date(x,          origin = "1899-12-30")
  paste0(format(d, "%Y"), "Q", ceiling(as.integer(format(d, "%m")) / 3L))
})
pub_quarters <- pub_quarters[!is.na(pub_quarters)]

parse_pub_hrs_row <- function(pattern) {
  row_idx <- which(sapply(seq_len(nrow(pub_total_hrs)), function(i)
    grepl(pattern, as.character(pub_total_hrs[[1L]][i]), fixed = FALSE)))[1L]
  vals <- suppressWarnings(
    as.numeric(unlist(pub_total_hrs[row_idx, 4:(3L + length(pub_quarters))]))
  )
  data.frame(year_quarter = pub_quarters, pub_total_hrs = vals,
             stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}

forestry_pub <- parse_pub_hrs_row("Forestry, fishing")
agri_pub     <- parse_pub_hrs_row("Agriculture, forestry")

make_agri_rows <- function(industry_name, hrs_df) {
  hrs_df |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$pub_total_hrs)) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      qilp_industry             = industry_name,
      year                      = as.integer(substr(.data$year_quarter, 1L, 4L)),
      quarter_num               = as.integer(substr(.data$year_quarter, 6L, 6L)),
      quarter                   = paste0("Q", .data$quarter_num),
      # Published Total Hours are in person-hours per quarter (annual level / 52
      # times 13 weeks).  Convert to average weekly hours × employment aggregate
      # by leaving as-is — the existing pipeline treats total_weekly_hours_sa_q
      # as the sum of (employment × awh) across the quarter's three months.
      # The published figure is already in that unit.
      total_weekly_hours_sa_q   = .data$pub_total_hrs,
      total_weekly_hours_raw_q  = .data$pub_total_hrs,
      months_available          = 3L,
      months_with_sa            = 3L,
      exclude_from_log_change   = (.data$year_quarter == "2018Q1")
    ) |>
    dplyr::select("qilp_industry", "year", "quarter_num", "quarter",
                  "year_quarter", "total_weekly_hours_sa_q",
                  "total_weekly_hours_raw_q", "months_available",
                  "months_with_sa", "exclude_from_log_change")
}

agri_rows     <- make_agri_rows("Agriculture, forestry, fishing, and hunting", agri_pub)
forestry_rows <- make_agri_rows("Forestry, fishing, and related activities",   forestry_pub)

quarterly_hours <- dplyr::bind_rows(quarterly_hours, agri_rows, forestry_rows) |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$qilp_industry, .data$year, .data$quarter_num)

readr::write_csv(quarterly_hours, paths$qilp_quarterly_hours)

list(
  n_industries   = dplyr::n_distinct(quarterly_hours$qilp_industry),
  n_quarters     = dplyr::n_distinct(quarterly_hours$year_quarter),
  quarter_range  = range(quarterly_hours$year_quarter),
  seam_rows      = dplyr::filter(quarterly_hours, .data$exclude_from_log_change) |>
                     dplyr::count(.data$year_quarter),
  completeness   = quarterly_hours |>
                     dplyr::group_by(.data$qilp_industry) |>
                     dplyr::summarise(
                       pct_sa_complete = mean(!is.na(.data$total_weekly_hours_sa_q)) * 100,
                       .groups = "drop"
                     ) |>
                     dplyr::arrange(.data$pct_sa_complete)
)
```

## Step 14: Quarterly industry-level ALP growth

```{r step-14-alp-growth}
# Δln V_it  = Δln(real_VA_it)  from BEA TVA106-Q (millions of 2017 chain dollars)
# Δln H_it  = Δln(total_weekly_hours_sa_q)
# Δln ALP_it = Δln V_it − Δln H_it
#
# BEA chain-type Fisher indexes do not satisfy dln(T1) − dln(T8) = dln(real VA)
# at the industry/quarterly level; TVA106-Q levels must be used directly.
# Nominal VA for Törnqvist weights comes from TVA105-Q (same file, millions $).
#
# Both computations skip any log change whose denominator quarter is 2018Q1
# (BEA definition-change seam).

va_wb <- here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bea", "bea_value_added_all_tables_20260625.xlsx")

parse_valueadded_sheet <- function(sheet) {
  raw     <- readxl::read_excel(va_wb, sheet = sheet, col_names = FALSE)
  header  <- as.character(unlist(raw[8, ]))
  periods <- header[4:length(header)]
  periods <- periods[!is.na(periods)]
  dr <- raw[9:nrow(raw), ]
  data.frame(
    line     = as.character(unlist(dr[, 1])),
    industry = as.character(unlist(dr[, 2]))
  ) |>
    cbind(as.data.frame(apply(dr[, 4:(3 + length(periods))], 2, as.numeric))) |>
    setNames(c("line", "industry", periods)) |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$line), !is.na(.data$industry)) |>
    tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -(1:2), names_to = "year_quarter", values_to = "value")
}

t106 <- parse_valueadded_sheet("TVA106-Q")  # real VA, millions 2017$
t105 <- parse_valueadded_sheet("TVA105-Q")  # nominal VA, millions $

# Map qilp_industry names to TVA industry names via bea_industry from split panel.
# Prefer bea_industry == qilp_industry where available (Finance/Funds/Securities).
sp_names <- readr::read_csv(paths$qilp_monthly_total_labor_inputs_split,
                             show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  dplyr::distinct(.data$qilp_industry, .data$bea_industry)

tva_industries <- unique(t106$industry)

qilp_to_tva <- sp_names |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    tva_industry = dplyr::if_else(
      .data$bea_industry %in% tva_industries,
      .data$bea_industry,
      dplyr::if_else(.data$qilp_industry %in% tva_industries, .data$qilp_industry, NA_character_)
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$qilp_industry, !is.na(.data$tva_industry)) |>
  dplyr::distinct(.data$qilp_industry, .keep_all = TRUE)

# bea_industry for Housing and Other real estate is "Real estate" (the parent),
# so the generic mapping above assigns them both the parent TVA106-Q series.
# BEA publishes separate rows for all three; override to use them directly.
# The published workbook's "Real estate" (level 3) uses the TVA106-Q "Other real
# estate" series rather than the 531 parent, because Housing is separately
# identified as the level-4 complement. The parent row ("Real estate" line 61)
# is not in the Törnqvist leaf set, so this mapping only affects the ALP panel
# for validation purposes.
re_tva_override <- tibble::tribble(
  ~qilp_industry,      ~tva_industry,
  "Housing",           "Housing",
  "Other real estate", "Other real estate",
  "Real estate",       "Other real estate"
)
qilp_to_tva <- qilp_to_tva |>
  dplyr::rows_update(re_tva_override, by = "qilp_industry", unmatched = "ignore")

# Agriculture and Forestry are not in sp_names (outside CES scope) so they have
# no entry in qilp_to_tva. Add them explicitly using the TVA106-Q industry names,
# which match the QILP industry names exactly.
agri_tva_rows <- tibble::tribble(
  ~qilp_industry,                                    ~bea_industry,                                     ~tva_industry,
  "Agriculture, forestry, fishing, and hunting",     "Agriculture, forestry, fishing, and hunting",     "Agriculture, forestry, fishing, and hunting",
  "Forestry, fishing, and related activities",       "Forestry, fishing, and related activities",       "Forestry, fishing, and related activities"
)
qilp_to_tva <- dplyr::bind_rows(qilp_to_tva, agri_tva_rows)

# Compute dln_V from TVA106-Q levels
dln_V_tva <- t106 |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$value > 0) |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$industry, .data$year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$industry) |>
  dplyr::mutate(dln_V = log(.data$value / dplyr::lag(.data$value))) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::select(tva_industry = "industry", "year_quarter",
                "dln_V", real_va = "value")

# Nominal VA from TVA105-Q
nom_va_tva <- t105 |>
  dplyr::select(tva_industry = "industry", "year_quarter",
                current_dollar_value_added = "value")

# Attach BEA series to quarterly hours
qh <- readr::read_csv(paths$qilp_quarterly_hours, show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  dplyr::left_join(qilp_to_tva |> dplyr::select("qilp_industry", "tva_industry"),
                   by = "qilp_industry") |>
  dplyr::left_join(dln_V_tva,   by = c("tva_industry", "year_quarter")) |>
  dplyr::left_join(nom_va_tva,  by = c("tva_industry", "year_quarter"))

# Compute log-changes within each industry, respecting the 2018Q1 seam
alp_panel <- qh |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$qilp_industry, .data$year, .data$quarter_num) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$qilp_industry) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    prev_is_seam = dplyr::lag(.data$exclude_from_log_change, default = FALSE),
    dln_V   = dplyr::if_else(.data$prev_is_seam | .data$exclude_from_log_change,
                             NA_real_, .data$dln_V),
    dln_H   = dplyr::if_else(
      .data$prev_is_seam | .data$exclude_from_log_change,
      NA_real_,
      log(.data$total_weekly_hours_sa_q) -
        log(dplyr::lag(.data$total_weekly_hours_sa_q))
    ),
    dln_ALP = dplyr::if_else(
      is.na(.data$dln_V) | is.na(.data$dln_H),
      NA_real_,
      .data$dln_V - .data$dln_H
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::select("qilp_industry", "year", "quarter_num",
                "quarter", "year_quarter", "exclude_from_log_change",
                "current_dollar_value_added",
                "total_weekly_hours_sa_q",
                "dln_V", "dln_H", "dln_ALP")

readr::write_csv(alp_panel, here::here("replication", "data", "processed", "qilp_alp_panel.csv"))

list(
  n_industries = dplyr::n_distinct(alp_panel$qilp_industry),
  n_quarters   = dplyr::n_distinct(alp_panel$year_quarter),
  na_counts    = alp_panel |>
    dplyr::summarise(
      na_dln_V   = sum(is.na(.data$dln_V)),
      na_dln_H   = sum(is.na(.data$dln_H)),
      na_dln_ALP = sum(is.na(.data$dln_ALP))
    ),
  seam_nas     = alp_panel |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$year %in% c(2017L, 2018L, 2019L)) |>
    dplyr::group_by(.data$year_quarter) |>
    dplyr::summarise(n_na_alp = sum(is.na(.data$dln_ALP)), .groups = "drop"),
  construction_sample = alp_panel |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$qilp_industry == "Construction",
                  .data$year %in% 2019:2021) |>
    dplyr::select("year_quarter", "dln_V", "dln_H", "dln_ALP")
)
```

## Step 15: Törnqvist aggregate measures

```{r step-15-tornqvist}
# Equations 1–3 from the paper:
#   Δln V_t  = Σ_i s̄^V_{it}  · Δln V_it
#   Δln H_t  = Σ_i s̄^H_{it}  · Δln H_it
#   Δln ALP_t = Δln V_t − Δln H_t
#
# s̄^V_{it} = ½ (s^V_{i,t-1} + s^V_{i,t})  [average nominal VA share]
# s̄^H_{it} = ½ (s^H_{i,t-1} + s^H_{i,t})  [average total hours share]
#
# Aggregation is over nonfarm private LEAF industries: the 65 industries from
# the published workbook that have no children in the QILP dataset (levels 3
# and 4, plus level-1/2 industries like Construction and Utilities whose
# sub-sectors are not separately identified). Parent aggregates (e.g.,
# Manufacturing, Finance and insurance) are excluded to prevent double-counting.
# Housing is included as a leaf industry; its hours are sourced from CEU5553111001.

alp_panel <- readr::read_csv(paths$qilp_alp_panel, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Leaf industries: no children in the QILP roster.
# Determined from the published workbook's industry hierarchy (level 3/4 are
# always leaves; level 1/2 industries included only when they have no children).
leaf_industries <- c(
  # Level 2 mining sub-sectors
  "Oil and gas extraction", "Mining, except oil and gas",
  "Support activities for mining",
  # Level 1 single-row sectors
  "Utilities", "Construction", "Wholesale trade",
  # Level 2 durable manufacturing
  "Wood products", "Nonmetallic mineral products", "Primary metals",
  "Fabricated metal products", "Machinery", "Computer and electronic products",
  "Electrical equipment, appliances, and components",
  "Motor vehicles, bodies and trailers, and parts",
  "Other transportation equipment", "Furniture and related products",
  "Miscellaneous manufacturing",
  # Level 2 nondurable manufacturing
  "Food and beverage and tobacco products",
  "Textile mills and textile product mills",
  "Apparel and leather and allied products", "Paper products",
  "Printing and related support activities", "Petroleum and coal products",
  "Chemical products", "Plastics and rubber products",
  # Level 2 retail sub-sectors
  "Motor vehicle and parts dealers", "Food and beverage stores",
  "General merchandise stores", "Other retail",
  # Level 2 transportation sub-sectors
  "Air transportation", "Rail transportation", "Water transportation",
  "Truck transportation", "Transit and ground passenger transportation",
  "Pipeline transportation", "Other transportation and support activities",
  "Warehousing and storage",
  # Level 2 information sub-sectors
  "Publishing industries, except internet (includes software)",
  "Motion picture and sound recording industries",
  "Broadcasting and telecommunications",
  "Data processing, internet publishing, and other information services",
  # Level 3 finance sub-sectors
  "Federal Reserve banks, credit intermediation, and related activities",
  "Securities, commodity contracts, and investments",
  "Insurance carriers and related activities",
  "Funds, trusts, and other financial vehicles",
  # Level 4 real estate sub-sectors
  "Housing", "Other real estate",
  # Level 3 rental
  "Rental and leasing services and lessors of intangible assets",
  # Level 3 professional sub-sectors
  "Legal services", "Computer systems design and related services",
  "Miscellaneous professional, scientific, and technical services",
  # Level 2 management / admin
  "Management of companies and enterprises",
  "Administrative and support services",
  "Waste management and remediation services",
  # Level 2 educational services
  "Educational services",
  # Level 3 health care sub-sectors
  "Ambulatory health care services", "Hospitals",
  "Nursing and residential care facilities", "Social assistance",
  # Level 3 arts/entertainment sub-sectors
  "Performing arts, spectator sports, museums, and related activities",
  "Amusements, gambling, and recreation industries",
  # Level 3 accommodation/food
  "Accommodation", "Food services and drinking places"
)

# ── Nominal VA shares (s^V_it) ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# Restricted to leaf industries.
va_shares <- alp_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$current_dollar_value_added),
                .data$qilp_industry %in% leaf_industries) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    total_nominal_va = sum(.data$current_dollar_value_added, na.rm = TRUE),
    s_V = .data$current_dollar_value_added / .data$total_nominal_va
  ) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::select("qilp_industry", "year_quarter", "s_V")

# ── Total hours shares (s^H_it) ────────────────────────────────────────────
# Restricted to leaf industries.
hours_shares <- alp_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$total_weekly_hours_sa_q),
                .data$qilp_industry %in% leaf_industries) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    total_hours = sum(.data$total_weekly_hours_sa_q, na.rm = TRUE),
    s_H = .data$total_weekly_hours_sa_q / .data$total_hours
  ) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::select("qilp_industry", "year_quarter", "s_H")

# ── Törnqvist weights: average adjacent-quarter shares ─────────────────────
# Restricted to leaf industries.
tornqvist_weights <- alp_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$qilp_industry %in% leaf_industries) |>
  dplyr::select("qilp_industry", "year", "quarter_num",
                "quarter", "year_quarter", "exclude_from_log_change",
                "dln_V", "dln_H", "dln_ALP") |>
  dplyr::left_join(va_shares,    by = c("qilp_industry", "year_quarter")) |>
  dplyr::left_join(hours_shares, by = c("qilp_industry", "year_quarter")) |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$qilp_industry, .data$year, .data$quarter_num) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$qilp_industry) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    s_V_bar = 0.5 * (.data$s_V + dplyr::lag(.data$s_V)),
    s_H_bar = 0.5 * (.data$s_H + dplyr::lag(.data$s_H))
  ) |>
  dplyr::ungroup()

# ── Aggregate Törnqvist indices per quarter ────────────────────────────────
# Only include a quarter in the aggregate if both dln_V and dln_H are
# available for that industry (non-NA), and the quarter is not the seam.
aggregate_alp <- tornqvist_weights |>
  dplyr::filter(
    !.data$exclude_from_log_change,
    !is.na(.data$dln_V), !is.na(.data$dln_H),
    !is.na(.data$s_V_bar), !is.na(.data$s_H_bar)
  ) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$year, .data$quarter_num, .data$quarter, .data$year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    dln_V_agg   = sum(.data$s_V_bar * .data$dln_V),
    dln_H_agg   = sum(.data$s_H_bar * .data$dln_H),
    n_industries = dplyr::n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(dln_ALP_agg = .data$dln_V_agg - .data$dln_H_agg) |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$year, .data$quarter_num)

readr::write_csv(aggregate_alp,
                 here::here("replication", "data", "processed", "qilp_aggregate_alp.csv"))

list(
  n_quarters        = nrow(aggregate_alp),
  quarter_range     = range(aggregate_alp$year_quarter),
  industries_per_q  = summary(aggregate_alp$n_industries),
  recent_alp        = aggregate_alp |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$year >= 2022) |>
    dplyr::select("year_quarter", "dln_V_agg", "dln_H_agg", "dln_ALP_agg"),
  pandemic_quarters = aggregate_alp |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$year %in% 2020:2021) |>
    dplyr::select("year_quarter", "dln_V_agg", "dln_H_agg", "dln_ALP_agg")
)
```

## Step 15b: Compare aggregate ALP growth to BLS Nonfarm Business series

The BLS Productivity and Costs release publishes quarterly nonfarm business sector
labor productivity growth (output per hour). Our aggregate is nonfarm private sector
(excludes general government, which is already outside the QILP universe).
The BLS nonfarm business series also excludes nonprofit institutions from households
and farms, so coverage is similar but not identical. The comparison tests whether our
Törnqvist approximation tracks the BLS headline series.

```{r step-15b-bls-comparison}
# PRS85006092: BLS nonfarm business output per hour, SA, annualized percent change.
# Downloaded from FRED as replication/data/raw/bls/PRS85006092.xlsx (2026-07-01).
# FRED layout: README sheet + Quarterly sheet; data rows start after 8-line header,
# columns are date (first day of quarter) and value (annualized percent change).
bls_nfb_alp <- readxl::read_excel(
  here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bls", "PRS85006092.xlsx"),
  sheet = "Quarterly",
  skip  = 8,
  col_names = c("date", "value")
) |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$date), !is.na(.data$value)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    date         = as.Date(.data$date),
    year         = as.integer(format(.data$date, "%Y")),
    quarter_num  = ceiling(as.integer(format(.data$date, "%m")) / 3L),
    year_quarter = paste0(.data$year, "Q", .data$quarter_num),
    dln_ALP_bls  = .data$value / 400
  ) |>
  dplyr::select("year_quarter", "dln_ALP_bls")

alp_vs_bls <- aggregate_alp |>
  dplyr::select("year_quarter", "dln_ALP_agg") |>
  dplyr::inner_join(bls_nfb_alp, by = "year_quarter") |>
  dplyr::mutate(diff = .data$dln_ALP_agg - .data$dln_ALP_bls)

alp_vs_bls_stats <- alp_vs_bls |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$diff)) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_quarters  = dplyr::n(),
    correlation = round(cor(.data$dln_ALP_agg, .data$dln_ALP_bls, use = "complete.obs"), 3),
    mae_annpct  = round(mean(abs(.data$diff)) * 400, 4),
    rmse_annpct = round(sqrt(mean(.data$diff^2)) * 400, 4)
  )

list(
  comparison_stats = alp_vs_bls_stats,
  recent           = alp_vs_bls |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter >= "2022Q1") |>
    dplyr::mutate(dplyr::across(c("dln_ALP_agg", "dln_ALP_bls", "diff"), ~ round(.x * 400, 2)))
)
```

## Step 16: Industry contributions to aggregate ALP growth

```{r step-16-industry-contributions}
# Industry i contribution in quarter t = s̄^V_{it} · Δln ALP_it
# This decomposes aggregate ALP growth into the part attributable to each industry.
# Averaged over pre-pandemic (2006Q2–2019Q4), pandemic (2020Q1–2021Q4),
# and post-pandemic (2022Q1 onward) periods.

alp_panel    <- readr::read_csv(paths$qilp_alp_panel,    show_col_types = FALSE)
aggregate_alp <- readr::read_csv(paths$qilp_aggregate_alp, show_col_types = FALSE)

va_shares_contrib <- alp_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$current_dollar_value_added),
                .data$qilp_industry %in% leaf_industries) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::mutate(s_V = .data$current_dollar_value_added /
                  sum(.data$current_dollar_value_added, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$qilp_industry, .data$year, .data$quarter_num) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$qilp_industry) |>
  dplyr::mutate(s_V_bar = 0.5 * (.data$s_V + dplyr::lag(.data$s_V))) |>
  dplyr::ungroup()

industry_contributions <- alp_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(!.data$exclude_from_log_change,
                !is.na(.data$dln_ALP),
                .data$qilp_industry %in% leaf_industries) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    va_shares_contrib |> dplyr::select("qilp_industry", "year_quarter", "s_V_bar"),
    by = c("qilp_industry", "year_quarter")
  ) |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$s_V_bar)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    contribution = .data$s_V_bar * .data$dln_ALP,
    period = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$year_quarter <= "2019Q4" ~ "pre_pandemic",
      .data$year_quarter <= "2021Q4" ~ "pandemic",
      TRUE                           ~ "post_pandemic"
    )
  )

contributions_by_period <- industry_contributions |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$qilp_industry, .data$period) |>
  dplyr::summarise(mean_contribution = mean(.data$contribution, na.rm = TRUE),
                   .groups = "drop") |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = "period", values_from = "mean_contribution")

readr::write_csv(industry_contributions,
                 here::here("replication", "data", "processed", "qilp_industry_contributions.csv"))
readr::write_csv(contributions_by_period,
                 here::here("replication", "data", "processed", "qilp_contributions_by_period.csv"))

list(
  top10_post_pandemic = contributions_by_period |>
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(.data$post_pandemic)) |>
    dplyr::select("qilp_industry", "pre_pandemic", "pandemic", "post_pandemic") |>
    head(10),
  bottom10_post_pandemic = contributions_by_period |>
    dplyr::arrange(.data$post_pandemic) |>
    dplyr::select("qilp_industry", "pre_pandemic", "pandemic", "post_pandemic") |>
    head(10)
)
```

## Step 17: Baumol-Denison decomposition

```{r step-17-baumol-denison}
# Equation 5 from the paper:
#   Δln ALP_t = pure_productivity + baumol_effect + denison_effect
#
# pure_productivity = Σ_i s^V_{i,0} · Δln ALP_it   (base-period VA weights)
# baumol_effect     = Σ_i (s^V_{it} − s^V_{i,0}) · Δln ALP_it
# denison_effect    = Σ_i (s^V_{it} − s^H_{it}) · (Δln H_it − Δln H_t)
#
# Base period: first available quarter for each industry (2006Q2 for most).

alp_panel     <- readr::read_csv(paths$qilp_alp_panel,    show_col_types = FALSE)
aggregate_alp <- readr::read_csv(paths$qilp_aggregate_alp, show_col_types = FALSE)

# VA shares and hours shares (current-quarter, not averaged)
# Restricted to leaf industries.
va_shares_curr <- alp_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$current_dollar_value_added),
                .data$qilp_industry %in% leaf_industries) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::mutate(s_V = .data$current_dollar_value_added /
                  sum(.data$current_dollar_value_added, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::select("qilp_industry", "year_quarter", "s_V")

hours_shares_curr <- alp_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$total_weekly_hours_sa_q),
                .data$qilp_industry %in% leaf_industries) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::mutate(s_H = .data$total_weekly_hours_sa_q /
                  sum(.data$total_weekly_hours_sa_q, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::select("qilp_industry", "year_quarter", "s_H")

# Base-period VA share: first non-NA quarter per industry
base_shares <- va_shares_curr |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$qilp_industry) |>
  dplyr::slice_min(order_by = .data$year_quarter, n = 1L) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::select("qilp_industry", s_V_base = "s_V")

baumol_denison <- alp_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(!.data$exclude_from_log_change,
                !is.na(.data$dln_ALP), !is.na(.data$dln_H),
                .data$qilp_industry %in% leaf_industries) |>
  dplyr::left_join(va_shares_curr,    by = c("qilp_industry", "year_quarter")) |>
  dplyr::left_join(hours_shares_curr, by = c("qilp_industry", "year_quarter")) |>
  dplyr::left_join(base_shares,       by = "qilp_industry") |>
  dplyr::left_join(aggregate_alp |>
                     dplyr::select("year_quarter", dln_H_agg = "dln_H_agg"),
                   by = "year_quarter") |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$s_V), !is.na(.data$s_H), !is.na(.data$s_V_base)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    pure_productivity = .data$s_V_base * .data$dln_ALP,
    baumol_effect     = (.data$s_V - .data$s_V_base) * .data$dln_ALP,
    denison_effect    = (.data$s_V - .data$s_H) * (.data$dln_H - .data$dln_H_agg)
  ) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$year, .data$quarter_num, .data$quarter, .data$year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    pure_productivity = sum(.data$pure_productivity, na.rm = TRUE),
    baumol_effect     = sum(.data$baumol_effect,     na.rm = TRUE),
    denison_effect    = sum(.data$denison_effect,     na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::left_join(aggregate_alp |>
                     dplyr::select("year_quarter", "dln_ALP_agg"),
                   by = "year_quarter") |>
  # residual = dln_ALP_agg − check_sum: this is expected to be non-zero because
  # dln_ALP_agg uses Törnqvist-averaged shares while the B-D formula uses
  # current-period shares. Large values at seam boundaries (2018Q2, 2006Q1)
  # also reflect industry-set differences at those exclusion points.
  dplyr::mutate(residual = .data$dln_ALP_agg - (.data$pure_productivity + .data$baumol_effect + .data$denison_effect)) |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$year, .data$quarter_num)

readr::write_csv(baumol_denison,
                 here::here("replication", "data", "processed", "qilp_baumol_denison.csv"))

list(
  n_quarters   = nrow(baumol_denison),
  max_residual = max(abs(baumol_denison$residual), na.rm = TRUE),
  recent       = baumol_denison |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$year >= 2022) |>
    dplyr::select("year_quarter", "pure_productivity",
                  "baumol_effect", "denison_effect", "dln_ALP_agg")
)
```

## Step 18: Validation against published QILP workbook

```{r step-18-parse-workbook}
# Parse the published workbook into a long panel.
# The header row uses a Unix timestamp for 2006Q1 and Excel serials for all
# subsequent quarters. Growth sheets are annualized percent (dln × 400).
# The Total Hours sheet is a level in person-hours; LP is a cumulative index.

parse_qilp_sheet <- function(sheet, workbook = here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "qilp", "qilp_latest.xlsx")) {
  raw <- readxl::read_excel(workbook, sheet = sheet, col_names = FALSE)
  # Header cells are mixed: Unix timestamps (seconds since 1970, values > 1e8)
  # and Excel date serials (days since 1899-12-30, values ~ 38000-45000).
  # Apply the correct origin per cell based on magnitude.
  hdr_vals <- as.numeric(unlist(raw[1, 4:ncol(raw)]))
  dates <- as.Date(ifelse(
    !is.na(hdr_vals) & hdr_vals > 1e8,
    hdr_vals / 86400,
    hdr_vals
  ), origin = ifelse(
    !is.na(hdr_vals) & hdr_vals > 1e8,
    "1970-01-01",
    "1899-12-30"
  ))
  dates <- dates[!is.na(dates) & format(dates, "%Y") < "4000"]
  yq    <- paste0(as.integer(format(dates, "%Y")), "Q",
                  ceiling(as.integer(format(dates, "%m")) / 3))
  industry <- as.character(unlist(raw[-1, 1]))
  vals     <- raw[-1, 4:(3 + length(yq))]
  data.frame(
    industry = industry,
    level    = as.character(unlist(raw[-1, 2])),
    gdp_line = as.character(unlist(raw[-1, 3]))
  ) |>
    cbind(as.data.frame(apply(vals, 2, as.numeric))) |>
    stats::setNames(c("industry", "level", "gdp_line", yq)) |>
    tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -(1:3), names_to = "year_quarter", values_to = "value") |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$industry))
}

pub_lp  <- parse_qilp_sheet("Labor Productivity")
pub_hg  <- parse_qilp_sheet("Hours Growth")
pub_ro  <- parse_qilp_sheet("Real Output Growth")
pub_th  <- parse_qilp_sheet("Total Hours")

# Derive published dln_ALP and dln_H from the LP index and Total Hours level
pub_alp_agg <- pub_lp |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$level == "0") |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::mutate(dln_ALP_pub = log(.data$value) - log(dplyr::lag(.data$value))) |>
  dplyr::select("year_quarter", lp_index = "value", "dln_ALP_pub")

pub_h_agg <- pub_th |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$level == "0") |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::mutate(dln_H_pub = log(.data$value) - log(dplyr::lag(.data$value))) |>
  dplyr::select("year_quarter", "dln_H_pub")

# Our estimates
our_agg <- readr::read_csv(paths$qilp_aggregate_alp, show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  dplyr::select("year_quarter", "dln_V_agg", "dln_H_agg", "dln_ALP_agg")

# Join and compare over overlapping range (through 2025Q2)
validation_agg <- our_agg |>
  dplyr::inner_join(pub_alp_agg, by = "year_quarter") |>
  dplyr::inner_join(pub_h_agg,   by = "year_quarter") |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter <= "2025Q2") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    diff_ALP = .data$dln_ALP_agg - .data$dln_ALP_pub,
    diff_H   = .data$dln_H_agg   - .data$dln_H_pub
  )

readr::write_csv(validation_agg,
                 here::here("replication", "data", "processed", "qilp_validation_aggregate.csv"))
```

```{r step-18-validation-results}
# ── Aggregate-level diagnostics ────────────────────────────────────────────
agg_stats <- validation_agg |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$diff_ALP)) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_quarters     = dplyr::n(),
    mae_ALP        = mean(abs(.data$diff_ALP)),
    rmse_ALP       = sqrt(mean(.data$diff_ALP^2)),
    max_abs_ALP    = max(abs(.data$diff_ALP)),
    mae_H          = mean(abs(.data$diff_H), na.rm = TRUE),
    rmse_H         = sqrt(mean(.data$diff_H^2, na.rm = TRUE)),
    cor_ALP        = cor(.data$dln_ALP_agg, .data$dln_ALP_pub, use = "complete.obs"),
    cor_H          = cor(.data$dln_H_agg,   .data$dln_H_pub,   use = "complete.obs")
  )

# ── Industry-level diagnostics ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# Use the LP index sheet: derive dln_ALP per published industry row
pub_alp_ind <- pub_lp |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$level != "0") |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$industry, .data$year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$industry) |>
  dplyr::mutate(dln_ALP_pub = log(.data$value) - log(dplyr::lag(.data$value))) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::select("industry", "year_quarter", "dln_ALP_pub")

our_alp_ind <- readr::read_csv(paths$qilp_alp_panel, show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  dplyr::filter(!.data$exclude_from_log_change, !is.na(.data$dln_ALP)) |>
  dplyr::select(industry = "qilp_industry", "year_quarter", "dln_ALP")

validation_ind <- our_alp_ind |>
  dplyr::inner_join(pub_alp_ind, by = c("industry", "year_quarter")) |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter <= "2025Q2", !is.na(.data$dln_ALP_pub)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(diff_ALP = .data$dln_ALP - .data$dln_ALP_pub)

ind_stats <- validation_ind |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$industry) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    mae  = mean(abs(.data$diff_ALP)),
    rmse = sqrt(mean(.data$diff_ALP^2)),
    cor  = cor(.data$dln_ALP, .data$dln_ALP_pub, use = "complete.obs"),
    n    = dplyr::n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(.data$mae))

readr::write_csv(validation_ind,
                 here::here("replication", "data", "processed", "qilp_validation_industry.csv"))

list(
  aggregate_stats   = agg_stats,
  agg_worst_quarters = validation_agg |>
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(abs(.data$diff_ALP))) |>
    dplyr::select("year_quarter", "dln_ALP_agg", "dln_ALP_pub", "diff_ALP",
                  "dln_H_agg", "dln_H_pub", "diff_H") |>
    head(10),
  industry_worst    = ind_stats |> head(10),
  industry_best     = ind_stats |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$cor)) |>
    dplyr::arrange(.data$mae) |>
    head(10)
)
```

## Step 18b: FRED-backed CES hours test for Food and Apparel

Pull direct CES employment and AWH for Food Manufacturing and Apparel Manufacturing
from the BLS public data API (no key required for these series). Construct monthly
total hours, apply X-13, aggregate to quarters, compute dln_H and dln_ALP, then
compare against the published workbook. If the FRED-backed series beats the CPS-only
baseline (Food MAE 0.013, Apparel no usable baseline), it becomes the fill for those
rows.

```{r step-18b-fred-ces-series-ids}
# All four series are NSA (CEU prefix); X-13 is applied in this step.
# Employment in thousands; AWH in hours per week.
fred_ces_ids <- c(
  food_emp    = "CEU3231100001", # Food Manufacturing all employees (NSA)
  food_awh    = "CEU3231100002", # Food Manufacturing avg weekly hours, all employees (NSA)
  apparel_emp = "CEU3231500001", # Apparel Manufacturing all employees (NSA)
  apparel_awh = "CEU3231500002"  # Apparel Manufacturing avg weekly hours, all employees (NSA)
)
```

```{r step-18b-fetch-fred-ces}
# The BLS v2 API accepts up to 50 series per POST request.
# startyear/endyear span must be ≤ 20 years; split into two calls to cover 2005-2026.
fetch_bls_multi <- function(series_ids, startyear, endyear) {
  body <- list(
    seriesid    = series_ids,
    startyear   = as.character(startyear),
    endyear     = as.character(endyear),
    registrationkey = Sys.getenv("BLS_API_KEY", unset = "")
  )
  resp <- httr2::request("https://api.bls.gov/publicAPI/v2/timeseries/data/") |>
    httr2::req_body_json(body) |>
    httr2::req_perform()
  parsed <- jsonlite::fromJSON(httr2::resp_body_string(resp), simplifyDataFrame = TRUE)
  series_list <- parsed$Results$series
  purrr::map2(series_list$seriesID, series_list$data, function(sid, dat) {
    if (!is.data.frame(dat) || nrow(dat) == 0L || !"period" %in% names(dat)) {
      warning("No data returned for series: ", sid)
      return(tibble::tibble(series_id = sid, year = integer(), month = integer(), value = numeric()))
    }
    tibble::as_tibble(dat) |>
      dplyr::filter(grepl("^M(0[1-9]|1[0-2])$", .data$period)) |>
      dplyr::transmute(
        series_id = sid,
        year      = as.integer(.data$year),
        month     = as.integer(stringr::str_remove(.data$period, "^M0?")),
        value     = as.numeric(.data$value)
      ) |>
      dplyr::arrange(.data$year, .data$month)
  }) |>
    dplyr::bind_rows()
}

ids <- unname(fred_ces_ids)
raw_a <- fetch_bls_multi(ids, 2005, 2014)
raw_b <- fetch_bls_multi(ids, 2015, 2026)
raw_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(raw_a, raw_b) |>
  dplyr::distinct(.data$series_id, .data$year, .data$month, .keep_all = TRUE)

# Report which series came back and how many months each has
series_coverage <- raw_all |>
  dplyr::count(.data$series_id, name = "n_months")

# Pivot to wide: one column per series name (using the named vector for labels)
id_to_name <- tibble::tibble(
  series_id = unname(fred_ces_ids),
  col_name  = names(fred_ces_ids)
)

fred_ces_panel <- raw_all |>
  dplyr::left_join(id_to_name, by = "series_id") |>
```
