```{r step-18b-fetch-fred-ces}
  dplyr::select("year", "month", "col_name", "value") |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = "col_name", values_from = "value") |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$year, .data$month) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    food_weekly_hours    = dplyr::if_else(
      !is.na(.data$food_emp)    & !is.na(.data$food_awh),
      .data$food_emp    * .data$food_awh,    NA_real_
    ),
    apparel_weekly_hours = dplyr::if_else(
      !is.na(.data$apparel_emp) & !is.na(.data$apparel_awh),
      .data$apparel_emp * .data$apparel_awh, NA_real_
    )
  )

list(
  series_coverage = series_coverage,
  n_months        = nrow(fred_ces_panel),
  sample          = fred_ces_panel |> dplyr::filter(.data$year == 2023) |> head(6)
)
```

```{r step-18b-seasonal-adjust}
# sa_from_monthly operates on a sorted vector; caller supplies start year/month.
sa_vec <- function(x, start_year, start_month) {
  n <- length(x)
  if (all(is.na(x))) return(rep(NA_real_, n))
  last_valid <- max(which(!is.na(x)))
  x_trim <- x[seq_len(last_valid)]
  ts_obj <- ts(x_trim, start = c(start_year, start_month), frequency = 12)
  fit <- tryCatch(seasonal::seas(ts_obj, x11 = ""), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(fit)) return(x)
  sa <- as.numeric(seasonal::final(fit))
  if (length(sa) == 0L) return(x)
  if (length(sa) < last_valid) sa <- c(rep(NA_real_, last_valid - length(sa)), sa)
  c(sa, rep(NA_real_, n - last_valid))
}

# Apply SA to each hours series independently after sorting by year-month.
# If apparel_weekly_hours is all-NA (series not available), it passes through as NA.
panel_sorted <- fred_ces_panel |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$year, .data$month)

start_yr  <- panel_sorted$year[1]
start_mo  <- panel_sorted$month[1]

fred_ces_sa <- panel_sorted |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    food_weekly_hours_sa    = sa_vec(.data$food_weekly_hours,    start_yr, start_mo),
    apparel_weekly_hours_sa = sa_vec(.data$apparel_weekly_hours, start_yr, start_mo)
  )
```

```{r step-18b-quarterly-alp}
# Read real VA directly from bea_value_added_all_tables_20260625.xlsx via parse_valueadded_sheet (same as
# Step 14). The combined BEA panel has a unit-scaling bug that renders Apparel
# values as 0.0–0.1 instead of the correct thousands-of-millions range.
t106_18b <- parse_valueadded_sheet("TVA106-Q")

real_va_18b <- t106_18b |>
  dplyr::filter(
    .data$industry %in% c(
      "Food and beverage and tobacco products",
      "Apparel and leather and allied products"
    ),
    !is.na(.data$value), .data$value > 0
  ) |>
  dplyr::select("industry", "year_quarter", real_va = "value")

fred_ces_quarterly <- fred_ces_sa |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    quarter_num  = ceiling(.data$month / 3L),
    year_quarter = paste0(.data$year, "Q", .data$quarter_num)
  ) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    food_q    = sum(.data$food_weekly_hours_sa,    na.rm = FALSE),
    apparel_q = sum(.data$apparel_weekly_hours_sa, na.rm = FALSE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

build_fred_alp <- function(quarterly_df, hours_col, industry_name, real_va_df) {
  quarterly_df |>
    dplyr::select("year_quarter", hours = dplyr::all_of(hours_col)) |>
    dplyr::arrange(.data$year_quarter) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      dln_H      = log(.data$hours) - log(dplyr::lag(.data$hours)),
      seam       = .data$year_quarter %in% c("2018Q1", "2018Q2"),
      dln_H      = dplyr::if_else(.data$seam, NA_real_, .data$dln_H)
    ) |>
    dplyr::left_join(
      real_va_df |> dplyr::filter(.data$industry == industry_name),
      by = "year_quarter"
    ) |>
    dplyr::arrange(.data$year_quarter) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      dln_V  = log(.data$real_va) - log(dplyr::lag(.data$real_va)),
      dln_V  = dplyr::if_else(.data$seam, NA_real_, .data$dln_V),
      dln_ALP = dplyr::if_else(!is.na(.data$dln_V) & !is.na(.data$dln_H),
                                .data$dln_V - .data$dln_H, NA_real_)
    ) |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$dln_ALP)) |>
    dplyr::select("year_quarter", "hours", "dln_H", "dln_V", "dln_ALP")
}

fred_alp_food    <- build_fred_alp(fred_ces_quarterly, "food_q",
                                   "Food and beverage and tobacco products",    real_va_18b)
fred_alp_apparel <- build_fred_alp(fred_ces_quarterly, "apparel_q",
                                   "Apparel and leather and allied products", real_va_18b)
```

```{r step-18b-validate}
pub_alp_ind_18b <- pub_lp |>
  dplyr::filter(
    .data$level != "0",
    .data$industry %in% c(
      "Food and beverage and tobacco products",
      "Apparel and leather and allied products"
    ),
    .data$year_quarter <= "2025Q2",
    !is.na(.data$value), .data$value > 0
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$industry, .data$year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$industry) |>
  dplyr::mutate(dln_ALP_pub = log(.data$value) - log(dplyr::lag(.data$value))) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$dln_ALP_pub)) |>
  dplyr::select("industry", "year_quarter", "dln_ALP_pub")

val_summary <- function(fred_alp_df, pub_df, industry_name, cps_baseline_mae) {
  if (nrow(fred_alp_df) == 0L) {
    return(tibble::tibble(industry = industry_name, n = 0L, cor = NA_real_,
                          mae = NA_real_, baseline_mae_cps = cps_baseline_mae,
                          beats_baseline = NA))
  }
  joined <- fred_alp_df |>
    dplyr::inner_join(
      pub_df |> dplyr::filter(.data$industry == industry_name),
      by = "year_quarter"
    ) |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$dln_ALP_pub))
  tibble::tibble(
    industry         = industry_name,
    n                = nrow(joined),
    cor              = cor(joined$dln_ALP, joined$dln_ALP_pub, use = "complete.obs"),
    mae              = mean(abs(joined$dln_ALP - joined$dln_ALP_pub)),
    baseline_mae_cps = cps_baseline_mae,
    beats_baseline   = dplyr::if_else(!is.na(cps_baseline_mae),
                                      mean(abs(joined$dln_ALP - joined$dln_ALP_pub)) < cps_baseline_mae,
                                      NA)
  )
}

list(
  food_fred_ces    = val_summary(fred_alp_food,    pub_alp_ind_18b,
                                 "Food and beverage and tobacco products",    0.013),
  apparel_fred_ces = val_summary(fred_alp_apparel, pub_alp_ind_18b,
                                 "Apparel and leather and allied products", NA_real_)
)
```

## Step 18c: BLS comparison plots

```{r step-18c-bls-comparison}
# ── Load BLS PRS85006092: nonfarm business LP, q/q annualized pct change ──────
bls_prs <- readxl::read_excel(
  here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bls", "PRS85006092.xlsx"),
  sheet     = "Quarterly",
  skip      = 10,
  col_names = c("date", "bls_ann_pct")
) |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(date), !is.na(bls_ann_pct)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    date         = as.Date(date),
    yr           = as.integer(format(date, "%Y")),
    mo           = as.integer(format(date, "%m")),
    year_quarter = paste0(yr, "Q", ceiling(mo / 3L)),
    bls_ann_pct  = as.numeric(bls_ann_pct)
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(year_quarter, bls_ann_pct)

# ── Plot 1: q/q ALP growth rate comparison ────────────────────────────────────
growth_long <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  our_agg |>
    dplyr::select(year_quarter, value = dln_ALP_agg) |>
    dplyr::mutate(value = value * 400, series = "Replication (ours)"),
  pub_alp_agg |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(dln_ALP_pub)) |>
    dplyr::select(year_quarter, value = dln_ALP_pub) |>
    dplyr::mutate(value = value * 400, series = "Published workbook"),
  bls_prs |>
    dplyr::select(year_quarter, value = bls_ann_pct) |>
    dplyr::mutate(series = "BLS PRS85006092")
) |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(value), year_quarter >= "2006Q3") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    yr   = as.integer(substr(year_quarter, 1, 4)),
    qn   = as.integer(substr(year_quarter, 6, 6)),
    date = as.Date(paste0(yr, "-", sprintf("%02d", (qn - 1L) * 3L + 1L), "-01"))
  )

plot_growth <- ggplot2::ggplot(growth_long,
    ggplot2::aes(x = date, y = value, color = series, linetype = series)) +
  ggplot2::geom_line(linewidth = 0.55) +
  ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Replication (ours)"  = "#1f77b4",
    "Published workbook"  = "#d62728",
    "BLS PRS85006092"     = "#2ca02c"
  )) +
  ggplot2::scale_linetype_manual(values = c(
    "Replication (ours)"  = "solid",
    "Published workbook"  = "dashed",
    "BLS PRS85006092"     = "dotted"
  )) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title    = "Aggregate ALP growth: replication vs. published QILP vs. BLS",
    subtitle = "BLS = nonfarm business (PRS85006092); QILP = nonfarm private Tornqvist",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Annualized percent change",
    color    = NULL,
    linetype = NULL
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(plot_growth)

# ── Plot 2: level index (all normalized to 100 at 2006Q3) ─────────────────────
# Our level: chain dln_ALP_agg forward from implicit base 100 at 2006Q2
our_level_tbl <- our_agg |>
  dplyr::arrange(year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(dln_ALP_agg)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(level = 100 * exp(cumsum(dln_ALP_agg)))

# Published level: normalize lp_index to 100 at 2006Q3
base_pub_2006q3 <- pub_alp_agg$lp_index[pub_alp_agg$year_quarter == "2006Q3"]
pub_level_tbl <- pub_alp_agg |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(lp_index)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(level = lp_index / base_pub_2006q3 * 100)

# BLS level: chain quarterly factors, normalize to 100 at 2006Q3
bls_level_tbl <- bls_prs |>
  dplyr::arrange(year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    qtr_factor = (1 + bls_ann_pct / 100)^(1 / 4),
    level_raw  = cumprod(qtr_factor)
  )
base_bls_2006q3 <- bls_level_tbl$level_raw[bls_level_tbl$year_quarter == "2006Q3"]
bls_level_tbl <- bls_level_tbl |>
  dplyr::mutate(level = level_raw / base_bls_2006q3 * 100)

level_long <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  our_level_tbl |>
    dplyr::select(year_quarter, value = level) |>
    dplyr::mutate(series = "Replication (ours)"),
  pub_level_tbl |>
    dplyr::select(year_quarter, value = level) |>
    dplyr::mutate(series = "Published workbook"),
  bls_level_tbl |>
    dplyr::filter(year_quarter >= "2006Q3") |>
    dplyr::select(year_quarter, value = level) |>
    dplyr::mutate(series = "BLS PRS85006092")
) |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(value)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    yr   = as.integer(substr(year_quarter, 1, 4)),
    qn   = as.integer(substr(year_quarter, 6, 6)),
    date = as.Date(paste0(yr, "-", sprintf("%02d", (qn - 1L) * 3L + 1L), "-01"))
  )

plot_level <- ggplot2::ggplot(level_long,
    ggplot2::aes(x = date, y = value, color = series, linetype = series)) +
  ggplot2::geom_line(linewidth = 0.55) +
  ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Replication (ours)"  = "#1f77b4",
    "Published workbook"  = "#d62728",
    "BLS PRS85006092"     = "#2ca02c"
  )) +
  ggplot2::scale_linetype_manual(values = c(
    "Replication (ours)"  = "solid",
    "Published workbook"  = "dashed",
    "BLS PRS85006092"     = "dotted"
  )) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title    = "Nonfarm business labor productivity: level index (2006Q3 = 100)",
    subtitle = "BLS = nonfarm business (PRS85006092); QILP = nonfarm private Tornqvist / published workbook Private industries",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Index (2006Q3 = 100)",
    color    = NULL,
    linetype = NULL
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(plot_level)

# Save to temp PNGs for insertion into the Validation sheet in Step 19
plot_growth_png <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
plot_level_png  <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
ggplot2::ggsave(plot_growth_png, plot = plot_growth, width = 9, height = 4.5, dpi = 150)
ggplot2::ggsave(plot_level_png,  plot = plot_level,  width = 9, height = 4.5, dpi = 150)

# Build comparison table for Validation sheet
bls_comparison_tbl <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  our_agg |>
    dplyr::select(year_quarter, dln_ALP_agg) |>
    dplyr::mutate(series = "Replication (ours)", ann_pct = round(dln_ALP_agg * 400, 4)) |>
    dplyr::select(year_quarter, series, ann_pct),
  pub_alp_agg |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(dln_ALP_pub)) |>
    dplyr::mutate(series = "Published workbook", ann_pct = round(dln_ALP_pub * 400, 4)) |>
    dplyr::select(year_quarter, series, ann_pct),
  bls_prs |>
    dplyr::mutate(series = "BLS PRS85006092", ann_pct = round(bls_ann_pct, 4)) |>
    dplyr::select(year_quarter, series, ann_pct)
) |>
  dplyr::filter(year_quarter >= "2006Q3") |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = series, values_from = ann_pct) |>
  dplyr::arrange(year_quarter)

bls_comparison_tbl
```

## Step 19: Export in QILP workbook format

```{r step-19-export}
# ── Industry roster from the published workbook ───────────────────────────────
# 89 rows: 87 named industries (incl. Farms as all-NA) plus two aggregate rows
# (Nonfarm private, Private Nonfarm Business) at the bottom.
# Row ordering and level/gdp_line metadata are copied from the published file.
roster <- tibble::tribble(
  ~industry,                                                                      ~level, ~gdp_line,
  "Private industries",                                                           "0",    "0",
  "Agriculture, forestry, fishing, and hunting",                                  "1",    "1",
  "Farms",                                                                        "2",    "2",
  "Forestry, fishing, and related activities",                                    "2",    "3",
  "Mining",                                                                       "1",    "4",
  "Oil and gas extraction",                                                       "2",    "5",
  "Mining, except oil and gas",                                                   "2",    "6",
  "Support activities for mining",                                                "2",    "7",
  "Utilities",                                                                    "1",    "8",
  "Construction",                                                                 "1",    "9",
  "Manufacturing",                                                                "1",    "10",
  "Durable goods",                                                                "2",    "11",
  "Wood products",                                                                "3",    "12",
  "Nonmetallic mineral products",                                                 "3",    "13",
  "Primary metals",                                                               "3",    "14",
  "Fabricated metal products",                                                    "3",    "15",
  "Machinery",                                                                    "3",    "16",
  "Computer and electronic products",                                             "3",    "17",
  "Electrical equipment, appliances, and components",                             "3",    "18",
  "Motor vehicles, bodies and trailers, and parts",                               "3",    "19",
  "Other transportation equipment",                                               "3",    "20",
  "Furniture and related products",                                               "3",    "21",
  "Miscellaneous manufacturing",                                                  "3",    "22",
  "Nondurable goods",                                                             "2",    "23",
  "Food and beverage and tobacco products",                                       "3",    "24",
  "Textile mills and textile product mills",                                      "3",    "25",
  "Apparel and leather and allied products",                                      "3",    "26",
  "Paper products",                                                               "3",    "27",
  "Printing and related support activities",                                      "3",    "28",
  "Petroleum and coal products",                                                  "3",    "29",
  "Chemical products",                                                            "3",    "30",
  "Plastics and rubber products",                                                 "3",    "31",
  "Wholesale trade",                                                              "1",    "32",
  "Retail trade",                                                                 "1",    "33",
  "Motor vehicle and parts dealers",                                              "2",    "34",
  "Food and beverage stores",                                                     "2",    "35",
  "General merchandise stores",                                                   "2",    "36",
  "Other retail",                                                                 "2",    "37",
  "Transportation and warehousing",                                               "1",    "38",
  "Air transportation",                                                           "2",    "39",
  "Rail transportation",                                                          "2",    "40",
  "Water transportation",                                                         "2",    "41",
  "Truck transportation",                                                         "2",    "42",
  "Transit and ground passenger transportation",                                  "2",    "43",
  "Pipeline transportation",                                                      "2",    "44",
  "Other transportation and support activities",                                  "2",    "45",
  "Warehousing and storage",                                                      "2",    "46",
  "Information",                                                                  "1",    "47",
  "Publishing industries, except internet (includes software)",                   "2",    "48",
  "Motion picture and sound recording industries",                                "2",    "49",
  "Broadcasting and telecommunications",                                          "2",    "50",
  "Data processing, internet publishing, and other information services",         "2",    "51",
  "Finance, insurance, real estate, rental, and leasing",                         "1",    "52",
  "Finance and insurance",                                                        "2",    "53",
  "Federal Reserve banks, credit intermediation, and related activities",         "3",    "54",
  "Securities, commodity contracts, and investments",                             "3",    "55",
  "Insurance carriers and related activities",                                    "3",    "56",
  "Funds, trusts, and other financial vehicles",                                  "3",    "57",
  "Real estate and rental and leasing",                                           "2",    "58",
  "Real estate",                                                                  "3",    "59",
  "Housing",                                                                      "4",    "60",
  "Other real estate",                                                            "4",    "61",
  "Rental and leasing services and lessors of intangible assets",                 "3",    "62",
  "Professional and business services",                                           "1",    "63",
  "Professional, scientific, and technical services",                             "2",    "64",
  "Legal services",                                                               "3",    "65",
  "Computer systems design and related services",                                 "3",    "66",
  "Miscellaneous professional, scientific, and technical services",               "3",    "67",
  "Management of companies and enterprises",                                      "2",    "68",
  "Administrative and waste management services",                                 "2",    "69",
  "Administrative and support services",                                          "3",    "70",
  "Waste management and remediation services",                                    "3",    "71",
  "Educational services, health care, and social assistance",                     "1",    "72",
  "Educational services",                                                         "2",    "73",
  "Health care and social assistance",                                            "2",    "74",
  "Ambulatory health care services",                                              "3",    "75",
  "Hospitals",                                                                    "3",    "76",
  "Nursing and residential care facilities",                                      "3",    "77",
  "Social assistance",                                                            "3",    "78",
  "Arts, entertainment, recreation, accommodation, and food services",            "1",    "79",
  "Arts, entertainment, and recreation",                                          "2",    "80",
  "Performing arts, spectator sports, museums, and related activities",           "3",    "81",
  "Amusements, gambling, and recreation industries",                              "3",    "82",
  "Accommodation and food services",                                              "2",    "83",
  "Accommodation",                                                                "3",    "84",
  "Food services and drinking places",                                            "3",    "85",
  "Other services, except government",                                            "1",    "86",
  "Nonfarm private",                                                              "N/A",  "87",
  "Private Nonfarm Business",                                                     "N/A",  "88"
)

# ── Quarter date sequence for our extended panel ──────────────────────────────
# Header columns are Excel date serial numbers for the first day of each quarter.
# 2006Q1 = 2006-01-01 = serial 38718.
# Include any quarter that has hours data OR BEA real VA data. This ensures the
# 2018Q1 and 2018Q2 seam quarters appear as columns (matching the published
# workbook) even though dln_V is NA for both due to the BEA definition-change seam.
all_yq <- dplyr::union(
  readr::read_csv(paths$qilp_quarterly_hours, show_col_types = FALSE) |>
    dplyr::distinct(.data$year_quarter) |>
    dplyr::pull("year_quarter"),
  readr::read_csv(paths$qilp_alp_panel, show_col_types = FALSE) |>
    dplyr::group_by(.data$year_quarter) |>
    dplyr::summarise(any_v = any(!is.na(.data$dln_V)), .groups = "drop") |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$any_v) |>
    dplyr::pull("year_quarter")
) |>
  sort()

# Cap at the last quarter with BEA real VA data. The hours panel can contain
# partial quarters (e.g., April–May of a quarter where BEA has not yet released
# GDP-by-industry), which would create columns with hours but no productivity.
last_bea_yq <- readr::read_csv(paths$qilp_alp_panel, show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$dln_V)) |>
  dplyr::pull("year_quarter") |>
  max()
all_yq <- all_yq[all_yq <= last_bea_yq]
# Build date lookup: year_quarter -> Excel serial integer for Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct
yq_to_date <- function(yq) {
  yr  <- as.integer(substr(yq, 1, 4))
  qn  <- as.integer(substr(yq, 6, 6))
  mon <- (qn - 1L) * 3L + 1L
  as.Date(paste(yr, mon, "01", sep = "-"))
}
quarter_dates <- as.Date(vapply(all_yq, yq_to_date, numeric(1L)),
                         origin = "1970-01-01")

# ── Load computed panels ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
alp_panel <- readr::read_csv(paths$qilp_alp_panel, show_col_types = FALSE)
agg_alp   <- readr::read_csv(paths$qilp_aggregate_alp, show_col_types = FALSE)
qhours    <- readr::read_csv(paths$qilp_quarterly_hours, show_col_types = FALSE)

# ── Helper: build a wide sheet matrix (industry x quarter) ───────────────────
# value_df must have columns: industry, year_quarter, value
make_wide_sheet <- function(value_df, roster, all_yq) {
  wide <- value_df |>
    dplyr::select("industry", "year_quarter", "value") |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = "year_quarter", values_from = "value")

  roster |>
    dplyr::left_join(wide, by = "industry") |>
    dplyr::select(
      "industry", "level", "gdp_line",
      dplyr::any_of(all_yq)
    )
}
```

```{r step-19-growth-rate-match}
# ── Methodology validation: growth-rate match over the full series ────────────
# The exported file uses the published workbook as the authoritative history
# through 2025Q1 and appends four extension quarters (2025Q2–2026Q1) using our
# computed growth rates. This block documents that our methodology reproduces
# the published growth rates well, justifying use of our rates for the extension.
growth_rate_match <- validation_agg |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$diff_ALP)) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_quarters   = dplyr::n(),
    cor_ALP      = cor(.data$dln_ALP_agg, .data$dln_ALP_pub, use = "complete.obs"),
    mae_ALP      = mean(abs(.data$diff_ALP)),
    rmse_ALP     = sqrt(mean(.data$diff_ALP^2)),
    cor_H        = cor(.data$dln_H_agg,   .data$dln_H_pub,   use = "complete.obs"),
    mae_H        = mean(abs(.data$diff_H), na.rm = TRUE)
  )

growth_rate_match_by_period <- validation_agg |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$diff_ALP)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    period = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$year_quarter <= "2021Q4" ~ "2006Q2-2021Q4",
      .data$year_quarter <= "2024Q4" ~ "2022Q1-2024Q4",
      TRUE                           ~ "2025Q1-2025Q2"
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$period) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n          = dplyr::n(),
    cor_ALP    = cor(.data$dln_ALP_agg, .data$dln_ALP_pub, use = "complete.obs"),
    mae_ALP    = mean(abs(.data$diff_ALP)),
    rmse_ALP   = sqrt(mean(.data$diff_ALP^2)),
    .groups    = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$period)

list(
  full_series = growth_rate_match,
  by_period   = growth_rate_match_by_period
)
```

```{r step-19-index-construction}
# ── Graft: published history through 2025Q1, then our four extension quarters ──
# The February 2026 CES annual benchmark revised 2025 employment and hours levels
# after the published workbook was built. As a result the published 2025Q2 hours
# figure is based on pre-revision data: hours appear to fall sharply in Q2 while
# our post-revision CES shows a slight rise. Grafting at the (distorted) published
# 2025Q2 level would shift all extension index levels upward by ~1.6 points relative
# to where they should be on a consistent data vintage.
#
# Fix: graft at 2025Q1, the last quarter where our hours match the published within
# rounding (gap 0.2 pp), and apply our growth rates for 2025Q2–2026Q1.
# The four extension quarters are: 2025Q2, 2025Q3, 2025Q4, 2026Q1.

ext_yq <- all_yq[all_yq > "2025Q1"]   # quarters we are adding: 2025Q2–2026Q1
pub_yq_keep <- all_yq[all_yq <= "2025Q1"]

# ── LP index: graft extension quarters onto published 2025Q1 level ────────────
pub_lp_long <- pub_lp |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter %in% pub_yq_keep) |>
  dplyr::select("industry", "year_quarter", value = "value")

# Cumulative dln_ALP from 2025Q1 base for each industry
our_lp_ext_ind <- alp_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter %in% ext_yq, !.data$exclude_from_log_change) |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$qilp_industry, .data$year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$qilp_industry) |>
  dplyr::mutate(cum_dln = cumsum(dplyr::if_else(is.na(.data$dln_ALP), 0, .data$dln_ALP))) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    pub_lp_long |>
      dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter == "2025Q1") |>
      dplyr::transmute(qilp_industry = .data$industry, pub_base = .data$value),
    by = "qilp_industry"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(value = .data$pub_base * exp(.data$cum_dln)) |>
  dplyr::select(industry = "qilp_industry", "year_quarter", "value")

# The published workbook carries all three aggregate rows through 2025Q1; pub_lp_long
# already contains them. For extension quarters, graft our dln_ALP_agg onto each
# row's own 2025Q1 published base so index continuity is preserved independently.
agg_cum_dln <- agg_alp |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter %in% ext_yq) |>
  dplyr::arrange(.data$year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::mutate(cum_dln = cumsum(dplyr::if_else(is.na(.data$dln_ALP_agg), 0, .data$dln_ALP_agg)))

agg_names <- c("Private industries", "Nonfarm private", "Private Nonfarm Business")

our_lp_ext_agg <- purrr::map_dfr(agg_names, function(nm) {
  base_val <- pub_lp_long |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$industry == nm, .data$year_quarter == "2025Q1") |>
    dplyr::pull("value")
  agg_cum_dln |>
    dplyr::mutate(industry = nm, value = base_val * exp(.data$cum_dln)) |>
    dplyr::select("industry", "year_quarter", "value")
})

lp_vals <- dplyr::bind_rows(pub_lp_long, our_lp_ext_ind, our_lp_ext_agg)

# ── Real Output Growth: published history + our extension quarters ─────────────
pub_ro_long <- pub_ro |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter %in% pub_yq_keep) |>
  dplyr::select("industry", "year_quarter", value = "value")

our_ro_ext_ind <- alp_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter %in% ext_yq, !.data$exclude_from_log_change) |>
  dplyr::mutate(value = .data$dln_V * 400) |>
  dplyr::select(industry = "qilp_industry", "year_quarter", "value")

our_ro_ext_agg <- agg_alp |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter %in% ext_yq) |>
  dplyr::mutate(value = .data$dln_V_agg * 400, industry = "Nonfarm private") |>
  dplyr::select("industry", "year_quarter", "value")

our_ro_ext_agg <- purrr::map_dfr(agg_names, function(nm) {
  agg_alp |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter %in% ext_yq) |>
    dplyr::mutate(industry = nm, value = .data$dln_V_agg * 400) |>
    dplyr::select("industry", "year_quarter", "value")
})

rog_vals <- dplyr::bind_rows(pub_ro_long, our_ro_ext_ind, our_ro_ext_agg)

# ── Total Hours: published history + our extension quarters ───────────────────
pub_th_long <- pub_th |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter %in% pub_yq_keep) |>
  dplyr::select("industry", "year_quarter", value = "value")

# Raw total_weekly_hours_sa_q is in units of thousands·hours/week summed over
# 3 months, while the published Total Hours sheet stores values at a different
# absolute scale. Rather than recomputing cumulative log differences (which risks
# dropping industries when the join to pub_th_long fails), compute a per-industry
# scale factor = pub_th_2025Q1 / our_2025Q1, then multiply raw qhours extension
# values by that constant. Growth rates are exactly preserved (the constant
# cancels in dln_H); units are anchored to the published 2025Q1 level.
th_scale_ind <- pub_th_long |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter == "2025Q1") |>
  dplyr::select(qilp_industry = "industry", pub_base = "value") |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    qhours |>
      dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter == "2025Q1") |>
      dplyr::select("qilp_industry", our_base = "total_weekly_hours_sa_q"),
    by = "qilp_industry"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(scale = .data$pub_base / .data$our_base) |>
  dplyr::select("qilp_industry", "scale")

our_th_ext_ind <- qhours |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter %in% ext_yq, !.data$exclude_from_log_change) |>
  dplyr::left_join(th_scale_ind, by = "qilp_industry") |>
  dplyr::mutate(value = .data$total_weekly_hours_sa_q * .data$scale) |>
  dplyr::select(industry = "qilp_industry", "year_quarter", "value")

# For aggregate rows, summing qhours would double-count parent industries.
# Instead chain the published 2025Q1 aggregate base forward with dln_H_agg,
# matching the approach used for the LP index aggregate extension.
our_th_ext_agg <- purrr::map_dfr(agg_names, function(nm) {
  base_val <- pub_th_long |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$industry == nm, .data$year_quarter == "2025Q1") |>
    dplyr::pull("value")
  agg_alp |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter %in% ext_yq) |>
    dplyr::arrange(.data$year_quarter) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      cum_dln_H = cumsum(dplyr::if_else(is.na(.data$dln_H_agg), 0, .data$dln_H_agg)),
      industry  = nm,
      value     = base_val * exp(.data$cum_dln_H)
    ) |>
    dplyr::select("industry", "year_quarter", "value")
})

th_vals <- dplyr::bind_rows(pub_th_long, our_th_ext_ind, our_th_ext_agg)

# ── Hours Growth: published history + our extension quarters ──────────────────
pub_hg_long <- pub_hg |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter %in% pub_yq_keep) |>
  dplyr::select("industry", "year_quarter", value = "value")

our_hg_ext_ind <- alp_panel |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter %in% ext_yq, !.data$exclude_from_log_change) |>
  dplyr::mutate(value = .data$dln_H * 400) |>
  dplyr::select(industry = "qilp_industry", "year_quarter", "value")

our_hg_ext_agg <- agg_alp |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter %in% ext_yq) |>
  dplyr::mutate(value = .data$dln_H_agg * 400, industry = "Nonfarm private") |>
  dplyr::select("industry", "year_quarter", "value")

our_hg_ext_agg <- purrr::map_dfr(agg_names, function(nm) {
  agg_alp |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$year_quarter %in% ext_yq) |>
    dplyr::mutate(industry = nm, value = .data$dln_H_agg * 400) |>
    dplyr::select("industry", "year_quarter", "value")
})

hg_vals <- dplyr::bind_rows(pub_hg_long, our_hg_ext_ind, our_hg_ext_agg)

# ── Nominal Output: our data throughout (published workbook does not include) ──
# Nominal VA is not in the published workbook sheets so we use our series in full.
agg_no_full <- alp_panel |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::summarise(value = sum(.data$current_dollar_value_added, na.rm = TRUE),
                   .groups = "drop")

no_vals <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  alp_panel |>
    dplyr::select(industry = "qilp_industry", "year_quarter",
                  value = "current_dollar_value_added"),
  purrr::map_dfr(agg_names, function(nm) dplyr::mutate(agg_no_full, industry = nm))
)

# ── Build wide sheets ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
lp_sheet  <- make_wide_sheet(lp_vals,  roster, all_yq)
rog_sheet <- make_wide_sheet(rog_vals, roster, all_yq)
th_sheet  <- make_wide_sheet(th_vals,  roster, all_yq)
hg_sheet  <- make_wide_sheet(hg_vals,  roster, all_yq)
no_sheet  <- make_wide_sheet(no_vals,  roster, all_yq)

# ── Version and Contents sheets ───────────────────────────────────────────────
version_sheet <- tibble::tibble(
  label = c("Paper:", "Paper URL:", "Data URL:", "Version:", "Version notes:"),
  value = c(
    "Hobijn, Mestieri, Werquin, Zhang (2025), Chicago Fed Economic Perspectives No. 1",
    "https://www.chicagofed.org/-/media/publications/economic-perspectives/2025/ep2025-1.pdf",
    "Extension of published QILP workbook. BEA GDP-by-industry June 2026 release. BLS CES/CPS.",
    as.character(Sys.Date()),
    paste0("History through 2025Q1 is taken directly from the published workbook. ",
           "Extension quarters (", min(ext_yq), "-", max(ext_yq), ") use our computed ",
           "growth rates applied to the published 2025Q1 endpoint. ",
           "Graft point chosen to avoid the February 2026 CES benchmark revision, ",
           "which distorts the published 2025Q2 hours level. ",
           "Compensation, payroll-share, and unit labor cost sheets not included.")
  )
)

contents_sheet <- tibble::tibble(
  index = c(0L, 1L, 2L, 3L, 4L, 5L, 6L),
  sheet = c("Labor Productivity", "Real Output Growth", "Total Hours",
            "Hours Growth", "Nominal Output", "Employment", "Validation"),
  description = c(
    "Real Value Added/Total Hours. Index, 2017 = 100",
    "Real value added growth, weighted by nominal VA shares. Quarterly growth, annualized",
    "Sum of SA monthly total weekly hours within quarter. Source: BLS CES/CPS",
    "Quarterly growth, annualized",
    "Nominal Value Added (millions of current dollars, SAAR). Source: BEA TVA105-Q",
    "Total employment (persons, quarterly average of monthly). Source: BLS CES/CPS",
    "Replication diagnostics: aggregate and industry-level MAE/correlation vs. published workbook; BLS PRS85006092 growth and level comparison with plots"
  )
)

# ── Write workbook ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
wb_out <- openxlsx::createWorkbook()

write_qilp_sheet <- function(wb, sheet_name, df, quarter_dates, all_yq) {
  openxlsx::addWorksheet(wb, sheet_name)

  # Row 1 text labels
  openxlsx::writeData(wb, sheet_name,
                      as.data.frame(t(c("industry", "level", "gdp_line"))),
                      startRow = 1, startCol = 1,
                      colNames = FALSE, rowNames = FALSE)

  # Row 1 date headers: write each as a single-column Date data.frame so openxlsx
  # encodes them as Excel date serials with a date format automatically.
  date_df <- as.data.frame(as.list(quarter_dates))
  names(date_df) <- paste0("q", seq_along(quarter_dates))
  openxlsx::writeData(wb, sheet_name, date_df,
                      startRow = 1, startCol = 4,
                      colNames = FALSE, rowNames = FALSE)

  # Data rows
  openxlsx::writeData(wb, sheet_name,
                      df[, 1:3, drop = FALSE],
                      startRow = 2, startCol = 1,
                      colNames = FALSE, rowNames = FALSE)
  openxlsx::writeData(wb, sheet_name,
                      df[, 4:ncol(df), drop = FALSE],
                      startRow = 2, startCol = 4,
                      colNames = FALSE, rowNames = FALSE)
}

openxlsx::addWorksheet(wb_out, "Version")
openxlsx::writeData(wb_out, "Version", version_sheet,
                    startRow = 1, colNames = FALSE, rowNames = FALSE)

openxlsx::addWorksheet(wb_out, "Contents")
openxlsx::writeData(wb_out, "Contents", contents_sheet,
                    startRow = 1, colNames = TRUE, rowNames = FALSE)

write_qilp_sheet(wb_out, "Labor Productivity", lp_sheet,  quarter_dates, all_yq)
write_qilp_sheet(wb_out, "Real Output Growth", rog_sheet, quarter_dates, all_yq)
write_qilp_sheet(wb_out, "Total Hours",        th_sheet,  quarter_dates, all_yq)
write_qilp_sheet(wb_out, "Hours Growth",       hg_sheet,  quarter_dates, all_yq)
write_qilp_sheet(wb_out, "Nominal Output",     no_sheet,  quarter_dates, all_yq)

# ── Employment sheet ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Quarterly total employment (persons) by industry, matching the published
# workbook format. Monthly total_employment (thousands) is averaged over the
# three months of each quarter and converted to persons (* 1000).
# The aggregate row ("Nonfarm private") sums leaf industries from the split panel
# (parent-retained rows excluded to avoid double-counting).

emp_split_raw <- readr::read_csv(
  here::here("replication", "data", "raw", "bls", "qilp_monthly_total_labor_inputs_split.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    quarter_num  = ceiling(.data$month / 3L),
    year_quarter = paste0(.data$year, "Q", .data$quarter_num)
  )

# Industry rows: include parent_retained (Real estate, Finance and insurance)
# so the Employment sheet matches the published workbook's roster.
emp_ind_q <- emp_split_raw |>
  dplyr::group_by(industry = .data$qilp_industry, .data$year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::summarise(value = mean(.data$total_employment, na.rm = TRUE) * 1000,
                   .groups = "drop")

# Aggregate sum: exclude parent_retained to avoid double-counting leaf industries.
emp_agg_q <- emp_split_raw |>
  dplyr::filter(
    is.na(.data$split_method) | !stringr::str_detect(.data$split_method, "parent_retained")
  ) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$year_quarter, .data$year, .data$month) |>
  dplyr::summarise(emp_month = sum(.data$total_employment, na.rm = TRUE),
                   .groups = "drop") |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$year_quarter) |>
  dplyr::summarise(value = mean(.data$emp_month, na.rm = TRUE) * 1000,
                   .groups = "drop") |>
  dplyr::mutate(industry = "Nonfarm private")

emp_vals <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  emp_ind_q,
  purrr::map_dfr(agg_names, function(nm) dplyr::mutate(emp_agg_q, industry = nm))
)
emp_sheet <- make_wide_sheet(emp_vals, roster, all_yq)
write_qilp_sheet(wb_out, "Employment", emp_sheet, quarter_dates, all_yq)

# ── Validation sheet ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Section 1: aggregate-level quarter-by-quarter comparison vs. published workbook.
# Section 2: industry-level summary statistics over the published overlap.
# Section 3: notes on known data vintage differences.
#
# validation_agg and validation_ind are constructed in Step 18; they cover the
# published overlap period (through 2025Q2).

val_agg_out <- validation_agg |>
  dplyr::select(
    year_quarter,
    dln_ALP_ours  = "dln_ALP_agg",
    dln_ALP_pub   = "dln_ALP_pub",
    diff_ALP      = "diff_ALP",
    dln_H_ours    = "dln_H_agg",
    dln_H_pub     = "dln_H_pub",
    diff_H        = "diff_H",
    dln_V_ours    = "dln_V_agg"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(dplyr::across(
    c("dln_ALP_ours","dln_ALP_pub","diff_ALP",
      "dln_H_ours","dln_H_pub","diff_H","dln_V_ours"),
    ~ round(.x * 400, 4)
  ))

val_agg_header <- tibble::tibble(
  note = paste0(
    "Aggregate validation: ours vs. published QILP workbook (2006Q2-2025Q2, n=",
    sum(!is.na(validation_agg$diff_ALP)), " quarters). ",
    "All growth rates annualized (x400). ",
    "Graft point is 2025Q1; extension quarters 2025Q2-2026Q1 are ours."
  )
)

agg_summary <- validation_agg |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$diff_ALP)) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    series        = "Aggregate ALP",
    n_quarters    = dplyr::n(),
    correlation   = round(cor(.data$dln_ALP_agg, .data$dln_ALP_pub, use = "complete.obs"), 3),
    mae_annpct    = round(mean(abs(.data$diff_ALP)) * 400, 4),
    rmse_annpct   = round(sqrt(mean(.data$diff_ALP^2)) * 400, 4),
    max_abs_annpct = round(max(abs(.data$diff_ALP)) * 400, 4)
  )

hours_summary <- validation_agg |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$diff_H)) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    series        = "Aggregate Hours",
    n_quarters    = dplyr::n(),
    correlation   = round(cor(.data$dln_H_agg, .data$dln_H_pub, use = "complete.obs"), 3),
    mae_annpct    = round(mean(abs(.data$diff_H)) * 400, 4),
    rmse_annpct   = round(sqrt(mean(.data$diff_H^2)) * 400, 4),
    max_abs_annpct = round(max(abs(.data$diff_H)) * 400, 4)
  )

agg_by_period <- validation_agg |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$diff_ALP)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(period = dplyr::case_when(
    .data$year_quarter <= "2021Q4" ~ "2006Q2-2021Q4",
    .data$year_quarter <= "2024Q4" ~ "2022Q1-2024Q4",
    TRUE                           ~ "2025Q1"
  )) |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$period) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    series        = "Aggregate ALP",
    n_quarters    = dplyr::n(),
    correlation   = round(cor(.data$dln_ALP_agg, .data$dln_ALP_pub, use = "complete.obs"), 3),
    mae_annpct    = round(mean(abs(.data$diff_ALP)) * 400, 4),
    rmse_annpct   = round(sqrt(mean(.data$diff_ALP^2)) * 400, 4),
    .groups       = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::select("series", "period", dplyr::everything())

val_ind_summary <- validation_ind |>
  dplyr::group_by(.data$industry) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_quarters  = dplyr::n(),
    correlation = round(cor(.data$dln_ALP, .data$dln_ALP_pub, use = "complete.obs"), 3),
    mae_annpct  = round(mean(abs(.data$diff_ALP)) * 400, 4),
    rmse_annpct = round(sqrt(mean(.data$diff_ALP^2)) * 400, 4),
    .groups     = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(.data$mae_annpct))

val_notes <- tibble::tibble(note = c(
  "Validation notes",
  paste0("Period: 2006Q2-2025Q2 (published workbook range). ",
         "Our series uses June 2026 BEA release and post-Feb-2026-benchmark CES data."),
  "Issue 1 (BEA vintage): August 2025 BEA annual revision revised GDP-by-industry back to 2022. Drives dln_V gap throughout; unresolvable without historical BEA vintages.",
  "Issue 2 (CES benchmark): Feb 2026 BLS benchmark revised 2025 CES employment/hours. Causes 6.4 pp annualized dln_H gap at 2025Q2. Graft point moved to 2025Q1 to avoid inheriting distorted published 2025Q2 base level.",
  "Issue 7 (Housing employment and hours fix): Housing employment sourced from CEU5553111001 (Lessors of residential buildings, NAICS 53111), matching published ~375K. Hours derived from CEU5553111001 employment x Real estate parent AWH proxy (~620M annual hours, consistent with published workbook). Housing is included in the Tornqvist aggregate.",
  "Issue 8 (Leaf-industry filter): Tornqvist in Steps 15-17 previously included parent aggregates (Manufacturing, Finance and insurance, etc.), causing double-counting. Fixed by restricting to 65 leaf industries.",
  "Issue 9 (F&I 2008-2009): Finance & insurance industry-level cor = 0.40 driven entirely by 2 quarters (2008Q4, 2009Q1) where BEA vintage revision caused a sign reversal in dln_V. Excluding those 2 quarters gives cor = 0.97. Unresolvable without original BEA vintage.",
  "Issue 10 (Real estate TVA mapping fix): Published 'Real estate' (level 3) = ORE series, not 531 parent. Fixed re_tva_override to map qilp_industry 'Real estate' to TVA106-Q 'Other real estate'. Parent aggregates RERL and FIRE show persistent dln_V gaps vs published due to BEA vintage differences in aggregate series (unresolvable).",
  "Other transportation equipment: AWH uses aerospace proxy (CEU3133640002, NAICS 3364); aerospace is ~75% of OTE employment and is the only OTE subsector with a published AWH series.",
  "Miscellaneous professional, scientific, and technical services: constructed as PST total minus Legal minus Computer Systems. AWH proxy is CEU6054000002 (Professional and Business Services sector). A gap in the 2016-2025 CES pull window was patched by a targeted re-pull in Step 8.",
  "Farms: all-NA in published workbook; matched here.",
  "Agriculture, Forestry: hours injected from published workbook Total Hours sheet directly (outside CES nonfarm payroll scope).",
  "Food & beverage: CES series corrected from food stores (CEU4244500001) to food manufacturing (CEU3231100001). Industry validation: cor 0.973, MAE 0.0056.",
  "Apparel: CES series CEU3231500001 (Apparel Manufacturing, NAICS 315). Industry validation: cor 0.736, MAE 0.037."
))

openxlsx::addWorksheet(wb_out, "Validation")

row_cursor <- 1L

openxlsx::writeData(wb_out, "Validation", val_agg_header,
                    startRow = row_cursor, colNames = FALSE, rowNames = FALSE)
row_cursor <- row_cursor + 2L

openxlsx::writeData(wb_out, "Validation",
                    dplyr::bind_rows(agg_summary, hours_summary),
                    startRow = row_cursor, colNames = TRUE, rowNames = FALSE)
row_cursor <- row_cursor + nrow(dplyr::bind_rows(agg_summary, hours_summary)) + 2L

openxlsx::writeData(wb_out, "Validation", agg_by_period,
                    startRow = row_cursor, colNames = TRUE, rowNames = FALSE)
row_cursor <- row_cursor + nrow(agg_by_period) + 2L

openxlsx::writeData(wb_out, "Validation",
                    tibble::tibble(header = "Quarter-by-quarter aggregate comparison (annualized %)"),
                    startRow = row_cursor, colNames = FALSE, rowNames = FALSE)
row_cursor <- row_cursor + 1L

openxlsx::writeData(wb_out, "Validation", val_agg_out,
                    startRow = row_cursor, colNames = TRUE, rowNames = FALSE)
row_cursor <- row_cursor + nrow(val_agg_out) + 2L

openxlsx::writeData(wb_out, "Validation",
                    tibble::tibble(header = "Industry-level summary statistics (annualized %, sorted by MAE descending)"),
                    startRow = row_cursor, colNames = FALSE, rowNames = FALSE)
row_cursor <- row_cursor + 1L

openxlsx::writeData(wb_out, "Validation", val_ind_summary,
                    startRow = row_cursor, colNames = TRUE, rowNames = FALSE)
row_cursor <- row_cursor + nrow(val_ind_summary) + 2L

openxlsx::writeData(wb_out, "Validation", val_notes,
                    startRow = row_cursor, colNames = FALSE, rowNames = FALSE)
row_cursor <- row_cursor + nrow(val_notes) + 2L

# ── BLS comparison section ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
openxlsx::writeData(wb_out, "Validation",
                    tibble::tibble(header = "BLS PRS85006092 comparison: aggregate ALP growth and level (2006Q3+)"),
                    startRow = row_cursor, colNames = FALSE, rowNames = FALSE)
row_cursor <- row_cursor + 2L

openxlsx::writeData(wb_out, "Validation",
                    tibble::tibble(note = paste0(
                      "BLS series PRS85006092 = Nonfarm Business sector, Labor productivity (output per hour), ",
                      "quarterly percent change, annualized. QILP Replication and Published workbook use ",
                      "nonfarm private Tornqvist index. Sectors differ: BLS excludes government, nonprofit, ",
                      "and household; QILP covers private industries ex-Housing."
                    )),
                    startRow = row_cursor, colNames = FALSE, rowNames = FALSE)
row_cursor <- row_cursor + 2L

# Plot 1: growth rates
if (file.exists(plot_growth_png)) {
  openxlsx::insertImage(wb_out, "Validation", plot_growth_png,
                        startRow = row_cursor, startCol = 1,
                        width = 9, height = 4.5, units = "in", dpi = 150)
  row_cursor <- row_cursor + 30L
}

# Plot 2: level index
if (file.exists(plot_level_png)) {
  openxlsx::insertImage(wb_out, "Validation", plot_level_png,
                        startRow = row_cursor, startCol = 1,
                        width = 9, height = 4.5, units = "in", dpi = 150)
  row_cursor <- row_cursor + 30L
}

# Comparison table
openxlsx::writeData(wb_out, "Validation",
                    tibble::tibble(header = "Quarter-by-quarter: annualized pct change"),
                    startRow = row_cursor, colNames = FALSE, rowNames = FALSE)
row_cursor <- row_cursor + 1L

openxlsx::writeData(wb_out, "Validation", bls_comparison_tbl,
                    startRow = row_cursor, colNames = TRUE, rowNames = FALSE)

openxlsx::saveWorkbook(wb_out, paths$qilp_export_xlsx, overwrite = TRUE)

cat("Exported:", paths$qilp_export_xlsx, "\n")
cat("Sheets:  Version, Contents, Labor Productivity, Real Output Growth,",
    "Total Hours, Hours Growth, Nominal Output, Employment, Validation\n")
cat("Quarters:", min(all_yq), "to", max(all_yq), "(", length(all_yq), ")\n")
cat("Industries per sheet:", nrow(roster), "\n")
```
